Genesis1:1 In the beginning
Genesis1:2 the earth was a formless wasteland
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 God saw how good the light was. God then separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 God called the light day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 God made the dome
Genesis1:8 God called the dome the sky. Evening came
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land the earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 the earth brought forth every kind of plant that bears seed and every kind of fruit tree on earth that bears fruit with its seed in it. God saw how good it was.
Genesis1:13 Evening came
Genesis1:14 Then God said: Let there be lights in the dome of the sky
Genesis1:15 and serve as luminaries in the dome of the sky
Genesis1:16 God made the two great lights
Genesis1:17 God set them in the dome of the sky
Genesis1:18 to govern the day and the night
Genesis1:19 Evening came
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 God created the great sea monsters and all kinds of swimming creatures with which the water teems
Genesis1:22 and God blessed them
Genesis1:23 Evening came
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 God made all kinds of wild animals
Genesis1:26 Then God said: Let us make man in our image
Genesis1:27 God created man in his image; in the divine image he created him; male and female he created them.
Genesis1:28 God blessed them
Genesis1:29 God also said: See
Genesis1:30 and to all the animals of the land
Genesis1:31 God looked at everything he had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth and all their array were completed.
Genesis2:2 Since on the seventh day God was finished with the work he had been doing
Genesis2:3 So God blessed the seventh day and made it holy
Genesis2:4 Such is the story of the heavens and the earth at their creation. At the time when the LORD God made the earth and the heavens--
Genesis2:5 while as yet there was no field shrub on earth and no grass of the field had sprouted
Genesis2:6 but a stream was welling up out of the earth and was watering all the surface of the ground--
Genesis2:7 the LORD God formed man out of the clay of the ground and blew into his nostrils the breath of life
Genesis2:8 Then the LORD God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 Out of the ground the LORD God made various trees grow that were delightful to look at and good for food
Genesis2:10 A river rises in Eden to water the garden; beyond there it divides and becomes four branches.
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is the Pishon; it is the one that winds through the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 The gold of that land is excellent; bdellium and lapis lazuli are also there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is the Gihon; it is the one that winds all through the land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is the Tigris; it is the one that flows east of Asshur. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 The LORD God then took the man and settled him in the garden of Eden
Genesis2:16 The LORD God gave man this order: You are free to eat from any of the trees of the garden
Genesis2:17 except the tree of knowledge of good and bad. From that tree you shall not eat; the moment you eat from it you are surely doomed to die.
Genesis2:18 The LORD God said: It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.
Genesis2:19 So the LORD God formed out of the ground various wild animals and various birds of the air
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all the cattle
Genesis2:21 So the LORD God cast a deep sleep on the man
Genesis2:22 The LORD God then built up into a woman the rib that he had taken from the man. When he brought her to the man
Genesis2:23 the man said: This one
Genesis2:24 That is why a man leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife
Genesis2:25 The man and his wife were both naked
Genesis3:1 Now the serpent was the most cunning of all the animals that the LORD God had made. The serpent asked the woman
Genesis3:2 The woman answered the serpent: We may eat of the fruit of the trees in the garden;
Genesis3:3 it is only about the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden that God said
Genesis3:4 But the serpent said to the woman: You certainly will not die!
Genesis3:5 No
Genesis3:6 The woman saw that the tree was good for food
Genesis3:7 Then the eyes of both of them were opened
Genesis3:8 When they heard the sound of the LORD God moving about in the garden at the breezy time of the day

Genesis3:10 He answered
Genesis3:11 Then he asked
Genesis3:12 The man replied

Genesis4:2 Next she bore his brother Abel. Abel became a keeper of flocks
Genesis4:3 In the course of time Cain brought an offering to the LORD from the fruit of the soil
Genesis4:4 while Abel
Genesis4:5 but on Cain and his offering he did not. Cain greatly resented this and was crestfallen.

Genesis4:7 If you do well

Genesis4:9 Then the LORD asked Cain

Genesis5:1 This is the record of the descendants of Adam. When God created man
Genesis5:2 he created them male and female. When they were created
Genesis5:3 Adam was one hundred and thirty years old when he begot a son in his likeness

Genesis5:5 The whole lifetime of Adam was nine hundred and thirty years; then he died.
Genesis5:6 When Seth was one hundred and five years old
Genesis5:7 Seth lived eight hundred and seven years after the birth of Enosh

Genesis5:9 When Enosh was ninety years old

Genesis5:12 When Kenan was seventy years old
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived eight hundred and forty years after the birth of Mahalalel

Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel was sixty-five years old

Genesis5:19 Jared lived eight hundred years after the birth of Enoch

Genesis5:21 When Enoch was sixty-five years old

Genesis5:23 The whole lifetime of Enoch was three hundred and sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 Then Enoch walked with God
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah was one hundred and eighty-seven years old
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived seven hundred and eighty-two years after the birth of Lamech
Genesis5:27 The whole lifetime of Methuselah was nine hundred and sixty-nine years; then he died.

Exodus25:8 They shall make a sanctuary for me
Exodus25:9 This Dwelling and all its furnishings you shall make exactly according to the pattern that I will now show you.
Exodus25:10 You shall make an ark of acacia wood
Exodus25:11 Plate it inside and outside with pure gold
Exodus25:12 Cast four gold rings and fasten them on the four supports of the ark
Exodus25:13 Then make poles of acacia wood and plate them with gold.
Exodus25:14 These poles you are to put through the rings on the sides of the ark
Exodus25:15 they must remain in the rings of the ark and never be withdrawn.
Exodus25:16 In the ark you are to put the commandments which I will give you.
Exodus25:17 You shall then make a propitiatory of pure gold
Exodus25:18 Make two cherubim of beaten gold for the two ends of the propitiatory
Exodus25:19 fastening them so that one cherub springs direct from each end.
Exodus25:20 The cherubim shall have their wings spread out above
Exodus25:21 This propitiatory you shall then place on top of the ark. In the ark itself you are to put the commandments which I will give you.
Exodus25:22 There I will meet you and there
Exodus25:23 You shall also make a table of acacia wood
Exodus25:24 Plate it with pure gold and make a molding of gold around it.
Exodus25:25 Surround it with a frame
Exodus25:26 You shall also make four rings of gold for it and fasten them at the four corners
Exodus25:27 on two opposite sides of the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 These poles for carrying the table you shall make of acacia wood and plate with gold.
Exodus25:29 Of pure gold you shall make its plates and cups
Exodus25:30 On the table you shall always keep showbread set before me.
Exodus25:31 You shall make a lampstand of pure beaten gold-- its shaft and branches-- with its cups and knobs and petals springing directly from it.
Exodus25:32 Six branches are to extend from the sides of the lampstand
Exodus25:33 On one branch there are to be three cups
Exodus25:34 On the shaft there are to be four cups
Exodus25:35 including a knob below each of the three pairs of branches that extend from the lampstand.
Exodus25:36 Their knobs and branches shall so spring from it that the whole will form but a single piece of pure beaten gold.
Exodus25:37 You shall then make seven lamps for it and so set up the lamps that they shed their light on the space in front of the lampstand.
Exodus25:38 These
Exodus25:39 Use a talent of pure gold for the lampstand and all its appurtenances.
Exodus25:40 See that you make them according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 The Dwelling itself you shall make out of sheets woven of fine linen twined and of violet
Exodus26:2 The length of each shall be twenty-eight cubits
Exodus26:3 Five of the sheets are to be sewed together
Exodus26:4 Make loops of violet yarn along the edge of the end sheet in one set
Exodus26:5 There are to be fifty loops along the edge of the end sheet in the first set
Exodus26:6 Then make fifty clasps of gold
Exodus26:7 Also make sheets woven of goat hair
Exodus26:8 Eleven such sheets are to be made; the length of each shall be thirty cubits
Exodus26:9 Sew five of the sheets
Exodus26:10 Make fifty loops along the edge of the end sheet in one set
Exodus26:11 Also make fifty bronze clasps and put them into the loops
Exodus26:12 There will be an extra half sheet of tent covering
Exodus26:13 Likewise
Exodus26:14 Over the tent itself you shall make a covering of rams' skins dyed red
Exodus26:15 You shall make boards of acacia wood as walls for the Dwelling.
Exodus26:16 The length of each board is to be ten cubits
Exodus26:17 Each board shall have two arms that shall serve to fasten the boards in line. In this way all the boards of the Dwelling are to be made.
Exodus26:18 Set up the boards of the Dwelling as follows: twenty boards on the south side
Exodus26:19 with forty silver pedestals under the twenty boards
Exodus26:20 twenty boards on the other side of the Dwelling
Exodus26:21 with their forty silver pedestals
Exodus26:22 six boards for the rear of the Dwelling
Exodus26:23 and two boards for the corners at the rear of the Dwelling.
Exodus26:24 These two shall be double at the bottom
Exodus26:25 Thus
Exodus26:26 Also make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards on one side of the Dwelling
Exodus26:27 five for those on the other side
Exodus26:28 The center bar
Exodus26:29 Plate the boards with gold
Exodus26:30 You shall erect the Dwelling according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 You shall have a veil woven of violet
Exodus26:32 It is to be hung on four gold-plated columns of acacia wood
Exodus26:33 Hang the veil from clasps. The ark of the commandments you shall bring inside
Exodus26:34 Set the propitiatory on the ark of the commandments in the holy of holies.
Exodus26:35 Outside the veil you shall place the table and the lampstand
Exodus26:36 For the entrance of the tent make a variegated curtain of violet
Exodus26:37 Make five columns of acacia wood for this curtain; have them plated with gold

Exodus27:2 At the four corners there are to be horns

Exodus27:5 Put it down around the altar

Exodus27:12 On the west side

Exodus27:15 on the other side there shall be hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits

Exodus28:1 From among the Israelites have your brother Aaron
Exodus28:2 For the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron you shall have sacred vestments made.
Exodus28:3 Therefore
Exodus28:4 These are the vestments they shall make: a breastpiece

Exodus28:6 The ephod they shall make of gold thread and of violet

Exodus28:11 As a gem-cutter engraves a seal

Exodus28:14 as well as two chains of pure gold

Exodus28:19 in the third row

Exodus28:38 over Aaron's forehead. Since Aaron bears whatever guilt the Israelites may incur in consecrating any of their sacred gifts

Exodus29:1 This is the rite you shall perform in consecrating them as my priests. Procure a young bull and two unblemished rams.
Exodus29:2 With fine wheat flour make unleavened cakes mixed with oil
Exodus29:3 and put them in a basket. Take the basket of them along with the bullock and the two rams.
Exodus29:4 Aaron and his sons you shall also bring to the entrance of the meeting tent
Exodus29:5 Take the vestments and clothe Aaron with the tunic
Exodus29:6 Put the miter on his head
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and anoint him with it
Exodus29:8 Bring forward his sons also and clothe them with the tunics
Exodus29:9 gird them with the sashes
Exodus29:10 Now bring forward the bullock in front of the meeting tent. There Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on its head.
Exodus29:11 Then slaughter the bullock before the LORD
Exodus29:12 Take some of its blood and with your finger put it on the horns of the altar. All the rest of the blood you shall pour out at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 All the fat that covers its inner organs
Exodus29:14 But the flesh and hide and offal of the bullock you must burn up outside the camp
Exodus29:15 Then take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 slaughter it. The blood you shall take and splash on all the sides of the altar.
Exodus29:17 Cut the ram into pieces; its inner organs and shanks you shall first wash
Exodus29:18 The entire ram shall then be burned on the altar
Exodus29:19 After this take the other ram
Exodus29:20 slaughter it. Some of its blood you shall take and put on the tip of Aaron's right ear and on the tips of his sons' right ears and on the thumbs of their right hands and the great toes of their right feet. Splash the rest of the blood on all the sides of the altar.
Exodus29:21 Then take some of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 Now
Exodus29:23 then
Exodus29:24 All these things you shall put into the hands of Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:25 After you have received them back from their hands
Exodus29:26 Finally
Exodus29:27 Thus shall you set aside the breast of whatever wave offering is waved
Exodus29:28 Such things are due to Aaron and his sons from the Israelites by a perpetual ordinance as a contribution. From their peace offerings
Exodus29:29 The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed down to his descendants
Exodus29:30 The descendant who succeeds him as priest and who is to enter the meeting tent to minister in the sanctuary shall be clothed with them for seven days.
Exodus29:31 You shall take the flesh of the ordination ram and boil it in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 At the entrance of the meeting tent Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket.
Exodus29:33 They themselves are to eat of these things by which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration; but no layman may eat of them
Exodus29:34 If some of the flesh of the ordination sacrifice or some of the bread remains over on the next day
Exodus29:35 Carry out all these orders in regard to Aaron and his sons just as I have given them to you. Seven days you shall spend in ordaining them
Exodus29:36 sacrificing a bullock each day as a sin offering
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall spend in making atonement for the altar and in consecrating it. Then the altar will be most sacred
Exodus29:38 Now
Exodus29:39 one lamb in the morning and the other lamb at the evening twilight.
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb there shall be a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil of crushed olives and
Exodus29:41 The other lamb you shall offer at the evening twilight
Exodus29:42 Throughout your generations this established holocaust shall be offered before the LORD at the entrance of the meeting tent
Exodus29:43 There
Exodus29:44 Thus I will consecrate the meeting tent and the altar
Exodus29:45 I will dwell in the midst of the Israelites and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 They shall know that I

Exodus40:11 Likewise

Exodus40:14 Bring forward his sons also
Exodus40:15 As you have anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Moses did exactly as the LORD had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 On the first day of the first month of the second year the Dwelling was erected.
Exodus40:18 It was Moses who erected the Dwelling. He placed its pedestals
Exodus40:19 He spread the tent over the Dwelling and put the covering on top of the tent
Exodus40:20 He took the commandments and put them in the ark; he placed poles alongside the ark and set the propitiatory upon it.

Exodus40:27 and on it he burned fragrant incense
Exodus40:28 He hung the curtain at the entrance of the Dwelling.

Exodus40:30 He placed the laver between the meeting tent and the altar

Leviticus2:6 Such a cereal offering must be broken into pieces

Leviticus6:3 The priest
Leviticus6:4 Then
Leviticus6:5 The fire on the altar is to be kept burning; it must not go out. Every morning the priest shall put firewood on it. On this he shall lay out the holocaust and burn the fat of the peace offerings.
Leviticus6:6 The fire is to be kept burning continuously on the altar; it must not go out.
Leviticus6:7 This is the ritual of the cereal offering. One of Aaron's sons shall first present it before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 Then he shall take from it a handful of its fine flour and oil
Leviticus6:9 The rest of it Aaron and his sons may eat; but it must be eaten in the form of unleavened cakes and in a sacred place: in the court of the meeting tent they shall eat it.
Leviticus6:10 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it to them as their portion from the oblations of the LORD; it is most sacred
Leviticus6:11 All the male descendants of Aaron may partake of it as their rightful share in the oblations of the LORD perpetually throughout your generations. Whatever touches the oblations becomes sacred.
Leviticus6:12 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:13 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall present to the LORD (on the day he is anointed): one tenth of an ephah of fine flour for the established cereal offering
Leviticus6:14 It shall be well kneaded and fried in oil on a griddle when you bring it in. Having broken the offering into pieces
Leviticus6:15 Aaron's descendant who succeeds him as the anointed priest shall do likewise. This is a perpetual ordinance: for the Lord the whole offering shall be burned.
Leviticus6:16 Every cereal offering of a priest shall be a whole burnt offering; it may not be eaten.
Leviticus6:17 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:18 Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the ritual for sin offerings. At the place where holocausts are slaughtered
Leviticus6:19 The priest who presents the sin offering may partake of it; but it must be eaten in a sacred place
Leviticus6:20 Whatever touches its flesh shall become sacred. If any of its blood is spilled on a garment
Leviticus6:21 A clay vessel in which it has been cooked shall thereafter be broken; if it is cooked in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:22 All the males of the priestly line may partake of the sin offering
Leviticus6:23 But no one may partake of any sin offering of which some blood has been brought into the meeting tent to make atonement in the sanctuary; such an offering must be burned up in the fire.

Leviticus11:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: Of all land animals these are the ones you may eat:
Leviticus11:3 any animal that has hoofs you may eat

Leviticus11:7 which does indeed have hoofs and is cloven-footed
Leviticus11:8 Their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:9 Of the various creatures that live in the water
Leviticus11:10 But of the various creatures that crawl or swim in the water
Leviticus11:11 and you shall treat them as loathsome. Their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:12 Every water creature that lacks fins or scales is loathsome for you.
Leviticus11:13 Of the birds
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 the various species of crows
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the owl
Leviticus11:18 the barn owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 The various winged insects that walk on all fours are loathsome for you.
Leviticus11:21 But of the various winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs for leaping on the ground;
Leviticus11:22 hence of these you may eat the following: the various kinds of locusts
Leviticus11:23 All other winged insects that have four legs are loathsome for you.
Leviticus11:24 Such is the uncleanness that you contract
Leviticus11:25 and everyone who picks up any part of their dead bodies shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:26 All hoofed animals that are not cloven-footed or do not chew the cud are unclean for you; everyone who touches them becomes unclean.
Leviticus11:27 Of the various quadrupeds
Leviticus11:28 and everyone who picks up their dead bodies shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening. Such is their uncleanness for you.
Leviticus11:29 Of the creatures that swarm on the ground
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 Among the various swarming creatures
Leviticus11:32 Everything on which one of them falls when dead becomes unclean. Any such article that men use
Leviticus11:33 Should any of these creatures fall into a clay vessel
Leviticus11:34 Any solid food that was in contact with water
Leviticus11:35 Any object on which one of their dead bodies falls
Leviticus11:36 However
Leviticus11:37 Any sort of cultivated grain remains clean even though one of their dead bodies falls on it;
Leviticus11:38 but if the grain has become moistened
Leviticus11:39 When one of the animals that you could otherwise eat
Leviticus11:40 and anyone who eats of its dead body shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening; so also
Leviticus11:41 All the creatures that swarm on the ground are loathsome and shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whether it crawls on its belly
Leviticus11:43 Do not make yourselves loathsome or unclean with any swarming creature through being contaminated by them.

Leviticus11:45 Since I
Leviticus11:46 This is the law for animals and birds and for all the creatures that move about in the water or swarm on the ground
Leviticus11:47 that you may distinguish between the clean and the unclean

Leviticus15:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: Every man who is afflicted with a chronic flow from his private parts is thereby unclean.
Leviticus15:3 Such is his uncleanness from this flow that it makes no difference whether the flow drains off or is blocked up; his uncleanness remains.
Leviticus15:4 Any bed on which the man afflicted with the flow lies
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches his bed shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:6 Whoever sits on a piece of furniture on which the afflicted man was sitting
Leviticus15:7 Whoever touches the body of the afflicted man shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:8 If the afflicted man spits on a clean man
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle on which the afflicted man rides
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until evening; whoever lifts up any such thing shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:11 Anyone whom the afflicted man touches with unrinsed hands shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:12 Earthenware touched by the afflicted man shall be broken; and every wooden article shall be rinsed with water.
Leviticus15:13 When a man who has been afflicted with a flow becomes free of his affliction
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus15:15 who shall offer them up
Leviticus15:16 When a man has an emission of seed
Leviticus15:17 Any piece of cloth or leather with seed on it shall be washed with water and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:18 If a man lies carnally with a woman
Leviticus15:19 When a woman has her menstrual flow
Leviticus15:20 Anything on which she lies or sits during her impurity shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 Anyone who touches her bed shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches any article of furniture on which she was sitting
Leviticus15:23 But if she is on the bed or on the seat when he touches it
Leviticus15:24 If a man dares to lie with her
Leviticus15:25 When a woman is afflicted with a flow of blood for several days outside her menstrual period
Leviticus15:26 Any bed on which she lies during such a flow becomes unclean
Leviticus15:27 Anyone who touches them becomes unclean; he shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:28 If she becomes freed from her affliction
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance of the meeting tent.
Leviticus15:30 The priest shall offer up one of them as a sin offering and the other as a holocaust. Thus shall the priest make atonement before the LORD for her unclean flow.
Leviticus15:31 You shall warn the Israelites of their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for the man who is afflicted with a chronic flow
Leviticus15:33 as well as for the woman who has her menstrual period

Leviticus16:22 Since the goat is to carry off their iniquities to an isolated region
Leviticus16:23 After Aaron has again gone into the meeting tent
Leviticus16:24 After bathing his body with water in a sacred place
Leviticus16:25 and also burn the fat of the sin offering on the altar.

Leviticus16:27 The sin-offering bullock and goat whose blood was brought into the sanctuary to make atonement
Leviticus16:28 The one who burns them shall wash his garments and bathe his body in water; only then may he enter the camp.

Leviticus18:4 My decrees you shall carry out
Leviticus18:5 Keep
Leviticus18:6 None of you shall approach a close relative to have sexual intercourse with her. I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:7 You shall not disgrace your father by having intercourse with your mother. Besides
Leviticus18:8 You shall not have intercourse with your father's wife
Leviticus18:9 You shall not have intercourse with your sister
Leviticus18:10 You shall not have intercourse with your son's daughter or with your daughter's daughter
Leviticus18:11 You shall not have intercourse with the daughter whom your father's wife bore to him
Leviticus18:12 You shall not have intercourse with your father's sister
Leviticus18:13 You shall not have intercourse with your mother's sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not disgrace your father's brother by being intimate with his wife
Leviticus18:15 You shall not have intercourse with your daughter-in-law; she is your son's wife
Leviticus18:16 You shall not have intercourse with your brother's wife
Leviticus18:17 You shall not have intercourse with a woman and also with her daughter
Leviticus18:18 While your wife is still living you shall not marry her sister as her rival; for thus you would disgrace your first wife.
Leviticus18:19 You shall not approach a woman to have intercourse with her while she is unclean from menstruation.
Leviticus18:20 You shall not have carnal relations with your neighbor's wife
Leviticus18:21 You shall not offer any of your offspring to be immolated to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; such a thing is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 You shall not have carnal relations with an animal
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves by any of these things by which the nations whom I am driving out of your way have defiled themselves.
Leviticus18:25 Because their land has become defiled
Leviticus18:26 You
Leviticus18:27 by which the previous inhabitants defiled the land;
Leviticus18:28 otherwise the land will vomit you out also for having defiled it
Leviticus18:29 Everyone who does any of these abominations shall be cut off from among his people.

Leviticus19:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Speak to the whole Israelite community and tell them: Be holy
Leviticus19:3 Revere your mother and father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn aside to idols
Leviticus19:5 When you sacrifice your peace offering to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 it must be eaten on the very day of your sacrifice or on the following day. Whatever is left over until the third day shall be burned up in the fire.
Leviticus19:7 If any of it is eaten on the third day
Leviticus19:8 whoever eats of it then shall pay the penalty for having profaned what is sacred to the LORD. Such a one shall be cut off from his people.
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 Likewise
Leviticus19:11 You shall not steal. You shall not lie or speak falsely to one another.
Leviticus19:12 You shall not swear falsely by my name
Leviticus19:13 You shall not defraud or rob your neighbor. You shall not withhold overnight the wages of your day laborer.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf
Leviticus19:15 You shall not act dishonestly in rendering judgment. Show neither partiality to the weak nor deference to the mighty
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go about spreading slander among your kinsmen; nor shall you stand by idly when your neighbor's life is at stake. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:17 You shall not bear hatred for your brother in your heart. Though you may have to reprove your fellow man
Leviticus19:18 Take no revenge and cherish no grudge against your fellow countrymen. You shall love your neighbor as yourself. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:19 Keep my statutes: do not breed any of your domestic animals with others of a different species; do not sow a field of yours with two different kinds of seed; and do not put on a garment woven with two different kinds of thread.

Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant any fruit tree there
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year
Leviticus19:25 Not until the fifth year may you eat its fruit. Thus it will continue its yield for you. I
Leviticus19:26 Do not eat meat with the blood still in it. Do not practice divination or soothsaying.
Leviticus19:27 Do not clip your hair at the temples
Leviticus19:28 Do not lacerate your bodies for the dead
Leviticus19:29 You shall not degrade your daughter by making a prostitute of her; else the land will become corrupt and full of lewdness.
Leviticus19:30 Keep my sabbaths
Leviticus19:31 Do not go to mediums or consult fortune-tellers
Leviticus19:32 Stand up in the presence of the aged
Leviticus19:33 When an alien resides with you in your land

Leviticus19:36 You shall have a true scale and true weights

Leviticus22:6 the one who touches such as these shall be unclean until evening and may not eat of the sacred portions until he has first bathed his body in water
Leviticus22:7 then
Leviticus22:8 He shall not make himself unclean by eating of any animal that has died of itself or has been killed by wild beasts. I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:9 They shall keep my charge and not do wrong in this matter; else they will die for their profanation. I am the LORD who have consecrated them.
Leviticus22:10 Neither a lay person nor a priest's tenant or hired servant may eat of any sacred offering.
Leviticus22:11 But a slave whom a priest acquires by purchase or who is born in his house may eat of his food.
Leviticus22:12 A priest's daughter who is married to a layman may not eat of the sacred contributions.
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced and
Leviticus22:14 If such a one eats of a sacred offering through inadvertence
Leviticus22:15 The sacred offerings which the Israelites contribute to the LORD the priests shall not allow to be profaned
Leviticus22:16 nor in the eating of the sacred offering shall they bring down guilt that must be punished; it is I
Leviticus22:17 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites
Leviticus22:19 if it is to be acceptable
Leviticus22:20 You shall not offer one that has any defect
Leviticus22:21 When anyone presents a peace offering to the LORD from the herd or the flock in fulfillment of a vow
Leviticus22:22 One that is blind or crippled or maimed
Leviticus22:23 An ox or a sheep that is in any way ill-proportioned or stunted you may indeed present as a free-will offering
Leviticus22:24 One that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn out or cut off you shall not offer to the LORD. You shall neither do this in your own land
Leviticus22:25 nor receive from a foreigner any such animals to offer up as the food of your God; since they are deformed or defective
Leviticus22:26 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When an ox or a lamb or a goat is born
Leviticus22:28 You shall not slaughter an ox or a sheep on one and the same day with its young.
Leviticus22:29 Whenever you offer a thanksgiving sacrifice to the LORD
Leviticus22:30 it must
Leviticus22:31 Be careful to observe the commandments which I
Leviticus22:32 and do not profane my holy name; in the midst of the Israelites I
Leviticus22:33 and led you out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus23:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: The following are the festivals of the LORD
Leviticus23:3 For six days work may be done; but the seventh day is the sabbath rest
Leviticus23:4 These
Leviticus23:5 The Passover of the LORD falls on the fourteenth day of the first month
Leviticus23:6 The fifteenth day of this month is the LORD'S feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first of these days you shall hold a sacred assembly and do no sort of work.
Leviticus23:8 On each of the seven days you shall offer an oblation to the LORD. Then on the seventh day you shall again hold a sacred assembly and do no sort of work.
Leviticus23:9 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you come into the land which I am giving you
Leviticus23:11 who shall wave the sheaf before the LORD that it may be acceptable for you. On the day after the sabbath the priest shall do this.
Leviticus23:12 On this day
Leviticus23:13 Its cereal offering shall be two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 Until this day
Leviticus23:15 Beginning with the day after the sabbath
Leviticus23:16 and then on the day after the seventh week
Leviticus23:17 For the wave offering of your first fruits to the LORD
Leviticus23:18 Besides the bread
Leviticus23:19 One male goat shall be sacrificed as a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall wave the bread of the first fruits and the two lambs as a wave offering before the LORD; these shall be sacred to the LORD and belong to the priest.
Leviticus23:21 On this same day you shall by proclamation have a sacred assembly
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Tell the Israelites: On the first day of the seventh month you shall keep a sabbath rest
Leviticus23:25 you shall then do no sort of work
Leviticus23:26 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 The tenth of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement
Leviticus23:28 On this day you shall not do any work
Leviticus23:29 Anyone who does not mortify himself on this day shall be cut off from his people
Leviticus23:30 and if anyone does any work on this day
Leviticus23:31 This is a perpetual statute for you and your descendants wherever you dwell: you shall do no work
Leviticus23:32 but shall keep a sabbath of complete rest and mortify yourselves. Beginning on the evening of the ninth of the month
Leviticus23:33 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Tell the Israelites: The fifteenth day of this seventh month is the LORD'S feast of Booths
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there shall be a sacred assembly
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall offer an oblation to the LORD
Leviticus23:37 These
Leviticus23:38 in addition to those of the LORD'S sabbaths
Leviticus23:39 On the fifteenth day
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you shall gather foliage from majestic trees
Leviticus23:41 By perpetual statute for you and your descendants you shall keep this pilgrim feast of the LORD for one whole week in the seventh month of the year.
Leviticus23:42 During this week every native Israelite among you shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 that your descendants may realize that
Leviticus23:44 Thus did Moses announce to the Israelites the festivals of the LORD.
Leviticus24:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Order the Israelites to bring you clear oil of crushed olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 In the meeting tent
Leviticus24:4 the lamps shall be set up on the pure gold lampstand
Leviticus24:5 You shall take fine flour and bake it into twelve cakes
Leviticus24:6 These you shall place in two piles
Leviticus24:7 On each pile put some pure frankincense
Leviticus24:8 Regularly on each sabbath day this bread shall be set out afresh before the LORD
Leviticus24:9 It shall belong to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Among the Israelites there was a man born of an Israelite mother (Shelomith
Leviticus24:11 This man quarreled publicly with another Israelite and cursed and blasphemed the LORD'S name. So the people brought him to Moses
Leviticus24:12 who kept him in custody till a decision from the LORD should settle the case for them.
Leviticus24:13 The LORD then said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Take the blasphemer outside the camp
Leviticus24:15 Tell the Israelites: Anyone who curses his God shall bear the penalty of his sin;
Leviticus24:16 whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD shall be put to death. The whole community shall stone him; alien and native alike must be put to death for blaspheming the LORD'S name.
Leviticus24:17 Whoever takes the life of any human being shall be put to death;
Leviticus24:18 whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution of another animal. A life for a life!
Leviticus24:19 Anyone who inflicts an injury on his neighbor shall receive the same in return.
Leviticus24:20 Limb for limb
Leviticus24:21 Whoever slays an animal shall make restitution
Leviticus24:22 You shall have but one rule
Leviticus24:23 When Moses told this to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 The LORD said to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land that I am giving you
Leviticus25:3 For six years you may sow your field
Leviticus25:4 But during the seventh year the land shall have a complete rest
Leviticus25:5 The aftergrowth of your harvest you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 While the land has its sabbath
Leviticus25:7 and likewise for your livestock and for the wild animals on your land.
Leviticus25:8 Seven weeks of years shall you count-- seven times seven years-- so that the seven cycles amount to forty-nine years.
Leviticus25:9 Then
Leviticus25:10 This fiftieth year you shall make sacred by proclaiming liberty in the land for all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you
Leviticus25:11 In this fiftieth year
Leviticus25:12 Since this is the jubilee
Leviticus25:13 In this year of jubilee
Leviticus25:14 Therefore
Leviticus25:15 On the basis of the number of years since the last jubilee shall you purchase the land from him; and so also
Leviticus25:16 When the years are many
Leviticus25:17 Do not deal unfairly
Leviticus25:18 Observe my precepts and be careful to keep my regulations
Leviticus25:19 The land will yield its fruit and you will have food in abundance
Leviticus25:20 Therefore
Leviticus25:21 I will bestow such blessings on you in the sixth year that there will then be crop enough for three years.
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be sold in perpetuity; for the land is mine
Leviticus25:24 Therefore
Leviticus25:25 When one of your countrymen is reduced to poverty and has to sell some of his property
Leviticus25:26 If
Leviticus25:27 he shall make a deduction from the price in proportion to the number of years since the sale
Leviticus25:28 But if he does not acquire sufficient means to buy back his land
Leviticus25:29 When someone sells a dwelling in a walled town
Leviticus25:30 But if such a house in a walled town has not been redeemed at the end of a full year
Leviticus25:31 However
Leviticus25:32 In levitical cities the Levites shall always have the right to redeem the town houses that are their property.
Leviticus25:33 Any town house of the Levites in their cities that had been sold and not redeemed
Leviticus25:34 Moreover
Leviticus25:35 When one of your fellow countrymen is reduced to poverty and is unable to hold out beside you
Leviticus25:36 Do not exact interest from your countryman either in money or in kind
Leviticus25:37 You are to lend him neither money at interest nor food at a profit.
Leviticus25:38 I
Leviticus25:39 When
Leviticus25:40 Rather
Leviticus25:41 when he
Leviticus25:42 Since those whom I brought out of the land of Egypt are servants of mine
Leviticus25:43 Do not lord it over them harshly
Leviticus25:44 Slaves
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy them from among the aliens who reside with you and from their children who are born and reared in your land. Such slaves you may own as chattels
Leviticus25:46 and leave to your sons as their hereditary property
Leviticus25:47 When one of your countrymen is reduced to such poverty that he sells himself to a wealthy alien who has a permanent or a temporary residence among you
Leviticus25:48 even after he has thus sold his services he still has the right of redemption; he may be redeemed by one of his own brothers
Leviticus25:49 or by his uncle or cousin
Leviticus25:50 With his purchaser he shall compute the years from the sale to the jubilee
Leviticus25:51 The more such years there are
Leviticus25:52 the fewer years there are left before the jubilee year
Leviticus25:53 The alien shall treat him as a servant hired on an annual basis
Leviticus25:54 If he is not thus redeemed
Leviticus25:55 For to me the Israelites belong as servants; they are servants of mine
Leviticus26:1 Do not make false gods for yourselves. You shall not erect an idol or a sacred pillar for yourselves
Leviticus26:2 Keep my sabbaths
Leviticus26:3 If you live in accordance with my precepts and are careful to observe my commandments
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rain in due season
Leviticus26:5 your threshing will last till vintage time
Leviticus26:6 I will establish peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 You will rout your enemies and lay them low with your sword.
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will put a hundred of your foes to flight
Leviticus26:9 I will look with favor upon you
Leviticus26:10 So much of the old crops will you have stored up for food that you will have to discard them to make room for the new.
Leviticus26:11 I will set my Dwelling among you
Leviticus26:12 Ever present in your midst
Leviticus26:13 for it is I
Leviticus26:14 But if you do not heed me and do not keep all these commandments
Leviticus26:15 if you reject my precepts and spurn my decrees
Leviticus26:16 then I
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you
Leviticus26:18 If even after this you do not obey me
Leviticus26:19 to break your haughty confidence. I will make the sky above you as hard as iron
Leviticus26:20 so that your strength will be spent in vain; your land will bear no crops
Leviticus26:21 If then you become defiant in your unwillingness to obey me
Leviticus26:22 I will unleash the wild beasts against you
Leviticus26:23 If
Leviticus26:24 I
Leviticus26:25 I will make the sword
Leviticus26:26 And as I cut off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 If
Leviticus26:28 I
Leviticus26:29 till you begin to eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will demolish your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will lay waste your cities and devastate your sanctuaries
Leviticus26:32 So devastated will I leave the land that your very enemies who come to live there will stand aghast at the sight of it.
Leviticus26:33 You yourselves I will scatter among the nations at the point of my drawn sword
Leviticus26:34 Then shall the land retrieve its lost sabbaths during all the time it lies waste
Leviticus26:35 during all the time that it lies desolate
Leviticus26:36 Those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies I will make so fainthearted that
Leviticus26:37 stumbling over one another as if to escape a weapon
Leviticus26:38 You will be lost among the Gentiles
Leviticus26:39 Those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies will waste away for their own and their fathers' guilt.
Leviticus26:40 Thus they will have to confess that they and their fathers were guilty of having rebelled against me and of having defied me
Leviticus26:41 so that I
Leviticus26:42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 But the land must first be rid of them
Leviticus26:44 Yet even so
Leviticus26:45 I will remember them because of the covenant I made with their forefathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the precepts
Leviticus27:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When anyone fulfills a vow of offering one or more persons to the LORD
Leviticus27:3 for persons between the ages of twenty and sixty
Leviticus27:4 and thirty shekels for a woman;
Leviticus27:5 for persons between the ages of five and twenty
Leviticus27:6 for persons between the ages of one month and five years
Leviticus27:7 for persons of sixty or more
Leviticus27:8 However
Leviticus27:9 If the offering vowed to the LORD is an animal that may be sacrificed
Leviticus27:10 The offerer shall not present a substitute for it by exchanging either a better for a worse one or a worse for a better one. If he attempts to offer one animal in place of another
Leviticus27:11 If the animal vowed to the LORD is unclean and therefore unfit for sacrifice
Leviticus27:12 who shall determine its value in keeping with its good or bad qualities
Leviticus27:13 If the offerer wishes to redeem the animal
Leviticus27:14 When someone dedicates his house as sacred to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 If the one who dedicated his house wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:16 If the object which someone dedicates to the LORD is a piece of his hereditary land
Leviticus27:17 If the dedication of a field is made at the beginning of a jubilee period
Leviticus27:18 but if it is some time after this
Leviticus27:19 If the one who dedicated his field wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 If
Leviticus27:21 but at the jubilee it shall be released as sacred to the LORD; like a field that is doomed
Leviticus27:22 If the field that some man dedicates to the LORD is one he had purchased and not a part of his hereditary property
Leviticus27:23 the priest shall compute its value in proportion to the number of years until the next jubilee
Leviticus27:24 at the jubilee
Leviticus27:25 Every valuation shall be made according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel. There are twenty gerahs to the shekel.
Leviticus27:26 Note that a first-born animal
Leviticus27:27 but if it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 Note
Leviticus27:29 All human beings that are doomed lose the right to be redeemed; they must be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 All tithes of the land
Leviticus27:31 If someone wishes to buy back any of his tithes
Leviticus27:32 The tithes of the herd and the flock shall be determined by ceding to the LORD as sacred every tenth animal as they are counted by the herdsman's rod.
Leviticus27:33 It shall not matter whether good ones or bad ones are thus chosen
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the LORD gave Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
Numbers1:1 In the year following that of the Israelites' departure from the land of Egypt
Numbers1:2 Take a census of the whole community of the Israelites
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron shall enroll in companies all the men in Israel of twenty years or more who are fit for military service.
Numbers1:4 To assist you there shall be a man from each tribe
Numbers1:5 These are the names of those who are to assist you: from Reuben: Elizur
Numbers1:6 from Simeon: Shelumiel
Numbers1:7 from Judah: Nahshon
Numbers1:8 from Issachar: Nethanel
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun: Eliab
Numbers1:10 from Ephraim: Elishama
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin: Abidan
Numbers1:12 from Dan: Ahiezer
Numbers1:13 from Asher: Pagiel
Numbers1:14 from Gad: Eliasaph
Numbers1:15 from Naphtali: Ahira
Numbers1:16 These were councilors of the community
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated
Numbers1:18 and assembled the whole community on the first day of the second month. Every man of twenty years or more then declared his name and lineage according to clan and ancestral house
Numbers1:19 as the LORD had commanded Moses. This is their census as taken in the desert of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 Of the descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 forty-six thousand five hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Reuben.
Numbers1:22 Of the descendants of Simeon
Numbers1:23 fifty-nine thousand three hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Simeon.
Numbers1:24 Of the descendants of Gad
Numbers1:25 forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty were enrolled in the tribe of Gad.
Numbers1:26 Of the descendants of Judah
Numbers1:27 seventy-four thousand six hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Judah.
Numbers1:28 Of the descendants of Issachar
Numbers1:29 fifty-four thousand four hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Issachar.
Numbers1:30 Of the descendants of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 fifty-seven thousand four hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Zebulun.
Numbers1:32 Of the descendants of Joseph-- Of the descendants of Ephraim
Numbers1:33 forty thousand five hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Ephraim.
Numbers1:34 Of the descendants of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 thirty-two thousand two hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Manasseh.
Numbers1:36 Of the descendants of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 thirty-five thousand four hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Benjamin.
Numbers1:38 Of the descendants of Dan
Numbers1:39 sixty-two thousand seven hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Dan.
Numbers1:40 Of the descendants of Asher
Numbers1:41 forty-one thousand five hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Asher.
Numbers1:42 Of the descendants of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 fifty-three thousand four hundred were enrolled in the tribe of Naphtali.
Numbers1:44 It was these who were registered
Numbers1:45 The total number of the Israelites of twenty years or more who were fit for military service
Numbers1:46 was six hundred and three thousand
Numbers1:47 The Levites
Numbers1:48 For the LORD had told Moses
Numbers1:49 The tribe of Levi alone you shall not enroll nor include in the census along with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 You are to give the Levites charge of the Dwelling of the commandments with all its equipment and all that belongs to it. It is they who shall carry the Dwelling with all its equipment and who shall be its ministers. They shall therefore camp around the Dwelling.
Numbers1:51 When the Dwelling is to move on
Numbers1:52 While the other Israelites shall camp by companies
Numbers1:53 the Levites shall camp around the Dwelling of the commandments. Otherwise God's wrath will strike the Israelite community. The Levites
Numbers1:54 All this the Israelites fulfilled as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers2:2 The Israelites shall camp
Numbers2:3 Encamped on the east side
Numbers2:4 and his soldiers amounted in the census to seventy-four thousand six hundred.)
Numbers2:5 With Judah shall camp the tribe of Issachar (Their prince was Nethanel
Numbers2:6 and his soldiers amounted in the census to fifty-four thousand four hundred.)
Numbers2:7 and the tribe of Zebulun. (Their prince was Eliab
Numbers2:8 and his soldiers amounted in the census to fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:9 The total number of those registered by companies in the camp of Judah was one hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred.) These shall be first on the march.
Numbers2:10 On the south side shall be the divisional camp of Reuben
Numbers2:11 and his soldiers amounted in the census to forty-six thousand five hundred.)
Numbers2:12 Beside them shall camp the tribe of Simeon (Their prince was Shelumiel
Numbers2:13 and his soldiers amounted in the census to fifty-nine thousand three hundred.)
Numbers2:14 and next the tribe of Gad. (Their prince was Eliasaph
Numbers2:15 and his soldiers amounted in the census to forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:16 The total number of those registered by companies in the camp of Reuben was one hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty.) These shall be second on the march.
Numbers2:17 Then the meeting tent and the camp of the Levites shall set out in the middle of the line. As in camp
Numbers2:18 On the west side shall be the divisional camp of Ephraim
Numbers2:19 and his soldiers amounted in the census to forty thousand five hundred.)
Numbers2:20 Beside them shall camp the tribe of Manasseh (Their prince was Gamaliel
Numbers2:21 and his soldiers amounted in the census to thirty-two thousand two hundred.)
Numbers2:22 and the tribe of Benjamin. (Their prince was Abidan
Numbers2:23 and his soldiers amounted in the census to thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:24 The total number of those registered by companies in the camp of Ephraim was one hundred and eight thousand one hundred.) These shall be third on the march.
Numbers2:25 On the north side shall be the divisional camp of Dan
Numbers2:26 and his soldiers amounted in the census to sixty-two thousand seven hundred.)
Numbers2:27 Beside them shall camp the tribe of Asher (Their prince was Pagiel
Numbers2:28 and his soldiers amounted in the census to forty-one thousand five hundred.)
Numbers2:29 and next the tribe of Naphtali. (Their prince was Ahira
Numbers2:30 and his soldiers amounted in the census to fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:31 The total number of those registered by companies in the camp of Dan was one hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred.) These shall be the last of the divisions on the march.
Numbers2:32 This was the census of the Israelites taken by ancestral houses. The total number of those registered by companies in the camps was six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:33 The Levites
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses; both in camp and on the march they were in their own divisions
Numbers3:1 The following were the descendants of Aaron and Moses at the time that the LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 The sons of Aaron were Nadab his first-born
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But when Nadab and Abihu offered profane fire before the LORD in the desert of Sinai
Numbers3:5 Now the LORD said to Moses:
Numbers3:6 Summon the tribe of Levi and present them to Aaron the priest
Numbers3:7 They shall discharge his obligations and those of the whole community before the meeting tent by serving at the Dwelling.
Numbers3:8 They shall have custody of all the furnishings of the meeting tent and discharge the duties of the Israelites in the service of the Dwelling.
Numbers3:9 You shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they have been set aside from among the Israelites as dedicated to me.
Numbers3:10 But only Aaron and his descendants shall you appoint to have charge of the priestly functions. Any layman who comes near shall be put to death.
Numbers3:11 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:12 It is I who have chosen the Levites from the Israelites in place of every first-born that opens the womb among the Israelites. The Levites
Numbers3:13 because every first-born is mine. When I slew all the first-born in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 The LORD said to Moses in the desert of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Take a census of the Levites by ancestral houses and clans
Numbers3:16 Moses
Numbers3:17 The sons of Levi were named Gershon
Numbers3:18 The descendants of Gershon
Numbers3:19 The descendants of Kohath
Numbers3:20 The descendants of Merari
Numbers3:21 To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 When all their males of a month or more were registered
Numbers3:23 The clans of the Gershonites camped behind the Dwelling
Numbers3:24 The prince of their ancestral house was Eliasaph
Numbers3:25 At the meeting tent they had charge of whatever pertained to the Dwelling
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 To Kohath belonged the clans of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 When all their males of a month or more were registered
Numbers3:29 The clans of the Kohathites camped at the south side of the Dwelling.
Numbers3:30 The prince of their ancestral house was Elizaphan
Numbers3:31 They had charge of whatever pertained to the ark
Numbers3:32 The chief prince of the Levites
Numbers3:33 To Merari belonged the clans of the Mahlites and the Mushites; these were the clans of Merari.
Numbers3:34 When all their males of a month or more were registered
Numbers3:35 The prince of the ancestral house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel
Numbers3:36 The Merarites were charged with the care of whatever pertained to the boards of the Dwelling
Numbers3:37 as well as the columns of the surrounding court with their pedestals
Numbers3:38 East of the Dwelling
Numbers3:39 The total number of male Levites a month old or more whom Moses had registered by clans in keeping with the LORD'S command
Numbers3:40 The LORD then said to Moses
Numbers3:41 Then assign the Levites to me
Numbers3:42 So Moses took a census of all the first-born of the Israelites
Numbers3:43 When all the first-born males of a month or more were registered
Numbers3:44 The LORD said to Moses:
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites in place of all the first-born of the Israelites
Numbers3:46 As ransom for the two hundred and seventy-three first-born of the Israelites who outnumber the Levites
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels for each individual
Numbers3:48 Give this silver to Aaron and his sons as ransom for the extra number.
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the silver as ransom from those who were left when the rest had been redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 From the first-born of the Israelites he received in silver one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels according to the sanctuary standard.
Numbers3:51 He then gave this ransom silver to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:2 Among the Levites take a total of the Kohathites
Numbers4:3 between thirty and fifty years of age; these are to undertake obligatory tasks in the meeting tent.
Numbers4:4 The service of the Kohathites in the meeting tent concerns the most sacred objects.
Numbers4:5 In breaking camp
Numbers4:6 Over these they shall put a cover of tahash skin
Numbers4:7 On the table of the Presence they shall spread a violet cloth and put on it the plates and cups
Numbers4:8 Over these they shall spread a scarlet cloth and cover all this with tahash skin. They shall then put the poles in place.
Numbers4:9 They shall use a violet cloth to cover the lampstand with its lamps
Numbers4:10 The lampstand with all its utensils they shall then enclose in a covering of tahash skin
Numbers4:11 Over the golden altar they shall spread a violet cloth
Numbers4:12 Taking the utensils of the sanctuary service
Numbers4:13 After cleansing the altar of its ashes
Numbers4:14 On this they shall put all the utensils with which it is served: the fire pans
Numbers4:15 Only after Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sacred objects and all their utensils on breaking camp
Numbers4:16 Eleazar
Numbers4:17 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:18 Do not let the group of Kohathite clans perish from the body of the Levites.
Numbers4:19 That they may live and not die when they approach the most sacred objects
Numbers4:20 but the Kohathites shall not go in to look upon the sacred objects
Numbers4:21 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take a total among the Gershonites also
Numbers4:23 of all the men between thirty and fifty years of age; these are to undertake obligatory tasks in the meeting tent.
Numbers4:24 This is the task of the clans of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 they shall carry the sheets of the Dwelling
Numbers4:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers4:27 The service of the Gershonites shall be entirely under the direction of Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:28 This
Numbers4:29 Among the Merarites

Numbers5:1 The LORD said to Moses:
Numbers5:2 Order the Israelites to expel from camp every leper
Numbers5:3 Male and female alike
Numbers5:4 The Israelites obeyed the command that the LORD had given Moses; they expelled them from the camp.
Numbers5:5 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers5:6 Tell the Israelites: If a man (or a woman) commits a fault against his fellow man and wrongs him
Numbers5:7 he shall confess the wrong he has done
Numbers5:8 However
Numbers5:9 Likewise
Numbers5:10 Each Israelite man may dispose of his own sacred contributions; they become the property of the priest to whom he gives them.
Numbers5:11 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers5:12 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: If a man's wife goes astray and becomes unfaithful to him
Numbers5:13 by having intercourse with another man
Numbers5:14 or if a man is overcome by a feeling of jealousy that makes him suspect his wife
Numbers5:15 he shall bring his wife to the priest and shall take along as an offering for her a tenth of an ephah of barley meal. However
Numbers5:16 The priest shall first have the woman come forward and stand before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 In an earthen vessel he shall meanwhile put some holy water
Numbers5:18 Then
Numbers5:19 Then he shall adjure the woman
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under the authority of your husband and have acted impurely by letting a man other than your husband have intercourse with you'--
Numbers5:21 so shall the priest adjure the woman with this oath of imprecation-- 'may the LORD make you an example of malediction and imprecation among your people by causing your thighs to waste away and your belly to swell!
Numbers5:22 May this water
Numbers5:23 The priest shall put these imprecations in writing and shall then wash them off into the bitter water
Numbers5:24 which he is to have the woman drink
Numbers5:25 But first he shall take the cereal offering of jealousy from the woman's hand
Numbers5:26 where he shall take a handful of the cereal offering as its token offering and burn it on the altar. Only then shall he have the woman drink the water.
Numbers5:27 Once she has done so
Numbers5:28 If
Numbers5:29 This

Numbers5:31 The man shall be free from guilt
Numbers6:1 The LORD said to Moses:
Numbers6:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When a man (or a woman) solemnly takes the nazirite vow to dedicate himself to the LORD
Numbers6:3 he shall abstain from wine and strong drink; he may neither drink wine vinegar
Numbers6:4 As long as he is a nazirite he shall not eat anything of the produce of the vine; not even unripe grapes or grapeskins.
Numbers6:5 While he is under the nazirite vow
Numbers6:6 As long as he is dedicated to the LORD
Numbers6:7 Not even for his father or mother
Numbers6:8 As long as he is a nazirite he is sacred to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 If someone dies very suddenly in his presence
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the meeting tent.
Numbers6:11 The priest shall offer up the one as a sin offering and the other as a holocaust
Numbers6:12 and begin anew the period of his dedication to the LORD as a nazirite
Numbers6:13 This is the ritual for the nazirite: On the day he completes the period of his dedication he shall go to the entrance of the meeting tent
Numbers6:14 bringing as his offering to the LORD one unblemished yearling lamb for a holocaust
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened cakes of fine flour mixed with oil and of unleavened wafers spread with oil.
Numbers6:16 The priest shall present them before the LORD
Numbers6:17 He shall then offer up the ram as a peace offering to the LORD
Numbers6:18 Then at the entrance of the meeting tent the nazirite shall shave his dedicated head
Numbers6:19 After the nazirite has shaved off his dedicated hair
Numbers6:20 The priest shall then wave them as a wave offering before the LORD. They become sacred and shall belong to the priest

Numbers6:25 The LORD let his face shine upon you
Numbers6:26 The LORD look upon you kindly and give you peace!

Numbers11:1 Now the people complained in the hearing of the LORD; and when he heard it his wrath flared up so that the fire of the LORD burned among them and consumed the outskirts of the camp.

Numbers14:1 At this
Numbers14:2 All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron

Numbers15:1 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:2 Give the Israelites these instructions: When you have entered the land that I will give you for your homesteads
Numbers15:3 if you make to the LORD a sweet-smelling oblation from the herd or from the flock
Numbers15:4 whoever does so shall also present to the LORD a cereal offering consisting of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil
Numbers15:5 as well as a libation of a fourth of a hin of wine
Numbers15:6 With each sacrifice of a ram you shall present a cereal offering of two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of oil
Numbers15:7 and a libation of a third of a hin of wine
Numbers15:8 When you sacrifice an ox as a holocaust
Numbers15:9 with it you shall present a cereal offering of three tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of oil
Numbers15:10 and a libation of half a hin of wine
Numbers15:11 The same is to be done for each ox
Numbers15:12 Whatever the number you offer
Numbers15:13 All the native-born shall make these offerings in the same way
Numbers15:14 Likewise
Numbers15:15 There is but one rule for you and for the resident alien
Numbers15:16 with the same law and the same application of it for the alien residing among you as for yourselves.
Numbers15:17 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:18 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land into which I will bring you
Numbers15:19 and begin to eat of the food of that land
Numbers15:20 consisting of a cake of your first batch of dough. You shall offer it just as you offer a contribution from the threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 Throughout your generations you shall give a contribution to the LORD from your first batch of dough.
Numbers15:22 When through inadvertence you fail to carry out any of these commandments which the LORD gives to Moses
Numbers15:23 and through Moses to you
Numbers15:24 if the community itself unwittingly becomes guilty of the fault of inadvertence
Numbers15:25 Then the priest shall make atonement for the whole Israelite community; thus they will be forgiven the inadvertence for which they have brought their holocaust as an oblation to the LORD.
Numbers15:26 Not only the whole Israelite community
Numbers15:27 However
Numbers15:28 and the priest shall make atonement before the LORD for him who sinned inadvertently; when atonement has been made for him
Numbers15:29 You shall have but one law for him who sins inadvertently
Numbers15:30 But anyone who sins defiantly
Numbers15:31 Since he has despised the word of the LORD and has broken his commandment
Numbers15:32 While the Israelites were in the desert
Numbers15:33 Those who caught him at it brought him to Moses and Aaron and the whole assembly.
Numbers15:34 But they kept him in custody
Numbers15:35 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:36 So the whole community led him outside the camp and stoned him to death
Numbers15:37 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers15:38 Speak to the Israelites and tell them that they and their descendants must put tassels on the corners of their garments
Numbers15:39 When you use these tassels
Numbers15:40 Thus you will remember to keep all my commandments and be holy to your God.

Numbers18:1 The LORD said to Aaron

Numbers18:5 You shall have charge of the sanctuary and of the altar
Numbers18:6 Remember

Numbers18:8 The LORD said to Aaron
Numbers18:9 You shall have the right to share in the oblations that are most sacred

Numbers18:11 You shall also have what is removed from the gift in every wave offering of the Israelites; by perpetual ordinance I have assigned it to you and to your sons and daughters. All in your family who are clean may partake of it.

Numbers19:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers19:2 This is the regulation which the law of the LORD prescribes. Tell the Israelites to procure for you a red heifer that is free from every blemish and defect and on which no yoke has ever been laid.
Numbers19:3 This is to be given to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Eleazar the priest shall take some of its blood on his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the meeting tent.
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer shall be burned in his sight
Numbers19:6 and the priest shall take some cedar wood
Numbers19:7 The priest shall then wash his garments and bathe his body in water. He remains unclean until the evening
Numbers19:8 Likewise
Numbers19:9 Finally
Numbers19:10 He who has gathered up the ashes of the heifer shall also wash his garments and be unclean until evening. This is a perpetual ordinance
Numbers19:11 Whoever touches the dead body of any human being shall be unclean for seven days;
Numbers19:12 he shall purify himself with the water on the third and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Everyone who fails to purify himself after touching the body of any deceased person
Numbers19:14 This is the law: When a man dies in a tent
Numbers19:15 likewise
Numbers19:16 Moreover
Numbers19:17 For anyone who is thus unclean
Numbers19:18 Then a man who is clean shall take some hyssop
Numbers19:19 The clean man shall sprinkle the unclean on the third and on the seventh day; thus purified on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 Any unclean man who fails to have himself purified shall be cut off from the community
Numbers19:21 This shall be a perpetual ordinance for you. One who sprinkles the lustral water shall wash his garments

Numbers30:7 If she marries while under a vow or under a rash pledge to which she bound herself
Numbers30:8 and her husband learns of it
Numbers30:9 But if on the day he learns of it her husband expresses to her his disapproval
Numbers30:10 The vow of a widow or of a divorced woman
Numbers30:11 If it is in her husband's house that she makes a vow or binds herself under oath to a pledge
Numbers30:12 and her husband learns of it yet says nothing to express to her his disapproval
Numbers30:13 But if on the day he learns of them her husband annuls them
Numbers30:14 Any vow or any pledge that she makes under oath to mortify herself
Numbers30:15 But if her husband
Numbers30:16 If

Numbers35:1 The LORD gave these instructions to Moses on the plains of Moab beside the Jericho stretch of the Jordan:
Numbers35:2 Tell the Israelites that out of their hereditary property they shall give the Levites cities for homes
Numbers35:3 The cities shall serve them to dwell in
Numbers35:4 The pasture lands of the cities to be assigned the Levites shall extend a thousand cubits from the city walls in each direction.
Numbers35:5 Thus you shall measure out two thousand cubits outside the city along each side-- east
Numbers35:6 Now these are the cities you shall give to the Levites: the six cities of asylum which you must establish as places where a homicide can take refuge
Numbers35:7 a total of forty-eight cities with their pasture lands to be assigned the Levites.
Numbers35:8 In assigning the cities from the property of the Israelites
Numbers35:9 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers35:10 Tell the Israelites: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan
Numbers35:11 select for yourselves cities to serve as cities of asylum
Numbers35:12 These cities shall serve you as places of asylum from the avenger of blood
Numbers35:13 Six cities of asylum shall you assign:
Numbers35:14 three beyond the Jordan
Numbers35:15 These six cities of asylum shall serve not only the Israelites but all the resident or transient aliens among them
Numbers35:16 If a man strikes another with an iron instrument and causes his death
Numbers35:17 If a man strikes another with a death-dealing stone in his hand and causes his death
Numbers35:18 If a man strikes another with a death-dealing club in his hand and causes his death
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood may execute the murderer
Numbers35:20 If a man pushes another out of hatred
Numbers35:21 or if he strikes another out of enmity and causes his death
Numbers35:22 However
Numbers35:23 or without seeing him throws a death-dealing stone which strikes him and causes his death
Numbers35:24 then the community
Numbers35:25 shall free the homicide from the avenger of blood and shall remand him to the city of asylum where he took refuge; and he shall stay there until the death of the high priest who has been anointed with sacred oil.
Numbers35:26 If the homicide of his own accord leaves the bounds of the city of asylum where he has taken refuge
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him beyond these bounds and kills him
Numbers35:28 the homicide was bound to stay in his city of asylum until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the homicide return to his own district.
Numbers35:29 These shall be norms for you and all your descendants
Numbers35:30 Whenever someone kills another
Numbers35:31 You shall not accept indemnity in place of the life of a murderer who deserves the death penalty; he must be put to death.
Numbers35:32 Nor shall you accept indemnity to allow a refugee to leave his city of asylum and again dwell elsewhere in the land before the death of the high priest.
Numbers35:33 You shall not desecrate the land where you live. Since bloodshed desecrates the land
Numbers35:34 Do not defile the land in which you live and in the midst of which I dwell; for I am the LORD who dwells in the midst of the Israelites.

Joshua6:9 In front of the priests with the horns marched the picked troops; the rear guard followed the ark
Joshua6:10 But the people had been commanded by Joshua not to shout or make any noise or outcry until he gave the word: only then were they to shout.

Joshua6:17 and everything in it. It is under the LORD'S ban. Only the harlot Rahab and all who are in the house with her are to be spared

2Samuel7:10 I will fix a place for my people Israel; I will plant them so that they may dwell in their place without further disturbance. Neither shall the wicked continue to afflict them as they did of old
2Samuel7:11 since the time I first appointed judges over my people Israel. I will give you rest from all your enemies. The LORD also reveals to you that he will establish a house for you.
2Samuel7:12 And when your time comes and you rest with your ancestors
2Samuel7:13 It is he who shall build a house for my name. And I will make his royal throne firm forever.
2Samuel7:14 I will be a father to him
2Samuel7:15 but I will not withdraw my favor from him as I withdrew it from your predecessor Saul
2Samuel7:16 Your house and your kingdom shall endure forever before me; your throne shall stand firm forever.'

2Samuel22:1 David sang the words of this song to the LORD when the LORD had rescued him from the grasp of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 This is what he sang: I O LORD
2Samuel22:3 my God
2Samuel22:4 'Praised be the LORD
2Samuel22:5 The breakers of death surged round about me
2Samuel22:6 The cords of the nether world enmeshed me
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the LORD and cried out to my God; From his temple he heard my voice
2Samuel22:8 The earth swayed and quaked; the foundations of the heavens trembled and shook when his wrath flared up.
2Samuel22:9 Smoke rose from his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He inclined the heavens and came down
2Samuel22:11 He mounted a cherub and flew
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness the shelter about him
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness of his presence coals were kindled to flame.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High gave forth his voice.
2Samuel22:15 He sent forth arrows to put them to flight; he flashed lightning and routed them.
2Samuel22:16 Then the wellsprings of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He reached out from on high and grasped me; he drew me out of the deep waters.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my mighty enemy
2Samuel22:19 They attacked me on my day of calamity
2Samuel22:20 He set me free in the open
2Samuel22:21 The LORD rewarded me according to my justice; according to the cleanness of my hands he requited me.
2Samuel22:22 For I kept the ways of the LORD and was not disloyal to my God.
2Samuel22:23 For his ordinances were all present to me
2Samuel22:24 But I was wholehearted toward him
2Samuel22:25 And the LORD requited me according to my justice
2Samuel22:26 Toward the faithful you are faithful; toward the wholehearted you are wholehearted;
2Samuel22:27 Toward the sincere you are sincere; but toward the crooked you are astute.
2Samuel22:28 You save lowly people
2Samuel22:29 You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For with your aid I run against an armed band
2Samuel22:31 God's way is unerring; the promise of the LORD is fire-tried; he is a shield to all who take refuge in him. I
2Samuel22:32 For who is God except the LORD? Who is a rock save our God?
2Samuel22:33 The God who girded me with strength and kept my way unerring;
2Samuel22:34 Who made my feet swift as those of hinds and set me on the heights;
2Samuel22:35 Who trained my hands for war till my arms could bend a bow of brass. II
2Samuel22:36 You have given me your saving shield
2Samuel22:37 You made room for my steps; unwavering was my stride.
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 I smote them and they did not rise; they fell beneath my feet. III
2Samuel22:40 You girded me with strength for war; you subdued my adversaries beneath me.
2Samuel22:41 My enemies you put to flight before me and those who hated me I destroyed.
2Samuel22:42 They cried for help-- but no one saved them; to the LORD-- but he answered them not.
2Samuel22:43 I ground them fine as the dust of the earth; like the mud in the streets I trampled them down. IV
2Samuel22:44 You rescued me from the strife of my people; you made me head over nations. A people I had not known became my slaves;
2Samuel22:45 as soon as they heard me
2Samuel22:46 The foreigners fawned and cringed before me; they staggered forth from their fortresses.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD live! And blessed be my Rock! Extolled be my God
2Samuel22:48 O God
2Samuel22:49 and helped me escape from my enemies
2Samuel22:50 Therefore will I proclaim you
2Samuel22:51 You who gave great victories to your king and showed kindness to your anointed

1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year from the departure of the Israelites from the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The temple which King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits long

1Kings6:4 Splayed windows with trellises were made for the temple

1Kings6:20 twenty cubits long

1Kings6:22 The entire temple was overlaid with gold so that it was completely covered with it; the whole altar before the sanctuary was also overlaid with gold.
1Kings6:23 In the sanctuary were two cherubim
1Kings6:24 Each wing of a cherub measured five cubits so that the space from wing tip to wing tip of each was ten cubits.
1Kings6:25 The cherubim were identical in size and shape
1Kings6:26 and each was exactly ten cubits high.
1Kings6:27 The cherubim were placed in the inmost part of the temple
1Kings6:28 The cherubim
