1Kings8:29 May your eyes watch night and day over this temple
1Kings8:30 Listen to the petitions of your servant and of your people Israel which they offer in this place. Listen from your heavenly dwelling and grant pardon.
1Kings8:31 If a man sins against his neighbor and is required to take an oath sanctioned by a curse
1Kings8:32 listen in heaven; take action and pass judgment on your servants. Condemn the wicked and punish him for his conduct
1Kings8:33 If your people Israel sin against you and are defeated by an enemy
1Kings8:34 listen in heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel
1Kings8:35 If the sky is closed
1Kings8:36 listen in heaven and forgive the sin of your servant and of your people Israel
1Kings8:37 If there is famine in the land or pestilence; or if blight comes
1Kings8:38 if then any one (of your entire people Israel) has remorse of conscience and offers some prayer or petition
1Kings8:39 listen from your heavenly dwelling place and forgive. You who alone know the hearts of all men
1Kings8:40 that they may fear you as long as they live on the land you gave our fathers.
1Kings8:41 To the foreigner
1Kings8:42 (since men will learn of your great name and your mighty hand and your outstretched arm)
1Kings8:43 listen from your heavenly dwelling. Do all that the foreigner asks of you
1Kings8:44 Whatever the direction in which you may send your people forth to war against their enemies
1Kings8:45 listen in heaven to their prayer and petition
1Kings8:46 When they sin against you (for there is no man who does not sin)
1Kings8:47 may they repent in the land of their captivity and be converted. If then they entreat you in the land of their captors and say
1Kings8:48 if with their whole heart and soul they turn back to you in the land of the enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 listen from your heavenly dwelling.
1Kings8:50 Forgive your people their sins and all the offenses they have committed against you
1Kings8:51 For they are your people and your inheritance
1Kings8:52 Thus may your eyes be open to the petition of your servant and to the petition of your people Israel. Hear them whenever they call upon you
1Kings8:53 because you have set them apart among all the peoples of the earth for your inheritance

1Kings8:61 You must be wholly devoted to the LORD

2Chronicles3:5 The nave he overlaid with cypress wood which he covered with fine gold

2Chronicles3:7 The house
2Chronicles3:8 He also made the room of the holy of holies. Its length corresponded to the width of the house

2Chronicles3:10 For the room of the holy of holies he made two cherubim of carved workmanship
2Chronicles3:11 The wings of the cherubim spanned twenty cubits:
2Chronicles3:12 one wing of each cherub
2Chronicles3:13 The combined wingspread of the two cherubim was thus twenty cubits. They stood upon their own feet
2Chronicles3:14 He made the veil of violet
2Chronicles3:15 In front of the building he set two columns thirty-five cubits high; the capital topping each was of five cubits.
2Chronicles3:16 He worked out chains in the form of a collar with which he encircled the capitals of the columns
2Chronicles3:17 He set up the columns to correspond with the nave

2Chronicles23:3 the whole assembly made a covenant with the king in the house of God. Jehoiada said to them: Here is the king's son who must reign
2Chronicles23:4 This is what you must do: a third of your number
2Chronicles23:5 another third must be at the king's palace
2Chronicles23:6 Let no one enter the LORD'S house except the priests and those Levites who are ministering. They may enter because they are holy; but all the other people must observe the prescriptions of the LORD.
2Chronicles23:7 The Levites shall surround the king on all sides
2Chronicles23:8 The Levites and all Judah did just as Jehoiada the priest commanded. Each brought his men
2Chronicles23:9 Jehoiada the priest gave the captains the spears
2Chronicles23:10 He stationed all the people
2Chronicles23:11 Then they brought out the king's son
2Chronicles23:12 When Athaliah heard the din of the people running and acclaiming the king
2Chronicles23:13 She looked
2Chronicles23:14 Then Jehoiada the priest sent out the captains who were in command of the army; he said to them: Take her outside through the ranks
2Chronicles23:15 So they seized her
2Chronicles23:16 Then Jehoiada made a covenant between himself and all the people and the king
2Chronicles23:17 And all the people went to the temple of Baal and tore it down. They smashed its altars and images
2Chronicles23:18 Then Jehoiada gave the charge of the LORD'S temple into the hands of the levitical priests
2Chronicles23:19 Moreover
2Chronicles23:20 Then he took the captains
2Chronicles23:21 All the people of the land rejoiced and the city was quiet
2Chronicles24:1 Joash was seven years old when he became king
2Chronicles24:2 Joash did what was pleasing to the LORD as long as Jehoiada the priest lived.
2Chronicles24:3 Jehoiada provided him with two wives
2Chronicles24:4 After some time
2Chronicles24:5 He called together the priests and Levites and said to them: Go out to all the cities of Judah and collect money from all Israel that you may repair the house of your God over the years. You must hasten this affair. But the Levites did not hasten.
2Chronicles24:6 Then the king summoned Jehoiada
2Chronicles24:7 For the wicked Athaliah and her sons had damaged the house of God and had even turned over to the Baals the dedicated resources of the LORD'S temple.
2Chronicles24:8 At the king's command
2Chronicles24:9 They had it proclaimed throughout Judah and Jerusalem that the tax which Moses
2Chronicles24:10 All the princes and the people rejoiced; they brought what was asked and cast it into the chest until it was filled.
2Chronicles24:11 Whenever the chest was brought to the royal officials by the Levites and they saw that it contained much money
2Chronicles24:12 Then the king and Jehoiada gave it to the workmen in charge of the labor on the LORD'S temple
2Chronicles24:13 The workmen labored
2Chronicles24:14 After they had finished
2Chronicles24:15 Jehoiada lived to a ripe old age; he was a hundred and thirty years old when he died.
2Chronicles24:16 He was buried in the City of David with the kings
2Chronicles24:17 After the death of Jehoiada
2Chronicles24:18 They forsook the temple of the LORD
2Chronicles24:19 Although prophets were sent to them to convert them to the LORD
2Chronicles24:20 Then the spirit of God possessed Zechariah
2Chronicles24:21 But they conspired against him
2Chronicles24:22 Thus King Joash was unmindful of the devotion shown him by Jehoiada
2Chronicles24:23 At the turn of the year a force of Arameans came up against Joash. They invaded Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles24:24 Though the Aramean force came with few men
2Chronicles24:25 After the Arameans had departed from him
2Chronicles24:26 These conspired against him: Zabad
2Chronicles24:27 Of his sons
2Chronicles25:1 Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Chronicles25:2 He did what was pleasing in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles25:3 After he had strengthened his hold on the kingdom
2Chronicles25:4 but he did not put their children to death
2Chronicles25:5 Amaziah mustered Judah and placed them
2Chronicles25:6 He also hired a hundred thousand valiant warriors from Israel for a hundred talents of silver.
2Chronicles25:7 But a man of God came to him and said: O king
2Chronicles25:8 Instead
2Chronicles25:9 Amaziah answered the man of God
2Chronicles25:10 Amaziah then disbanded the troops that had come to him from Ephraim
2Chronicles25:11 Amaziah now assumed command of his army. They proceeded to the Valley of Salt
2Chronicles25:12 The Judahites also brought back another ten thousand alive
2Chronicles25:13 Meanwhile
2Chronicles25:14 When Amaziah returned from his conquest of the Edomites he brought back with him the gods of the people of Seir
2Chronicles25:15 Then the anger of the LORD blazed out against Amaziah
2Chronicles25:16 While he was still speaking
2Chronicles25:17 Having taken counsel

2Chronicles25:19 You are thinking
2Chronicles25:20 But Amaziah would not listen
2Chronicles25:21 Therefore King Joash of Israel advanced and he and King Amaziah met in battle at Beth-shemesh of Judah.
2Chronicles25:22 There Judah was defeated by Israel
2Chronicles25:23 King Joash of Israel captured Amaziah
2Chronicles25:24 He took away all the gold and silver and all the vessels he found in the house of God with Obed-edom
2Chronicles25:25 Amaziah

2Chronicles26:1 All the people of Judah chose Uzziah
2Chronicles26:2 He rebuilt Elath and restored it to Judah; this was after King Amaziah had gone to rest with his ancestors.
2Chronicles26:3 Uzziah was sixteen years old when he became king
2Chronicles26:4 He pleased the LORD
2Chronicles26:5 He was prepared to seek God as long as Zechariah lived
2Chronicles26:6 He went out and fought the Philistines and razed the walls of Gath
2Chronicles26:7 God helped him against the Philistines
2Chronicles26:8 The Ammonites paid tribute to Uzziah and his fame spread as far as Egypt
2Chronicles26:9 Moreover
2Chronicles26:10 He built towers in the desert and dug numerous cisterns
2Chronicles26:11 Uzziah also had a standing army of fit soldiers divided into bands according to the number in which they were mustered by Jeiel the scribe and Maaseiah the recorder
2Chronicles26:12 The entire number of family heads over these valiant warriors was two thousand six hundred
2Chronicles26:13 and at their disposal was a mighty army of three hundred seven thousand five hundred fighting men of great valor to help the king against his enemies.
2Chronicles26:14 Uzziah provided for them-- for the entire army-- bucklers
2Chronicles26:15 He also built machines in Jerusalem
2Chronicles26:16 But after he had become strong
2Chronicles26:17 But Azariah the priest
2Chronicles26:18 They opposed King Uzziah
2Chronicles26:19 Uzziah
2Chronicles26:20 Azariah the chief priest and all the other priests examined him
2Chronicles26:21 King Uzziah remained a leper to the day of his death. As a leper he dwelt in a segregated house
2Chronicles26:22 The prophet Isaiah
2Chronicles26:23 Uzziah rested with his ancestors; he was buried with them in the field adjoining the royal cemetery

2Chronicles32:1 But after he had proved his fidelity by such deeds
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib was coming with the intention of attacking Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he decided in counsel with his princes and warriors to stop the waters of the springs outside the city. When they had pledged him their support
2Chronicles32:4 a large crowd was gathered which stopped all the springs and also the running stream in the valley nearby. For they said
2Chronicles32:5 He then looked to his defenses: he rebuilt the wall where it was broken down
2Chronicles32:6 Then he appointed army commanders over the people. He gathered them together in his presence in the open space at the gate of the city and encouraged them with these words:
2Chronicles32:7 Be brave and steadfast; do not be afraid or dismayed because of the king of Assyria and all the throng that is coming with him
2Chronicles32:8 For he has only an arm of flesh
2Chronicles32:9 After this
2Chronicles32:10 King Sennacherib of Assyria has this to say: On what are you relying
2Chronicles32:11 Has not Hezekiah deceived you
2Chronicles32:12 Has not this same Hezekiah removed his high places and altars and commanded Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what my fathers and I have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations in those lands able to save their lands from my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who among all the gods of those nations which my fathers put under the ban was able to save his people from my hand? Will your god
2Chronicles32:15 Let not Hezekiah mislead you further and deceive you in any such way. Do not believe him! Since no other god of any other nation or kingdom has been able to save his people from my hand or the hands of my fathers
2Chronicles32:16 His officials said still more against the LORD God and against his servant Hezekiah
2Chronicles32:17 for he had written letters to deride the LORD
2Chronicles32:18 In a loud voice they shouted in the Judean language to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 They spoke of the God of Israel as though he were one of the gods of the other peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 But because of this
2Chronicles32:21 Then the LORD sent an angel
2Chronicles32:22 Thus the LORD saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib
2Chronicles32:23 Many brought gifts for the LORD to Jerusalem and costly objects for King Hezekiah of Judah
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became mortally ill. He prayed to the LORD
2Chronicles32:25 Hezekiah
2Chronicles32:26 But then Hezekiah humbled himself for his pride-- both he and the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and therefore the LORD did not vent his anger on them during the time of Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:27 Hezekiah possessed very great wealth and glory. He had treasuries made for his silver
2Chronicles32:28 also storehouses for the harvest of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He built cities for himself
2Chronicles32:30 This same Hezekiah stopped the upper outflow of water from Gihon and led it underground westward to the City of David. Hezekiah prospered in all his undertakings.

2Chronicles33:2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles33:3 He rebuilt the high places which his father Hezekiah had torn down
2Chronicles33:4 He even built altars in the temple of the LORD
2Chronicles33:5 he built altars to the whole host of heaven in the two courts of the LORD'S house.
2Chronicles33:6 It was he
2Chronicles33:7 He placed an idol that he had carved in the house of God

2Chronicles33:11 Therefore the LORD brought against them the army commanders of the Assyrian king; they took Manasseh with hooks
2Chronicles33:12 In this distress
2Chronicles33:13 and prayed to him. The LORD let himself be won over: he heard his prayer and restored him to his kingdom in Jerusalem. Then Manasseh understood that the LORD is indeed God.
2Chronicles33:14 Afterward he built an outer wall for the City of David to the west of Gihon in the valley

2Chronicles33:23 Moreover
2Chronicles33:24 His servants conspired against him and put him to death in his own house.
2Chronicles33:25 But the people of the land slew all those who had conspired against King Amon

2Chronicles35:3 He said to the Levites who were to instruct all Israel
2Chronicles35:4 Prepare yourselves in your ancestral houses and your classes according to the prescriptions of King David of Israel and his son Solomon.
2Chronicles35:5 Stand in the sanctuary according to the divisions of the ancestral houses of your brethren
2Chronicles35:6 Slay the Passover sacrifice
2Chronicles35:7 Josiah contributed to the common people a flock of lambs and kids
2Chronicles35:8 His princes also gave a free-will gift to the people
2Chronicles35:9 Conaniah and his brothers Shemaiah
2Chronicles35:10 When the service had been arranged
2Chronicles35:11 The Passover sacrifice was slaughtered
2Chronicles35:12 They separated what was destined for the holocaust and gave it to various groups of the ancestral houses of the common people to offer to the LORD
2Chronicles35:13 They cooked the Passover on the fire as prescribed
2Chronicles35:14 Afterward they prepared the Passover for themselves and for the priests. Indeed the priests
2Chronicles35:15 The singers
2Chronicles35:16 Thus the entire service of the LORD was arranged that day so that the Passover could be celebrated and the holocausts offered on the altar of the LORD
2Chronicles35:17 The Israelites who were present on that occasion kept the Passover and the feast of the Unleavened Bread for seven days.
2Chronicles35:18 No such Passover had been observed in Israel since the time of the prophet Samuel
2Chronicles35:19 It was in the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign that this Passover was observed.

Nehemiah2:1 In the month Nisan of the twentieth year of King Artaxerxes
Nehemiah2:2 the king asked me
Nehemiah2:3 I answered the king: May the king live forever! How could I not look sad when the city where my ancestors are buried lies in ruins
Nehemiah2:4 The king asked me
Nehemiah2:5 and then answered the king: If it please the king
Nehemiah2:6 Then the king
Nehemiah2:7 I asked the king further: If it please the king
Nehemiah2:8 also a letter for Asaph
Nehemiah2:9 Thus I proceeded to the governors of West-of-Euphrates and presented the king's letters to them. The king also sent with me army officers and cavalry.
Nehemiah2:10 When Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite slave had heard of this
Nehemiah2:11 When I had arrived in Jerusalem
Nehemiah2:12 Then I set out by night with only a few other men (for I had not told anyone what my God had inspired me to do for Jerusalem) and with no other animals but my own mount.
Nehemiah2:13 I rode out at night by the Valley Gate
Nehemiah2:14 Then I passed over to the Spring Gate and to the King's Pool. Since there was no room here for my mount to pass with me astride
Nehemiah2:15 I continued on foot up the wadi by night
Nehemiah2:16 The magistrates knew nothing of where I had gone or what I was doing
Nehemiah2:17 Afterward I said to them: You see the evil plight in which we stand: how Jerusalem lies in ruins and its gates have been gutted by fire. Come
Nehemiah2:18 Then I explained to them how the favoring hand of my God had rested upon me
Nehemiah2:19 On hearing of this
Nehemiah2:20 My answer to them was this: It is the God of heaven who will grant us success. We

Nehemiah5:1 Then there rose a great outcry of the common people and their wives against certain of their fellow Jews.
Nehemiah5:2 Some said: We are forced to pawn our sons and daughters in order to get grain to eat that we may live.
Nehemiah5:3 Others said: We are forced to pawn our fields
Nehemiah5:4 Still others said: To pay the king's tax we have borrowed money on our fields and our vineyards.
Nehemiah5:5 And though these are our own kinsmen and our children are as good as theirs
Nehemiah5:6 I was extremely angry when I heard the reasons they had for complaint.
Nehemiah5:7 After some deliberation
Nehemiah5:8 saying to them: As far as we were able
Nehemiah5:9 I continued: What you are doing is not good. Should you not walk in the fear of our God
Nehemiah5:10 I myself
Nehemiah5:11 I ask that you return to them this very day their fields
Nehemiah5:12 They answered: We will return everything and exact nothing further from them. We will do just what you ask. Then I called for the priests and had them administer an oath to these men that they would do as they had promised.
Nehemiah5:13 I also shook out the folds of my garment
Nehemiah5:14 Moreover
Nehemiah5:15 The earlier governors
Nehemiah5:16 Moreover
Nehemiah5:17 Though I set my table for a hundred and fifty persons
Nehemiah5:18 and though the daily preparations were made at my expense-- one beef
Nehemiah5:19 Keep in mind
Nehemiah6:1 When it had been reported to Sanballat
Nehemiah6:2 Sanballat and Geshem sent me this message: Come
Nehemiah6:3 However
Nehemiah6:4 Four times they sent me this same proposal
Nehemiah6:5 Then
Nehemiah6:6 containing this text: Among the nations it has been reported-- Geshem is witness to this-- that you and the Jews are planning a rebellion; that for this reason you are rebuilding the wall; and that you are to be their king-- and so on.
Nehemiah6:7 Also
Nehemiah6:8 I sent him this answer: Nothing of what you report has taken place; rather
Nehemiah6:9 They were all trying to frighten us
Nehemiah6:10 I went to the house of Shemaiah
Nehemiah6:11 My answer was: A man like me take flight? Can a man like me enter the temple to save his life? I will not go!
Nehemiah6:12 For on consideration it was plain to me that God had not sent him; rather
Nehemiah6:13 that I might act on it out of fear and commit this sin. Then they would have had a shameful story with which to discredit me.
Nehemiah6:14 Keep in mind Tobiah and Sanballat
Nehemiah6:15 The wall was finished on the twenty-fifth day of Elul; it had taken fifty-two days.
Nehemiah6:16 When all our enemies had heard of this
Nehemiah6:17 At that same time
Nehemiah6:18 for many in Judah were in league with him
Nehemiah6:19 Thus they would praise his good deeds in my presence and relate to him whatever I said; and Tobiah sent letters trying to frighten me.

Nehemiah9:1 On the twenty-fourth day of this month
Nehemiah9:2 Those of Israelite descent separated themselves from all who were of foreign extraction

Nehemiah9:4 Standing on the platform of the Levites were Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 The Levites Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 Then Ezra said: It is you
Nehemiah9:7 You
Nehemiah9:8 When you had found his heart faithful in your sight
Nehemiah9:9 You saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You worked signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 The sea you divided before them
Nehemiah9:12 With a column of cloud you led them by day
Nehemiah9:13 On Mount Sinai you came down
Nehemiah9:14 Your holy sabbath you made known to them
Nehemiah9:15 Food from heaven you gave them in their hunger
Nehemiah9:16 But they
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey and no longer remembered the miracles you had worked for them. They stiffened their necks and turned their heads to return to their slavery in Egypt. But you are a God of pardons
Nehemiah9:18 Though they made for themselves a molten calf
Nehemiah9:19 yet in your great mercy you did not forsake them in the desert. The column of cloud did not cease to lead them by day on their journey
Nehemiah9:20 Your good spirit you bestowed on them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years in the desert you sustained them: they did not want; their garments did not become worn
Nehemiah9:22 You gave them kingdoms and peoples
Nehemiah9:23 You made their children as numerous as the stars of the heavens
Nehemiah9:24 The sons went in to take possession of the land
Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land; they took possession of houses filled with all good things
Nehemiah9:26 But they were contemptuous and rebellious: they cast your law behind their backs
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore you delivered them into the power of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 As soon as they had relief
Nehemiah9:29 You bore witness against them
Nehemiah9:30 You were patient with them for many years
Nehemiah9:31 Yet in your great mercy you did not completely destroy them and you did not forsake them
Nehemiah9:32 Now
Nehemiah9:33 In all that has come upon us you have been just
Nehemiah9:34 Yes
Nehemiah9:35 While they were yet in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 But
Nehemiah9:37 Its rich produce goes to the kings whom you set over us because of our sins

Esther1:1 (A:1) In the second year of the reign of the great King Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 while he was occupying the royal throne in the stronghold of Susa
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 For as many as a hundred and eighty days
Esther1:5 At the end of this time the king gave a feast of seven days in the garden court of the royal palace for all the people
Esther1:6 There were white cotton draperies and violet hangings
Esther1:7 Liquor was served in a variety of golden cups
Esther1:8 By ordinance of the king the drinking was unstinted
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a feast for the women inside the royal palace of King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti into his presence wearing the royal crown
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the royal order issued through the eunuchs. At this the king's wrath flared up
Esther1:13 He conferred with the wise men versed in the law
Esther1:14 He summoned Carshena
Esther1:15 and asked them
Esther1:16 In the presence of the king and of the officials
Esther1:17 For the queen's conduct will become known to all the women
Esther1:18 This very day the Persian and Median ladies who hear of the queen's conduct will rebel against all the royal officials
Esther1:19 If it please the king
Esther1:20 Thus
Esther1:21 This proposal found acceptance with the king and the officials
Esther1:22 He sent letters to all the royal provinces
Esther2:1 After this
Esther2:2 Then the king's personal attendants suggested: Let beautiful young virgins be sought for the king.
Esther2:3 Let the king appoint commissaries in all the provinces of his realm to bring together all beautiful young virgins to the harem in the stronghold of Susa. Under the care of the royal eunuch Hegai
Esther2:4 Then the girl who pleases the king shall reign in place of Vashti. This suggestion pleased the king
Esther2:5 There was in the stronghold of Susa a certain Jew named Mordecai
Esther2:6 who had been exiled from Jerusalem with the captives taken with Jeconiah
Esther2:7 He was foster father to Hadassah
Esther2:8 When the king's order and decree had been obeyed and many maidens brought together to the stronghold of Susa under the care of Hegai
Esther2:9 The girl pleased him and won his favor. So he promptly furnished her with cosmetics and provisions. Then picking out seven maids for her from the royal palace
Esther2:10 Esther did not reveal her nationality or family
Esther2:11 Day by day Mordecai would walk about in front of the court of the harem
Esther2:12 Each girl went in turn to visit King Ahasuerus after the twelve months' preparation decreed for the women. Of this period of beautifying treatment
Esther2:13 Then
Esther2:14 She would go in the evening and return in the morning to a second harem under the care of the royal eunuch Shaashgaz
Esther2:15 As for Esther
Esther2:16 Esther was led to King Ahasuerus in his palace in the tenth month
Esther2:17 The king loved Esther more than all other women
Esther2:18 Then the king gave a great feast in honor of Esther to all his officials and ministers
Esther2:19 To resume: From the time the virgins had been brought together
Esther2:20 Esther had not revealed her family or nationality
Esther2:21 And during the time that Mordecai spent at the king's gate
Esther2:22 When the plot became known to Mordecai
Esther2:23 The matter was investigated and verified

Esther9:18 (The Jews in Susa
Esther9:19 That is why the rural Jews
Esther9:20 Mordecai recorded these events and sent letters to all the Jews
Esther9:21 He ordered them to celebrate every year both the fourteenth and the fifteenth of the month of Adar
Esther9:22 as the days on which the Jews obtained rest from their enemies and as the month which was turned for them from sorrow into joy
Esther9:23 The Jews took upon themselves for the future this observance which they instituted at the written direction of Mordecai.
Esther9:24 Haman
Esther9:25 Yet
Esther9:26 And so these days have been named Purim after the word pur. Thus
Esther9:27 the Jews established and took upon themselves
Esther9:28 These days were to be commemorated and kept in every generation

Esther9:31 Thus were established
Esther9:32 The command of Esther confirmed these prescriptions for Purim and was recorded in the book.

Job1:1 In the land of Uz there was a blameless and upright man named Job
Job1:2 Seven sons and three daughters were born to him;
Job1:3 and he had seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 His sons used to take turns giving feasts
Job1:5 And when each feast had run its course
Job1:6 One day
Job1:7 And the LORD said to Satan
Job1:8 And the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 But Satan answered the LORD and said
Job1:10 Have you not surrounded him and his family and all that he has with your protection? You have blessed the work of his hands
Job1:11 But now put forth your hand and touch anything that he has
Job1:12 And the LORD said to Satan
Job1:13 And so one day
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and said
Job1:15 and the Sabeans carried them off in a raid. They put the herdsmen to the sword
Job1:16 While he was yet speaking
Job1:17 While he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was yet speaking
Job1:19 when suddenly a great wind came across the desert and smote the four corners of the house. It fell upon the young people and they are dead; and I alone have escaped to tell you.
Job1:20 Then Job began to tear his cloak and cut off his hair. He cast himself prostrate upon the ground
Job1:21 and said
Job1:22 In all this Job did not sin
Job2:1 Once again the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:3 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 And Satan answered the LORD and said
Job2:5 But now put forth your hand and touch his bone and his flesh
Job2:6 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went forth from the presence of the LORD and smote Job with severe boils from the soles of his feet to the crown of his head.
Job2:8 And he took a potsherd to scrape himself
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Now when three of Job's friends heard of all the misfortune that had come upon him
Job2:12 But when
Job2:13 Then they sat down upon the ground with him seven days and seven nights
Job3:1 After this
Job3:2 Job spoke out and said:
Job3:3 Perish the day on which I was born
Job3:4 May that day be darkness: let not God above call for it
Job3:5 May darkness and gloom claim it
Job3:6 May obscurity seize that day; let it not occur among the days of the year
Job3:7 May that night be barren; let no joyful outcry greet it!
Job3:8 Let them curse it who curse the sea
Job3:9 May the stars of its twilight be darkened; may it look for daylight
Job3:10 Because it kept not shut the doors of the womb to shield my eyes from trouble!
Job3:11 Why did I not perish at birth
Job3:12 Wherefore did the knees receive me? or why did I suck at the breasts?
Job3:13 For then I should have lain down and been tranquil; had I slept
Job3:14 With kings and counselors of the earth who built where now there are ruins
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold and filled their houses with silver.
Job3:16 Or why was I not buried away like an untimely birth
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 There the captives are at ease together
Job3:19 Small and great are there the same
Job3:20 Why is light given to the toilers
Job3:21 They wait for death and it comes not; they search for it rather than for hidden treasures
Job3:22 Rejoice in it exultingly
Job3:23 Men whose path is hidden from them
Job3:24 For sighing comes more readily to me than food
Job3:25 For what I fear overtakes me
Job3:26 I have no peace nor ease; I have no rest
Job4:1 Then spoke Eliphaz the Temanite
Job4:2 If someone attempts a word with you
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Your words have upheld the stumbler; you have strengthened his faltering knees.
Job4:5 But now that it comes to you
Job4:6 Is not your piety a source of confidence
Job4:7 Reflect now
Job4:8 As I see it
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 Though the lion roars
Job4:11 The old lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 For a word was stealthily brought to me
Job4:13 In my thoughts during visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed before me
Job4:16 It paused
Job4:17 Can a man be righteous as against God? Can a mortal be blameless against his Maker?
Job4:18 Lo
Job4:19 How much more with those that dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 Morning or evening they may be shattered; with no heed paid to it
Job4:21 The pegs of their tent are plucked up; they die without knowing wisdom.
Job5:1 Call now! Will anyone respond to you? To which of the holy ones will you appeal?
Job5:2 Nay
Job5:3 I have seen a fool spreading his roots
Job5:4 His children shall be far from safety; they shall be crushed at the gate without a rescuer.
Job5:5 What they have reaped the hungry shall eat up; (or God shall take it away by blight;) and the thirsty shall swallow their substance.
Job5:6 For mischief comes not out of the earth
Job5:7 But man himself begets mischief
Job5:8 In your place
Job5:9 ***
Job5:10 He gives rain upon the earth and sends water upon the fields;
Job5:11 He sets up on high the lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the plans of the cunning
Job5:13 He catches the wise in their own ruses
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime
Job5:15 But the poor from the edge of the sword and from the hand of the mighty
Job5:16 Thus the unfortunate have hope
Job5:17 Happy is the man whom God reproves! The Almighty's chastening do not reject.
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 Out of six troubles he will deliver you
Job5:20 In famine he will deliver you from death
Job5:21 From the scourge of the tongue you shall be hidden
Job5:22 At destruction and want you shall laugh; the beasts of the earth you need not dread.
Job5:23 You shall be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 And you shall know that your tent is secure; taking stock of your household
Job5:25 You shall know that your descendants are many
Job5:26 You shall approach the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 Lo
Job6:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job6:2 Ah
Job6:3 They would now outweigh the sands of the sea! Because of this I speak without restraint.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty pierce me
Job6:5 Does the wild ass bray when he has grass? Does the ox low over his fodder?
Job6:6 Can a thing insipid be eaten without salt? Is there flavor in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 I refuse to touch them; they are loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 Oh
Job6:9 Even that God would decide to crush me
Job6:10 Then I should still have consolation and could exult through unremitting pain
Job6:11 What strength have I that I should endure
Job6:12 Have I the strength of stones
Job6:13 Have I no helper
Job6:14 A friend owes kindness to one in despair
Job6:15 My brethren are undependable as a brook
Job6:16 Though they may be black with ice
Job6:17 Yet once they flow
Job6:18 Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go into the desert and perish.
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema search
Job6:20 They are disappointed
Job6:21 It is thus that you have now become for me; you see a terrifying thing and are afraid.
Job6:22 Have I asked you to give me anything
Job6:23 Or to deliver me from the enemy
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How agreeable are honest words; yet how unconvincing is your argument!
Job6:26 Do you consider your words as proof
Job6:27 You would even cast lots for the orphan
Job6:28 Come
Job6:29 Think it over; let there be no injustice. Think it over; I still am right.
Job6:30 Is there insincerity on my tongue
Job7:1 Is not man's life on earth a drudgery? Are not his days those of a hireling?
Job7:2 He is a slave who longs for the shade
Job7:3 So I have been assigned months of misery
Job7:4 If in bed I say
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and scabs; my skin cracks and festers;
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle; they come to an end without hope.
Job7:7 Remember that my life is like the wind; I shall not see happiness again.
Job7:8 The eye that now sees me shall no more behold me; as you look at me
Job7:9 As a cloud dissolves and vanishes
Job7:10 He shall not again return to his house; his place shall know him no more.
Job7:11 My own utterance I will not restrain; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then you affright me with dreams and with visions terrify me
Job7:15 So that I should prefer choking and death rather than my pains.
Job7:16 I waste away: I cannot live forever; let me alone
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 You observe him with each new day and try him at every moment!
Job7:19 How long will it be before you look away from me
Job7:20 Though I have sinned
Job7:21 Why do you not pardon my offense
Job8:1 Bildad the Shuhite spoke out and said:
Job8:2 How long will you utter such things? The words from your mouth are like a mighty wind!
Job8:3 Does God pervert judgment
Job8:4 If your children have sinned against him and he has left them in the grip of their guilt
Job8:5 Still
Job8:6 Should you be blameless and upright
Job8:7 Your former state will be of little moment
Job8:8 If you inquire of the former generations
Job8:9 (As we are but of yesterday and have no knowledge
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you and utter their words of understanding?
Job8:11 Can the papyrus grow up without mire? Can the reed grass flourish without water?
Job8:12 While it is yet green and uncut
Job8:13 So is the end of everyone who forgets God
Job8:14 His confidence is but a gossamer thread and his trust is a spider's web.
Job8:15 He shall rely upon his family
Job8:16 He is full of sap before sunrise
Job8:17 About a heap of stones are his roots entwined; among the rocks he takes hold.
Job8:18 Yet if one tears him from his place
Job8:19 There he lies rotting beside the road
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 Once more will he fill your mouth with laughter
Job8:22 They that hate you shall be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job9:2 I know well that it is so; but how can a man be justified before God?
Job9:3 Should one wish to contend with him
Job9:4 God is wise in heart and mighty in strength; who has withstood him and remained unscathed?
Job9:5 He removes the mountains before they know it; he overturns them in his anger.
Job9:6 He shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 He commands the sun
Job9:8 He alone stretches out the heavens and treads upon the crests of the sea.
Job9:9 He made the Bear and Orion
Job9:10 He does great things past finding out
Job9:11 Should he come near me
Job9:12 Should he seize me forcibly
Job9:13 He is God and he does not relent; the helpers of Rahab bow beneath him.
Job9:14 How much less shall I give him any answer
Job9:15 Even though I were right
Job9:16 If I appealed to him and he answered my call
Job9:17 With a tempest he might overwhelm me
Job9:18 He need not suffer me to draw breath
Job9:19 If it be a question of strength
Job9:20 Though I were right
Job9:21 Though I am innocent
Job9:22 It is all one! therefore I say: Both the innocent and the wicked he destroys.
Job9:23 When the scourge slays suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hands of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges. If it is not he
Job9:25 My days are swifter than a runner
Job9:26 They shoot by like skiffs of reed
Job9:27 If I say: I will forget my complaining
Job9:28 Then I am in dread of all my pains; I know that you will not hold me innocent.
Job9:29 If I must be accounted guilty
Job9:30 If I should wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye
Job9:31 Yet you would plunge me in the ditch
Job9:32 For he is not a man like myself
Job9:33 Would that there were an arbiter between us
Job9:34 and withdraw his rod from me. Would that his terrors did not frighten me;
Job9:35 that I might speak without being afraid of him. Since this is not the case with me
Job10:1 I loathe my life. I will give myself up to complaint; I will speak from the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God: Do not put me in the wrong! Let me know why you oppose me.
Job10:3 Is it a pleasure for you to oppress
Job10:4 Have you eyes of flesh? Do you see as man sees?
Job10:5 Are your days as the days of a mortal
Job10:6 That you seek for guilt in me and search after my sins
Job10:7 Even though you know that I am not wicked
Job10:8 Your hands have formed me and fashioned me; will you then turn and destroy me?
Job10:9 Oh
Job10:10 Did you not pour me out as milk
Job10:11 With skin and flesh you clothed me
Job10:12 Grace and favor you granted me
Job10:13 Yet these things you have hidden in your heart; I know that they are your purpose:
Job10:14 If I should sin
Job10:15 If I should be wicked
Job10:16 Should it lift up
Job10:17 You renew your attack upon me and multiply your harassment of me; in waves your troops come against me.
Job10:18 Why then did you bring me forth from the womb? I should have died and no eye have seen me.
Job10:19 I should be as though I had never lived; I should have been taken from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are not the days of my life few? Let me alone
Job10:21 Before I go whence I shall not return
Job10:22 The black
Job11:1 And Zophar the Naamathite spoke out and said:
Job11:2 Should not the man of many words be answered
Job11:3 Shall your babblings keep men silent
Job11:4 Shall you say: My teaching is pure
Job11:5 But oh
Job11:6 And tell you that the secrets of wisdom are twice as effective: So you might learn that God will make you answer for your guilt.
Job11:7 Can you penetrate the designs of God? Dare you vie with the perfection of the Almighty?
Job11:8 It is higher than the heavens; what can you do? It is deeper than the nether world; what can you know?
Job11:9 It is longer than the earth in measure
Job11:10 If he seize and imprison or call to judgment
Job11:11 For he knows the worthlessness of men and sees iniquity; will he then ignore it?
Job11:12 Will empty man then gain understanding
Job11:13 If you set your heart aright and stretch out your hands toward him
Job11:14 If you remove all iniquity from your conduct
Job11:15 Surely then you may lift up your face in innocence; you may stand firm and unafraid.
Job11:16 For then you shall forget your misery
Job11:17 Then your life shall be brighter than the noonday; its gloom shall become as the morning
Job11:18 And you shall be secure
Job11:19 and you shall take your rest with none to disturb. Many shall entreat your favor
Job11:20 but the wicked
Job12:1 Then Job replied and said:
Job12:2 No doubt you are the intelligent folk
Job12:3 But I have intelligence as well as you; for who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I have become the sport of my neighbors: The one whom God answers when he calls upon him
Job12:5 The undisturbed esteem my downfall a disgrace such as awaits unsteady feet;
Job12:6 Yet the tents of robbers are prosperous
Job12:7 But now ask the beasts to teach you
Job12:8 Or the reptiles on earth to instruct you
Job12:9 Which of all these does not know that the hand of God has done this?
Job12:10 In his hand is the soul of every living thing
Job12:11 Does not the ear judge words as the mouth tastes food?
Job12:12 So with old age is wisdom
Job12:13 With him are wisdom and might; his are counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 If he breaks a thing down
Job12:15 He holds back the waters and there is drought; he sends them forth and they overwhelm the land.
Job12:16 With him are strength and prudence; the misled and the misleaders are his.
Job12:17 He sends counselors away barefoot
Job12:18 He loosens the bonds imposed by kings and leaves but a waistcloth to bind the king's own loins.
Job12:19 and lets their never-failing waters flow away.
Job12:20 He silences the trusted adviser
Job12:21 He breaks down the barriers of the streams
Job12:22 The recesses of the darkness he discloses
Job12:23 He makes nations great and he destroys them; he spreads peoples abroad and he abandons them.
Job12:24 He takes understanding from the leaders of the land
Job12:25 till they grope in the darkness without light; he makes them stagger like drunken men.
Job13:1 Lo
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 But I would speak with the Almighty; I wish to reason with God.
Job13:4 You are glossing over falsehoods and offering vain remedies
Job13:5 Oh
Job13:6 Hear now the rebuke I shall utter and listen to the reproof from my lips.
Job13:7 Is it for God that you speak falsehood? Is it for him that you utter deceit?
Job13:8 Is it for him that you show partiality? Do you play advocate on behalf of God?
Job13:9 Will it be well when he shall search you out? Would you impose on him as one does on men?
Job13:10 He will openly rebuke you if even in secret you show partiality.
Job13:11 Surely will his majesty affright you and the dread of him fall upon you.
Job13:12 Your reminders are ashy maxims
Job13:13 Be silent
Job13:14 I will carry my flesh between my teeth
Job13:15 Slay me though he might
Job13:16 And this shall be my salvation
Job13:17 Pay careful heed to my speech
Job13:18 Behold
Job13:19 If anyone can make a case against me
Job13:20 These things only do not use against me
Job13:21 Withdraw your hand far from me
Job13:22 Then call me
Job13:23 What are my faults and my sins? My misdeeds and my sins make known to me!
Job13:24 Why do you hide your face and consider me your enemy?
Job13:25 Will you harass a wind-driven leaf
Job13:26 For you draw up bitter indictments against me
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks; you watch all my paths and trace out all my footsteps.
Job13:28 Though he wears out like a leather bottle
Job14:1 Man born of woman is short-lived and full of trouble
Job14:2 Like a flower that springs up and fades
Job14:3 Upon such a one will you cast your eyes so as to bring him into judgment before you
Job14:4 Can a man be found who is clean of defilement? There is none
Job14:5 however short his days. You know the number of his months; you have fixed the limit which he cannot pass.
Job14:6 Look away from him and let him be
Job14:7 For a tree there is hope
Job14:8 Even though its root grow old in the earth
Job14:9 Yet at the first whiff of water it may flourish again and put forth branches like a young plant.
Job14:10 But when a man dies
Job14:11 As when the waters of a lake fail
Job14:12 So men lie down and rise not again. Till the heavens are no more
Job14:13 Oh
Job14:14 When a man has died
Job14:15 You would call
Job14:16 Surely then you would count my steps
Job14:17 My misdeeds would be sealed up in a pouch
Job14:18 But as a mountain falls at last and its rock is moved from its place
Job14:19 As waters wear away the stones and floods wash away the soil of the land
Job14:20 You prevail once for all against him and he passes on; with changed appearance you send him away.
Job14:21 If his sons are honored
Job14:22 Only his own flesh pains him
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite spoke and said:
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer with airy opinions
Job15:3 Should he argue in speech which does not avail
Job15:4 You in fact do away with piety
Job15:5 Because your wickedness instructs your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Are you indeed the first-born of mankind
Job15:8 Are you privy to the counsels of God
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What intelligence have you which we have not?
Job15:10 There are gray-haired old men among us more advanced in years than your father.
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God not enough for you
Job15:12 Why do your notions carry you away
Job15:13 So that you turn your anger against God and let such words escape your mouth!
Job15:14 What is a man that he should be blameless
Job15:15 If in his holy ones God places no confidence
Job15:16 How much less so is the abominable
Job15:17 I will show you
Job15:18 What wise men relate and have not contradicted since the days of their fathers
Job15:19 To whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man is in torment all his days
Job15:21 The sound of terrors is in his ears; when all is prosperous
Job15:22 He despairs of escaping the darkness
Job15:23 A wanderer
Job15:24 By day the darkness fills him with dread; distress and anguish overpower him.
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God and bade defiance to the Almighty
Job15:26 One shall rush sternly upon him with the stout bosses of his shield
Job15:27 Because he has blinded himself with his crassness
Job15:28 He shall dwell in ruinous cities
Job15:29 with no shadow to lengthen over the ground. He shall not be rich
Job15:30 A flame shall wither him up in his early growth
Job15:31 for vain shall be his bartering.
Job15:32 His stalk shall wither before its time
Job15:33 He shall be like a vine that sheds its grapes unripened
Job15:34 For the breed of the impious shall be sterile
Job15:35 They conceive malice and bring forth emptiness; they give birth to failure.
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job16:2 I have heard this sort of thing many times. Wearisome comforters are you all!
Job16:3 Is there no end to windy words? Or what sickness have you that you speak on?
Job16:4 I also could talk as you do
Job16:5 I could strengthen you with talk
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 But now that I am exhausted and stunned
Job16:8 As a witness there rises up my traducer
Job16:9 I am the prey his wrath assails
Job16:10 their mouths are agape to bite me. They smite me on the cheek insultingly; they are all enlisted against me.
Job16:11 God has given me over to the impious; into the clutches of the wicked he has cast me.
Job16:12 I was in peace
Job16:13 his arrows strike me from all directions
Job16:14 He pierces me with thrust upon thrust; he attacks me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have fastened sackcloth over my skin
Job16:16 My face is inflamed with weeping and there is darkness over my eyes
Job16:17 Although my hands are free from violence
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends it is who wrong me; before God my eyes drop tears
Job16:21 That he may do justice for a mortal in his presence and decide between a man and his neighbor.
Job16:22 For my years are numbered now
Job17:1 My spirit is broken
Job17:2 I am indeed mocked
Job17:3 Grant me one to offer you a pledge on my behalf: who is there that will give surety for me?
Job17:4 You darken their minds to knowledge; therefore they do not understand.
Job17:5 My lot is described as evil
Job17:6 and I am made a byword of the people; their object lesson I have become.
Job17:7 My eye has grown blind with anguish
Job17:8 Upright men are astonished at this
Job17:9 Yet the righteous shall hold to his way
Job17:10 But turn now
Job17:11 My days are passed away
Job17:12 Such men change the night into day; where there is darkness they talk of approaching light.
Job17:13 If I look for the nether world as my dwelling
Job17:14 If I must call corruption my father
Job17:15 Where then is my hope
Job17:16 Will they descend with me into the nether world? Shall we go down together into the dust?
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite replied and said:
Job18:2 When will you put an end to words? Reflect
Job18:3 Why are we accounted like the beasts
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in your anger
Job18:5 Truly
Job18:6 The light is darkened in his tent; in spite of him
Job18:7 His vigorous steps are hemmed in
Job18:8 For he rushes headlong into a net
Job18:9 A trap seizes him by the heel
Job18:10 A noose for him is hid on the ground
Job18:11 On every side terrors affright him; they harry him at each step.
Job18:12 Disaster is ready at his side
Job18:13 the first-born of death consumes his limbs.
Job18:14 Fiery destruction lodges in his tent
Job18:15 over his abode brimstone is scattered.
Job18:16 Below
Job18:17 His memory perishes from the land
Job18:18 He is driven from light into darkness
Job18:19 He has neither son nor grandson among his people
Job18:20 They who come after shall be appalled at his fate; they who went before are struck with horror.
Job18:21 So is it then with the dwelling of the impious man
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job19:2 How long will you vex my soul
Job19:3 These ten times you have reviled me
Job19:4 Be it indeed that I am at fault and that my fault remains with me
Job19:5 Even so
Job19:6 Know then that God has dealt unfairly with me
Job19:7 If I cry out Injustice! I am not heard. I cry for help
Job19:8 He has barred my way and I cannot pass; he has veiled my path in darkness;
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my glory
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 His wrath he has kindled against me; he counts me among his enemies.
Job19:12 His troops advance as one man; they build up their road to attack me
Job19:13 My brethren have withdrawn from me
Job19:14 My kinsfolk and companions neglect me
Job19:15 Even my handmaids treat me as a stranger; I am an alien in their sight.
Job19:16 I call my servant
Job19:17 My breath is abhorred by my wife; I am loathsome to the men of my family.
Job19:18 The young children
Job19:19 All my intimate friends hold me in horror; those whom I loved have turned against me!
Job19:20 My bones cleave to my skin
Job19:21 Pity me
Job19:22 Why do you hound me as though you were divine
Job19:23 Oh
Job19:24 That with an iron chisel and with lead they were cut in the rock forever!
Job19:25 But as for me
Job19:26 And from my flesh I shall see God; my inmost being is consumed with longing.
Job19:27 Whom I myself shall see: my own eyes
Job19:28 But you who say
Job19:29 Be afraid of the sword for yourselves
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite spoke and said:
Job20:2 and because of this I am disturbed. So now my thoughts provide me with an answer
Job20:3 A rebuke which puts me to shame I hear
Job20:4 Do you not know this from olden time
Job20:5 That the triumph of the wicked is short and the joy of the impious but for a moment?
Job20:6 Though his pride mount up to the heavens and his head reach to the clouds
Job20:7 Yet he perishes forever like the fuel of his fire
Job20:8 Like a dream he takes flight and is not found again; he fades away like a vision of the night.
Job20:9 The eye which saw him does so no more; nor shall his dwelling again behold him.
Job20:10 and his hands shall yield up his riches.
Job20:11 Though his frame is full of youthful vigor
Job20:12 Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 Though he retains it and will not let it go but keeps it still within his mouth
Job20:14 Yet in his stomach the food shall turn; it shall be venom of asps inside him.
Job20:15 The riches he swallowed he shall disgorge; God shall compel his belly to disown them.
Job20:16 The poison of asps he shall drink in; the viper's fangs shall slay him.
Job20:17 He shall see no streams of oil
Job20:18 Restoring his gains
Job20:19 Because he has oppressed the poor
Job20:20 Though he has known no quiet in his greed
Job20:21 Therefore his prosperity shall not endure
Job20:22 When he abounds to overflowing
Job20:23 God shall send against him the fury of his wrath and rain down his missiles of war upon him.
Job20:24 Should he escape the iron weapon
Job20:25 The dart shall come out of his back; terrors shall fall upon him.
Job20:26 Complete darkness is in store for him; the fire which shall consume him needs not to be fanned.
Job20:27 The heavens shall reveal his guilt
Job20:28 The flood shall sweep away his house with the waters that run off in the day of God's anger.
Job20:29 This is the portion of a wicked man
Job21:1 Then Job said in reply:
Job21:2 At least listen to my words
Job21:3 Bear with me while I speak; and after I have spoken
Job21:4 Is my complaint toward man? And why should I not be impatient?
Job21:5 Look at me and be astonished
Job21:6 When I think of it
Job21:7 Why do the wicked survive
Job21:8 Their progeny is secure in their sight; they see before them their kinsfolk and their offspring.
Job21:9 Their homes are safe and without fear
Job21:10 Their bulls gender without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
Job21:11 These folk have infants numerous as lambs
Job21:12 They sing to the timbrel and harp
Job21:13 They live out their days in prosperity
Job21:14 Yet they say to God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty that we should serve him? And what gain shall we have if we pray to him?
Job21:16 If their happiness is not in their own hands and if the counsel of the wicked is repulsive to God
Job21:17 How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does destruction come upon them
Job21:18 Let them be like straw before the wind
Job21:19 May God not store up the man's misery for his children; let him requite the man himself so that he feels it
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see the calamity
Job21:21 For what interest has he in his family after him
Job21:22 Can anyone teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in his full vigor
Job21:24 His figure is full and nourished
Job21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 Alike they lie down in the dust
Job21:27 Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked the wayfarers and do you not recognize their monuments?
Job21:30 Nay
Job21:31 Who will charge him with his conduct to his face
Job21:32 and on the day he is carried to the grave
Job21:33 Sweet to him are the clods of the valley
Job21:34 How then can you offer me vain comfort
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable to God? Though to himself a wise man be profitable!
Job22:3 Is it of advantage to the Almighty if you are just? Or is it a gain to him if you make your ways perfect?
Job22:4 Is it because of your piety that he reproves you-- that he enters with you into judgment?
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness manifold? Are not your iniquities endless?
Job22:6 You have unjustly kept your kinsmen's goods in pawn
Job22:7 To the thirsty you have given no water to drink
Job22:8 As if the land belonged to the man of might
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty-handed
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about you
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 Does not God
Job22:13 Yet you say
Job22:14 Clouds hide him so that he cannot see; he walks upon the vault of the heavens!
Job22:15 Do you indeed keep to the ancient way trodden by worthless men
Job22:16 Who were snatched away before their time; whose foundations a flood swept away?
Job22:17 These men said to God
Job22:18 (Yet he had filled their houses with good things! But far be from me the mind of the impious!)
Job22:19 The just look on and are gladdened
Job22:20 Truly these have been destroyed where they stood
Job22:21 Come to terms with him to be at peace. In this shall good come to you:
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 And treat raw gold like dust
Job22:25 Then the Almighty himself shall be your gold and your sparkling silver.
Job22:26 For then you shall delight in the Almighty and you shall lift up your face toward God.
Job22:27 You shall entreat him and he will hear you
Job22:28 When you make a decision
Job22:29 For he brings down the pride of the haughty
Job22:30 God delivers him who is innocent; you shall be delivered through cleanness of hands.
Job23:1 Again Job answered and said:
Job23:2 Though I know my complaint is bitter
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would set out my cause before him
Job23:5 I would learn the words with which he would answer
Job23:6 Even should he contend against me with his great power
Job23:7 There the upright man might reason with him
Job23:8 But if I go to the east
Job23:9 Where the north enfolds him
Job23:10 Yet he knows my way; if he proved me
Job23:11 My foot has always walked in his steps; his way I have kept and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 From the commands of his lips I have not departed; the words of his mouth I have treasured in my heart.
Job23:13 But he had decided
Job23:14 For he will carry out what is appointed for me; and many such things may yet be in his mind.
Job23:15 Therefore am I dismayed before him; when I take thought
Job23:16 Indeed God has made my courage fail; the Almighty has put me in dismay.
Job23:17 Yes
Job24:1 Why are not times set by the Almighty
Job24:2 The wicked remove landmarks; they steal away herds and pasture them.
Job24:3 The asses of orphans they drive away; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They force the needy off the road; all the poor of the land are driven into hiding.
Job24:5 Like wild asses in the desert
Job24:6 they harvest at night in the untilled land.
Job24:7 They pass the night naked
Job24:8 They are drenched with the rain of the mountains
Job24:9 ***
Job24:10 and famished are those who carry the sheaves.
Job24:11 Between the rows they press out the oil; they glean in the the vineyard of the wicked. They tread the wine presses
Job24:12 From the dust the dying groan
Job24:13 There are those who are rebels against the light; they know not its ways; they abide not in its paths.
Job24:14 When there is no light the murderer rises
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight; he says
Job24:16 in the dark he breaks into houses. By day they shut themselves in; none of them know the light
Job24:17 for daylight they regard as darkness.
Job24:18 Their portion in the land is accursed
Job24:19 ***
Job24:20 and wickedness is splintered like wood.
Job24:21 ***
Job24:22 To him who rises without assurance of his life
Job24:23 he gives safety and support. He sustains the mighty by his strength
Job24:24 They are exalted for a while
Job24:25 If this be not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job25:2 Dominion and awesomeness are his who brings about harmony in his heavens.
Job25:3 Is there any numbering of his troops? Yet to which of them does not his light extend?
Job25:4 How can a man be just in God's sight
Job25:5 Behold
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 Then Job spoke again and said:
Job26:2 What help you give to the powerless
Job26:3 How you counsel
Job26:4 With whose help have you uttered those words
Job26:5 The shades beneath writhe in terror
Job26:6 Naked before him is the nether world
Job26:7 He stretches out the North over empty space
Job26:8 He binds up the waters in his clouds
Job26:9 He holds back the appearance of the full moon by spreading his clouds before it.
Job26:10 He has marked out a circle on the surface of the deep as the boundary of light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are stunned at his thunderous rebuke;
Job26:12 By his power he stirs up the sea
Job26:13 With his angry breath he scatters the waters
Job26:14 Lo
Job27:1 ***
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 So long as I still have life in me and the breath of God is in my nostrils
Job27:4 My lips shall not speak falsehood
Job27:5 Far be it from me to account you right; till I die I will not renounce my innocence.
Job27:6 My justice I maintain and I will not relinquish it; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
Job27:7 Let my enemy be as the wicked and my adversary as the unjust!
Job27:8 For what can the impious man expect when he is cut off
Job27:9 Will God then attend to his cry when calamity comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he then delight in the Almighty and call upon him constantly?
Job27:11 I will teach you the manner of God's dealings
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man from God
Job27:14 Though his children be many
Job27:15 His survivors
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver like dust and store away mounds of clothing
Job27:17 What he has stored the just man shall wear
Job27:18 He builds his house as of cobwebs
Job27:19 He lies down a rich man
Job27:20 Terrors rush upon him by day; at night the tempest carries him off.
Job27:21 The storm wind seizes him and he disappears; it sweeps him out of his place.
Job28:1 There is indeed a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the earth
Job28:3 He has set a boundary for the darkness; to the farthest confines he penetrates.
Job28:4 ***
Job28:5 The earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the source of sapphires
Job28:7 The path to it no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it
Job28:9 He sets his hand to the flinty rock
Job28:10 He splits channels in the rocks; his eyes behold all that is precious.
Job28:11 He probes the wellsprings of the streams
Job28:12 But whence can wisdom be obtained
Job28:13 Man knows nothing to equal it
Job28:14 The abyss declares
Job28:15 Solid gold cannot purchase it
Job28:16 It cannot be bought with gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold or crystal cannot equal it
Job28:18 Neither coral nor jasper should be thought of; it surpasses pearls and
Job28:19 Arabian topaz.
Job28:20 Whence
Job28:21 It is hid from the eyes of any beast; from the birds of the air it is concealed.
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 God knows the way to it; it is he who is familiar with its place.
Job28:24 For he beholds the ends of the earth and sees all that is under the heavens.
Job28:25 He has weighed out the wind
Job28:26 When he made rules for the rain and a path for the thunderbolts
Job28:27 Then he saw wisdom and appraised it
Job28:28 And to man he said: Behold
Job29:1 Job took up his theme anew and said;
Job29:2 Oh
Job29:3 While he kept his lamp shining above my head
Job29:4 As I was in my flourishing days
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my footsteps were bathed in milk
Job29:7 When I went forth to the gate of the city and set up my seat in the square-
Job29:8 Then the young men saw me and withdrew
Job29:9 The chief men refrained from speaking and covered their mouths with their hands;
Job29:10 The voice of the princes was silenced
Job29:11 Whoever heard of me blessed me; those who saw me commended me.
Job29:12 For I rescued the poor who cried out for help
Job29:13 The blessing of those in extremity came upon me
Job29:14 I wore my honesty like a garment; justice was my robe and my turban.
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy; the rights of the stranger I studied
Job29:17 And I broke the jaws of the wicked man; from his teeth I forced the prey.
Job29:18 Then I said: In my own nest I shall grow old; I shall multiply years like the phoenix.
Job29:19 My root is spread out to the waters; the dew rests by night on my branches.
Job29:20 My glory is fresh within me
Job29:21 For me they listened and waited; they were silent for my counsel.
Job29:22 Once I spoke
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain; they drank in my words like the spring rains.
Job29:24 When I smiled on them they were reassured;
Job29:25 mourners took comfort from my cheerful glance. I chose out their way and presided; I took a king's place in the armed forces.
Job30:1 But now they hold me in derision who are younger in years than I; Whose fathers I should have disdained to rank with the dogs of my flock.
Job30:2 Such strength as they had
Job30:3 In want and hunger was their lot
Job30:4 They plucked saltwort and shrubs; the roots of the broom plant were their food.
Job30:5 They were banished from among men
Job30:6 To dwell on the slopes of the wadies
Job30:7 Among the bushes they raised their raucous cry; under the nettles they huddled together.
Job30:8 Irresponsible
Job30:9 Yet now they sing of me in mockery; I am become a byword among them.
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 Indeed
Job30:12 To subvert my paths they rise up; they build their approaches for my ruin.
Job30:13 To destroy me
Job30:14 as through a wide breach they advance. Amid the uproar they come on in waves;
Job30:15 over me rolls the terror. My dignity is borne off on the wind
Job30:16 My soul ebbs away from me;
Job30:17 My frame takes no rest by night; my inward parts seethe and will not be stilled.
Job30:18 One with great power lays hold of my clothing; by the collar of my tunic he seizes me:
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire; I am leveled with the dust and ashes.
Job30:20 I cry to you
Job30:21 Then you turn upon me without mercy and with your strong hand you buffet me.
Job30:22 You raise me up and drive me before the wind; I am tossed about by the tempest.
Job30:23 Indeed I know you will turn me back in death to the destined place of everyone alive.
Job30:24 Yet should not a hand be held out to help a wretched man in his calamity?
Job30:25 Or have I not wept for the hardships of others; was not my soul grieved for the destitute?
Job30:26 Yet when I looked for good
Job30:27 days of affliction have overtaken me.
Job30:28 I go about in gloom
Job30:29 I have become the brother of jackals
Job30:30 My blackened skin falls away from me; the heat scorches my very frame.
Job30:31 My harp is turned to mourning
Job31:1 If I have made an agreement with my eyes and entertained any thoughts against a maiden;
Job31:2 But what is man's lot from God above
Job31:3 Is it not calamity for the unrighteous
Job31:4 Does he not see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked in falsehood and my foot has hastened to deceit;
Job31:6 Let God weigh me in the scales of justice; thus will he know my innocence!
Job31:7 If my steps have turned out of the way
Job31:8 Then may I sow
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed toward a woman
Job31:10 Then may my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be heinous
Job31:12 A fire that should burn down to the abyss till it consumed all my possessions to the roots.
Job31:13 Had I refused justice to my manservant or to my maid
Job31:14 What then should I do when God rose up; what could I answer when he demanded an account?
Job31:15 Did not he who made me in the womb make him? Did not the same One fashion us before our birth?
Job31:16 If I have denied anything to the poor
Job31:17 While I ate my portion alone
Job31:18 Though like a father God has reared me from my youth
Job31:19 If I have seen a wanderer without clothing
Job31:20 Whose limbs have not blessed me when warmed with the fleece of my sheep;
Job31:21 If I have raised my hand against the innocent because I saw that I had supporters at the gate--
Job31:22 Then may my arm fall from the shoulder
Job31:23 For the dread of God will be upon me
Job31:24 Had I put my trust in gold or called fine gold my security;
Job31:25 Or had I rejoiced that my wealth was great
Job31:26 Had I looked upon the sun as it shone
Job31:27 And had my heart been secretly enticed to waft them a kiss with my hand;
Job31:28 This too would be a crime for condemnation
Job31:29 Had I rejoiced at the destruction of my enemy or exulted when evil fell upon him
Job31:30 Even though I had not suffered my mouth to sin by uttering a curse against his life--
Job31:31 Had not the men of my tent exclaimed
Job31:32 Because no stranger lodged in the street
Job31:33 Had I
Job31:34 Because I feared the noisy multitude and the scorn of the tribes terrified me- then I should have remained silent
Job31:35 Oh
Job31:36 Surely
Job31:37 Of all my steps I should give him an account; like a prince I should present myself before him. This is my final plea; let the Almighty answer me! The words of Job are ended.
Job31:38 If my land has cried out against me till its very furrows complained;
Job31:39 If I have eaten its produce without payment and grieved the hearts of its tenants;
Job31:40 Then let the thistles grow instead of wheat and noxious weeds instead of barley!

Job32:7 Days should speak

Job32:9 It is not those of many days who are wise

Job32:22 For I know nought of flattery; if I did
Job33:1 Therefore
Job33:2 Behold

Job33:4 For the spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 If you are able
Job33:6 Behold I
Job33:7 Therefore no fear of me should dismay you
Job33:8 But you have said in my hearing
Job33:9 I am clean and without transgression; I am innocent; there is no guilt in me.

Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks; he watches all my ways!
Job33:12 In this you are not just
Job33:13 Why
Job33:14 For God does speak
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 It is then he opens the ears of men and as a warning to them
Job33:17 By turning man from evil and keeping pride away from him
Job33:18 He withholds his soul from the pit and his life from passing to the grave.
Job33:19 Or a man is chastened on his bed by pain and unceasing suffering within his frame
Job33:20 So that to his appetite food becomes repulsive
Job33:21 His flesh is wasted so that it cannot be seen
Job33:22 His soul draws near to the pit
Job33:23 If then there be for him an angel
Job33:24 He will take pity on him and say
Job33:25 Then his flesh shall become soft as a boy's; he shall be again as in the days of his youth.
Job33:26 He shall pray and God will favor him; he shall see God's face with rejoicing.
Job33:27 He shall sing before men and say
Job33:28 He delivered my soul from passing to the pit

Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Then Elihu continued and said:
Job34:2 Hear
Job34:3 For the ear tests words

Job34:11 Rather

Job34:16 Now

Job34:19 Who neither favors the person of princes
Job34:20 in a moment they die
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness so dense that evildoers can hide in it.
Job34:23 For he forewarns no man of his time to come before God in judgment.

Job34:25 Therefore he discerns their works; he turns at night and crushes them.

Job34:34 Men of understanding will say to me
Job34:35 Job speaks without intelligence
Job34:36 Let Job be tried to the limit
Job34:37 For he is adding rebellion to his sin by brushing off our arguments and addressing many words to God.
Job35:1 Then Elihu proceeded and said:
Job35:2 Do you think it right to say
Job35:3 To say

Job35:10 Saying
Job35:11 Taught us rather than the beasts of the earth

Job35:15 But now that you have done otherwise

Job36:1 Elihu proceeded further and said:
Job36:2 Wait yet a little and I will instruct you
Job36:3 I will bring my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For indeed
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He withholds not the just man's rights
Job36:7 And with kings upon thrones he sets them
Job36:8 Or if they are bound with fetters and held fast by bonds of affliction
Job36:9 Then he makes known to them what they have done and their sins of boastful pride.
Job36:10 He opens their ears to correction and exhorts them to turn back from evil.
Job36:11 If they obey and serve him
Job36:12 But if they obey not
Job36:13 The impious in heart lay up anger for themselves; they cry not for help when he enchains them;
Job36:14 Therefore they expire in youth
Job36:15 But he saves the unfortunate through their affliction

Job36:28 Till the skies run with them and the showers rain down on mankind.
Job36:29 ***

Job36:31 For by these he nourishes the nations
Job36:32 In his hands he holds the lightning
Job36:33 His thunder speaks for him and incites the fury of the storm.
Job37:1 At this my heart trembles and leaps out of its place
Job37:2 To hear his angry voice as it rumbles forth from his mouth!
Job37:3 Everywhere under the heavens he sends it
Job37:4 Again his voice roars-- the majestic sound of his thunder.
Job37:5 He does great things beyond our knowing; wonders past our searching out.
Job37:6 For he says to the snow
Job37:7 He shuts up all mankind indoors;
Job37:8 the wild beasts take to cover and remain quietly in their dens.
Job37:9 Out of its chamber comes forth the tempest; from the north winds
Job37:10 With his breath God brings the frost
Job37:11 With hail
Job37:12 He it is who changes their rounds
Job37:13 whether for punishment or mercy
Job37:14 Hearken to this
Job37:15 Do you know how God lays his commands upon them

Job37:17 You
