Job37:20 Will he be told about it when I speak
Job37:21 Nay
Job37:22 From the North the splendor comes
Job37:23 The Almighty! we cannot discover him
Job37:24 Therefore men revere him
Job38:1 Then the LORD addressed Job out of the storm and said:
Job38:2 Who is this that obscures divine plans with words of ignorance?
Job38:3 Gird up your loins now
Job38:4 Where were you when I founded the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who determined its size; do you know? Who stretched out the measuring line for it?
Job38:6 Into what were its pedestals sunk
Job38:7 While the morning stars sang in chorus and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 And who shut within doors the sea
Job38:9 When I made the clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling bands?
Job38:10 When I set limits for it and fastened the bar of its door
Job38:11 And said: Thus far shall you come but no farther
Job38:12 Have you ever in your lifetime commanded the morning and shown the dawn its place
Job38:13 For taking hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 The earth is changed as is clay by the seal
Job38:15 But from the wicked the light is withheld
Job38:16 Have you entered into the sources of the sea
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been shown to you
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the breadth of the earth? Tell me
Job38:19 Which is the way to the dwelling place of light
Job38:20 That you may take them to their boundaries and set them on their homeward paths?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouse of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for times of stress
Job38:24 Which way to the parting of the winds
Job38:25 Who has laid out a channel for the downpour and for the thunderstorm a path
Job38:26 To bring rain to no man's land
Job38:27 To enrich the waste and desolate ground till the desert blooms with verdure?
Job38:28 Has the rain a father; or who has begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb comes the ice
Job38:30 When the waters lie covered as though with stone that holds captive the surface of the deep?
Job38:31 Have you fitted a curb to the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you bring forth the Mazzaroth in their season
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens; can you put into effect their plan on the earth?
Job38:34 Can you raise your voice among the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth the lightnings on their way
Job38:36 Who puts wisdom in the heart
Job38:37 Who counts the clouds in his wisdom? Or who tilts the water jars of heaven
Job38:38 So that the dust of earth is fused into a mass and its clods made solid?
Job38:39 Do you hunt the prey for the lioness or appease the hunger of her cubs
Job38:40 While they crouch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provides nourishment for the ravens when their young ones cry out to God
Job39:1 Do you know about the birth of the mountain goats
Job39:2 Number the months that they must fulfill
Job39:3 They crouch down and bear their young; they deliver their progeny in the desert.
Job39:4 When their offspring thrive and grow
Job39:5 Who has given the wild ass his freedom
Job39:6 I have made the wilderness his home and the salt flats his dwelling.
Job39:7 He scoffs at the uproar of the city
Job39:8 He ranges the mountains for pasture
Job39:9 Will the wild ox consent to serve you
Job39:10 Will a rope bind him in the furrow
Job39:11 Will you trust him for his great strength and leave to him the fruits of your toil?
Job39:12 Can you rely on him to thresh out your grain and gather in the yield of your threshing floor?
Job39:13 The wings of the ostrich beat idly; her plumage is lacking in pinions.
Job39:14 When she leaves her eggs on the ground and deposits them in the sand
Job39:15 Unmindful that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 She cruelly disowns her young and ruthlessly makes nought of her brood;
Job39:17 For God has withheld wisdom from her and has given her no share in understanding.
Job39:18 Yet in her swiftness of foot she makes sport of the horse and his rider.
Job39:19 Do you give the horse his strength
Job39:20 Do you make the steed to quiver while his thunderous snorting spreads terror?
Job39:21 He jubilantly paws the plain and rushes in his might against the weapons.
Job39:22 He laughs at fear and cannot be deterred; he turns not back from the sword.
Job39:23 Around him rattles the quiver
Job39:24 Frenzied and trembling he devours the ground; he holds not back at the sound of the trumpet
Job39:25 but at each blast he cries
Job39:26 Is it by your discernment that the hawk soars
Job39:27 Does the eagle fly up at your command to build his nest aloft?
Job39:28 On the cliff he dwells and spends the night
Job39:29 From thence he watches for his prey; his eyes behold it afar off.
Job39:30 His young ones greedily drink blood; where the slain are
Job40:1 The LORD then said to Job:
Job40:2 Will we have arguing with the Almighty by the critic? Let him who would correct God give answer!
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD and said:
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Though I have spoken once
Job40:6 Then the LORD addressed Job out of the storm and said:
Job40:7 Gird up your loins now
Job40:8 Would you refuse to acknowledge my right? Would you condemn me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Have you an arm like that of God
Job40:10 Adorn yourself with grandeur and majesty
Job40:11 Let loose the fury of your wrath;
Job40:12 tear down the wicked and shatter them. Bring down the haughty with a glance;
Job40:13 bury them in the dust together; in the hidden world imprison them.
Job40:14 Then will I too acknowledge that your own right hand can save you.
Job40:15 See
Job40:16 Behold the strength in his loins
Job40:17 He carries his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are like cables.
Job40:18 His bones are like tubes of bronze; his frame is like iron rods.
Job40:19 He came at the beginning of God's ways
Job40:20 For the produce of the mountains is brought to him
Job40:21 Under the lotus trees he lies
Job40:22 The lotus trees cover him with their shade; all about him are the poplars on the bank.
Job40:23 If the river grows violent
Job40:24 Who can capture him by his eyes
Job40:25 Can you lead about Leviathan with a hook
Job40:26 Can you put a rope into his nose
Job40:27 Will he then plead with you
Job40:28 Will he make an agreement with you that you may have him as a slave forever?
Job40:29 Can you play with him
Job40:30 Will the traders bargain for him? Will the merchants divide him up?
Job40:31 Can you fill his hide with barbs
Job40:32 Once you but lay a hand upon him
Job41:1 Whoever might vainly hope to do so need only see him to be overthrown.
Job41:2 Is he not relentless when aroused; who then dares stand before him?
Job41:3 Who has assailed him and come off safe-- Who under all the heavens?
Job41:4 I need hardly mention his limbs
Job41:5 Who can strip off his outer garment
Job41:6 Who can force open the doors of his mouth
Job41:7 Rows of scales are on his back
Job41:8 They are fitted each so close to the next that no space intervenes;
Job41:9 So joined one to another that they hold fast and cannot be parted.
Job41:10 When he sneezes
Job41:11 Out of his mouth go forth firebrands; sparks of fire leap forth.
Job41:12 From his nostrils issues steam
Job41:13 His breath sets coals afire; a flame pours from his mouth.
Job41:14 Strength abides in his neck
Job41:15 ***
Job41:16 His heart is hard as stone; his flesh
Job41:17 When he rises up
Job41:18 Should the sword reach him
Job41:19 He regards iron as straw
Job41:20 The arrow will not put him to flight; slingstones used against him are but straws.
Job41:21 Clubs he esteems as splinters; he laughs at the crash of the spear.
Job41:22 His belly is sharp as pottery fragments; he spreads like a threshing sledge upon the mire.
Job41:23 He makes the depths boil like a pot; the sea he churns like perfume in a kettle.
Job41:24 Behind him he leaves a shining path; you would think the deep had the hoary head of age.
Job41:25 Upon the earth there is not his like
Job41:26 All
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD and said:
Job42:2 I know that you can do all things
Job42:3 I have dealt with great things that I do not understand; things too wonderful for me
Job42:4 ***
Job42:5 I had heard of you by word of mouth
Job42:6 Therefore I disown what I have said
Job42:7 And it came to pass after the LORD had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 Now
Job42:9 Then Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 Also
Job42:11 Then all his brethren and his sisters came to him
Job42:12 Thus the LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his earlier ones. For he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 And he had seven sons and three daughters
Job42:14 of whom he called the first Jemimah
Job42:15 In all the land no other women were as beautiful as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them an inheritance among their brethren.
Job42:16 After this
Job42:17 Then Job died
Psalms1:1 Happy those who do not follow the counsel of the wicked
Psalms1:2 Rather
Psalms1:3 They are like a tree planted near streams of water
Psalms1:4 But not the wicked! They are like chaff driven by the wind.
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not survive judgment
Psalms1:6 The LORD watches over the way of the just
Psalms2:1 Why do the nations protest and the peoples grumble in vain?
Psalms2:2 Kings on earth rise up and princes plot together against the LORD and his anointed:
Psalms2:3 Let us break their shackles and cast off their chains!
Psalms2:4 The one enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord derides them
Psalms2:5 Then speaks to them in anger
Psalms2:6 I myself have installed my king on Zion
Psalms2:7 I will proclaim the decree of the LORD
Psalms2:8 Only ask it of me
Psalms2:9 With an iron rod you shall shepherd them
Psalms2:10 And now
Psalms2:11 Serve the LORD with fear; with trembling bow down in homage
Psalms3:1 A psalm of David
Psalms3:2 How many are my foes
Psalms3:3 How many say of me
Psalms3:4 But you
Psalms3:5 Whenever I cried out to the LORD
Psalms3:6 Whenever I lay down and slept
Psalms3:7 I do not fear
Psalms3:8 Arise
Psalms3:9 Safety comes from the LORD! Your blessing for your people! Selah
Psalms4:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments. A psalm of David.
Psalms4:2 Answer when I call
Psalms4:3 How long will you people mock my honor
Psalms4:4 Know that the LORD works wonders for the faithful; the LORD hears when I call out.
Psalms4:5 Tremble and do not sin; upon your beds ponder in silence.
Psalms4:6 Offer fitting sacrifice and trust in the LORD.
Psalms4:7 Many say
Psalms4:8 But you have given my heart more joy than they have when grain and wine abound.
Psalms4:9 In peace I shall both lie down and sleep
Psalms5:1 For the leader; with wind instruments. A psalm of David.
Psalms5:2 Hear my words
Psalms5:3 Hear my cry for help
Psalms5:4 at dawn you will hear my cry; at dawn I will plead before you and wait.
Psalms5:5 You are not a god who delights in evil; no wicked person finds refuge with you;
Psalms5:6 the arrogant cannot stand before you. You hate all who do evil;
Psalms5:7 you destroy all who speak falsely. Murderers and deceivers the LORD abhors.
Psalms5:8 But I can enter your house because of your great love. I can worship in your holy temple because of my reverence for you
Psalms5:9 Guide me in your justice because of my foes; make straight your way before me.
Psalms5:10 For there is no sincerity in their mouths; their hearts are corrupt. Their throats are open graves; on their tongues are subtle lies.
Psalms5:11 Declare them guilty
Psalms5:12 Then all who take refuge in you will be glad and forever shout for joy. Protect them that you may be the joy of those who love your name.
Psalms5:13 For you
Psalms6:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments
Psalms6:2 Do not reprove me in your anger
Psalms6:3 Have pity on me
Psalms6:4 In utter terror is my soul-- and you
Psalms6:5 Turn
Psalms6:6 For who among the dead remembers you? Who praises you in Sheol?
Psalms6:7 I am wearied with sighing; all night long tears drench my bed; my couch is soaked with weeping.
Psalms6:8 My eyes are dimmed with sorrow
Psalms6:9 Away from me
Psalms6:10 The LORD has heard my prayer; the LORD takes up my plea.
Psalms6:11 My foes will be terrified and disgraced; all will fall back in sudden shame.
Psalms7:1 A plaintive song of David
Psalms7:2 LORD my God
Psalms7:3 Lest they maul me like lions
Psalms7:4 LORD my God
Psalms7:5 If I have repaid my friend with evil-- I spared even those who hated me without cause--
Psalms7:6 Then let my enemy pursue and overtake me
Psalms7:7 Rise up
Psalms7:8 Have the assembly of the peoples gather about you; sit on your throne high above them
Psalms7:9 O LORD
Psalms7:10 Bring the malice of the wicked to an end; uphold the innocent
Psalms7:11 A shield before me is God who saves the honest heart.
Psalms7:12 God is a just judge
Psalms7:13 If sinners do not repent
Psalms7:14 Prepares his deadly shafts
Psalms7:15 Sinners conceive iniquity; pregnant with mischief
Psalms7:16 They open a hole and dig it deep
Psalms7:17 Their mischief comes back upon themselves; their violence falls on their own heads. VI
Psalms7:18 I praise the justice of the LORD; I celebrate the name of the LORD Most High.

Psalms8:2 O LORD
Psalms8:3 Out of the mouths of babes and infants you have drawn a defense against your foes

Psalms8:5 What are humans that you are mindful of them
Psalms8:6 Yet you have made them little less than a god
Psalms8:7 You have given them rule over the works of your hands
Psalms8:8 All sheep and oxen

Psalms9:1 For the leader; according to Muth Labben. A psalm of David.
Psalms9:2 I will praise you
Psalms9:3 I will delight and rejoice in you; I will sing hymns to your name
Psalms9:4 For my enemies turn back; they stumble and perish before you.
Psalms9:5 You upheld my right and my cause
Psalms9:6 You rebuked the nations
Psalms9:7 The enemies have been ruined forever; you destroyed their cities; their memory has perished.
Psalms9:8 The LORD rules forever
Psalms9:9 It is God who governs the world with justice
Psalms9:10 The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed
Psalms9:11 Those who honor your name trust in you; you never forsake those who seek you
Psalms9:12 Sing hymns to the LORD enthroned on Zion; proclaim God's deeds among the nations!
Psalms9:13 For the avenger of bloodshed remembers
Psalms9:14 Have mercy on me
Psalms9:15 Then I will declare all your praises
Psalms9:16 The nations fall into the pit they dig; in the snare they hide
Psalms9:17 The LORD is revealed in this divine rule: by the deeds they do the wicked are trapped. Higgaion. Selah
Psalms9:18 To Sheol the wicked will depart
Psalms9:19 The needy will never be forgotten
Psalms9:20 Arise

Psalms10:1 Why
Psalms10:2 Arrogant scoundrels pursue the poor; they trap them by their cunning schemes.
Psalms10:3 The wicked even boast of their greed; these robbers curse and scorn the LORD.
Psalms10:4 In their insolence the wicked boast: God doesn't care
Psalms10:5 Yet their affairs always succeed; they ignore your judgment on high; they sneer at all who oppose them.
Psalms10:6 They say in their hearts
Psalms10:7 Their mouths are full of oaths
Psalms10:8 They wait in ambush near towns; their eyes watch for the helpless. to murder the innocent in secret.
Psalms10:9 They lurk in ambush like lions in a thicket
Psalms10:10 The helpless are crushed
Psalms10:11 Who say in their hearts
Psalms10:12 Rise up
Psalms10:13 Why should the wicked scorn God
Psalms10:14 But you do see; you do observe this misery and sorrow; you take the matter in hand. To you the helpless can entrust their cause; you are the defender of orphans.
Psalms10:15 Break the arms of the wicked and depraved; make them account for their crimes; let none of them survive.
Psalms10:16 The LORD is king forever; the nations have vanished from God's land.
Psalms10:17 You listen
Psalms10:18 You win justice for the orphaned and oppressed; no one on earth will cause terror again.

Psalms17:3 You have tested my heart
Psalms17:4 as humans often do. As your lips have instructed me
Psalms17:5 My steps have kept to your paths; my feet have not faltered.

Psalms17:7 Show your wonderful love

Psalms18:1 For the leader. Of David
Psalms18:2 He said: I love you
Psalms18:3 LORD
Psalms18:4 Praised be the LORD
Psalms18:5 The breakers of death surged round about me; the menacing floods terrified me.
Psalms18:6 The cords of Sheol tightened; the snares of death lay in wait for me.
Psalms18:7 In my distress I called out: LORD! I cried out to my God. From his temple he heard my voice; my cry to him reached his ears.
Psalms18:8 The earth rocked and shook; the foundations of the mountains trembled; they shook as his wrath flared up.
Psalms18:9 Smoke rose in his nostrils
Psalms18:10 He parted the heavens and came down
Psalms18:11 Mounted on a cherub he flew
Psalms18:12 He made darkness the cover about him; his canopy
Psalms18:13 Before him scudded his clouds
Psalms18:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice resound.
Psalms18:15 He let fly his arrows and scattered them; shot his lightning bolts and dispersed them.
Psalms18:16 Then the bed of the sea appeared; the world's foundations lay bare
Psalms18:17 He reached down from on high and seized me; drew me out of the deep waters.
Psalms18:18 He rescued me from my mighty enemy
Psalms18:19 They attacked me on a day of distress
Psalms18:20 He set me free in the open; he rescued me because he loves me.
Psalms18:21 The LORD acknowledged my righteousness
Psalms18:22 For I kept the ways of the LORD; I was not disloyal to my God.
Psalms18:23 His laws were all before me
Psalms18:24 I was honest toward him; I was on guard against sin.
Psalms18:25 So the LORD rewarded my righteousness
Psalms18:26 Toward the faithful you are faithful; to the honest you are honest;
Psalms18:27 Toward the sincere
Psalms18:28 Humble people you save; haughty eyes you bring low.
Psalms18:29 You
Psalms18:30 With you I can rush an armed band
Psalms18:31 God's way is unerring; the LORD'S promise is tried and true; he is a shield for all who trust in him.
Psalms18:32 Truly
Psalms18:33 This God who girded me with might
Psalms18:34 Who made my feet swift as a deer's
Psalms18:35 Who trained my hands for war
Psalms18:36 You have given me your protecting shield; your right hand has upheld me; you stooped to make me great.
Psalms18:37 You gave me room to stride; my feet never stumbled.
Psalms18:38 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not turn back till I destroyed them.
Psalms18:39 I struck them down; they could not rise; they fell dead at my feet.
Psalms18:40 You girded me with strength for war
Psalms18:41 My foes you put to flight before me; those who hated me I destroyed.
Psalms18:42 They cried for help
Psalms18:43 I ground them fine as dust in the wind; like mud in the streets I trampled them down.
Psalms18:44 You rescued me from the strife of peoples; you made me head over nations. A people I had not known became my slaves;
Psalms18:45 as soon as they heard of me they obeyed. Foreigners cringed before me;
Psalms18:46 their courage failed; they came trembling from their fortresses.
Psalms18:47 The LORD lives! Blessed be my rock! Exalted be God
Psalms18:48 O God who granted me vindication
Psalms18:49 and preserved me from my enemies
Psalms18:50 Thus I will proclaim you
Psalms18:51 You have given great victories to your king
Psalms19:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
Psalms19:2 The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky proclaims its builder's craft.
Psalms19:3 One day to the next conveys that message; one night to the next imparts that knowledge.
Psalms19:4 There is no word or sound; no voice is heard;
Psalms19:5 Yet their report goes forth through all the earth
Psalms19:6 it comes forth like a bridegroom from his chamber
Psalms19:7 From one end of the heavens it comes forth; its course runs through to the other; nothing escapes its heat.
Psalms19:8 The law of the LORD is perfect
Psalms19:9 The precepts of the LORD are right
Psalms19:10 The fear of the LORD is pure
Psalms19:11 More desirable than gold
Psalms19:12 By them your servant is instructed; obeying them brings much reward.
Psalms19:13 Who can detect heedless failings? Cleanse me from my unknown faults.
Psalms19:14 But from willful sins keep your servant; let them never control me. Then shall I be blameless
Psalms19:15 Let the words of my mouth meet with your favor

Psalms21:2 LORD
Psalms21:3 You have granted him his heart's desire; you did not refuse the prayer of his lips. Selah
Psalms21:4 For you welcomed him with goodly blessings; you placed on his head a crown of pure gold.

Psalms22:1 For the leader; according to The deer of the dawn. A psalm of David.
Psalms22:2 My God
Psalms22:3 My God
Psalms22:4 Yet you are enthroned as the Holy One; you are the glory of Israel.
Psalms22:5 In you our ancestors trusted; they trusted and you rescued them.
Psalms22:6 To you they cried out and they escaped; in you they trusted and were not disappointed.
Psalms22:7 But I am a worm
Psalms22:8 All who see me mock me; they curl their lips and jeer; they shake their heads at me:
Psalms22:9 You relied on the LORD-- let him deliver you; if he loves you
Psalms22:10 Yet you drew me forth from the womb
Psalms22:11 Upon you I was thrust from the womb; since birth you are my God.
Psalms22:12 Do not stay far from me
Psalms22:13 Many bulls surround me; fierce bulls of Bashan encircle me.
Psalms22:14 They open their mouths against me
Psalms22:15 Like water my life drains away; all my bones grow soft. My heart has become like wax
Psalms22:16 As dry as a potsherd is my throat; my tongue sticks to my palate; you lay me in the dust of death.
Psalms22:17 Many dogs surround me; a pack of evildoers closes in on me. So wasted are my hands and feet
Psalms22:18 that I can count all my bones. They stare at me and gloat;
Psalms22:19 they divide my garments among them; for my clothing they cast lots.
Psalms22:20 But you
Psalms22:21 Deliver me from the sword
Psalms22:22 Save me from the lion's mouth
Psalms22:23 Then I will proclaim your name to the assembly; in the community I will praise you:
Psalms22:24 You who fear the LORD
Psalms22:25 For God has not spurned or disdained the misery of this poor wretch
Psalms22:26 I will offer praise in the great assembly; my vows I will fulfill before those who fear him.
Psalms22:27 The poor will eat their fill; those who seek the LORD will offer praise. May your hearts enjoy life forever!
Psalms22:28 All the ends of the earth will worship and turn to the LORD; All the families of nations will bow low before you.
Psalms22:29 For kingship belongs to the LORD
Psalms22:30 All who sleep in the earth will bow low before God; All who have gone down into the dust will kneel in homage.
Psalms22:31 And I will live for the LORD; my descendants will serve you.
Psalms22:32 The generation to come will be told of the Lord
Psalms23:1 A psalm of David. I The LORD is my shepherd; there is nothing I lack.
Psalms23:2 In green pastures you let me graze; to safe waters you lead me;
Psalms23:3 you restore my strength. You guide me along the right path for the sake of your name.
Psalms23:4 Even when I walk through a dark valley
Psalms23:5 You set a table before me as my enemies watch; You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.
Psalms23:6 Only goodness and love will pursue me all the days of my life; I will dwell in the house of the LORD for years to come.

Psalms25:1 Of David. I I wait for you
Psalms25:2 to my God. In you I trust; do not let me be disgraced; do not let my enemies gloat over me.
Psalms25:3 No one is disgraced who waits for you
Psalms25:4 Make known to me your ways
Psalms25:5 Guide me in your truth and teach me
Psalms25:6 Remember your compassion and love
Psalms25:7 Remember no more the sins of my youth; remember me only in light of your love.
Psalms25:8 Good and upright is the LORD
Psalms25:9 Guides the humble rightly
Psalms25:10 All the paths of the LORD are faithful love toward those who honor the covenant demands.
Psalms25:11 For the sake of your name
Psalms25:12 Who are those who fear the LORD? God shows them the way to choose.
Psalms25:13 They live well and prosper
Psalms25:14 The counsel of the LORD belongs to the faithful; the covenant instructs them.
Psalms25:15 My eyes are ever upon the LORD
Psalms25:16 Look upon me
Psalms25:17 Relieve the troubles of my heart; bring me out of my distress.
Psalms25:18 Put an end to my affliction and suffering; take away all my sins.
Psalms25:19 See how many are my enemies
Psalms25:20 Preserve my life and rescue me; do not let me be disgraced
Psalms25:21 Let honesty and virtue preserve me; I wait for you
Psalms25:22 Redeem Israel

Psalms27:1 Of David I The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom do I fear? The LORD is my life's refuge; of whom am I afraid?
Psalms27:2 When evildoers come at me to devour my flesh
Psalms27:3 Though an army encamp against me
Psalms27:4 One thing I ask of the LORD; this I seek: To dwell in the LORD'S house all the days of my life
Psalms27:5 For God will hide me in his shelter in time of trouble
Psalms27:6 Even now my head is held high above my enemies on every side! I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and chant praise to the LORD.
Psalms27:7 Hear my voice
Psalms27:8 Come
Psalms27:9 Do not hide your face from me; do not repel your servant in anger. You are my help; do not cast me off; do not forsake me
Psalms27:10 Even if my father and mother forsake me
Psalms27:11 LORD
Psalms27:12 Do not abandon me to the will of my foes; malicious and lying witnesses have risen against me.
Psalms27:13 But I believe I shall enjoy the LORD'S goodness in the land of the living.
Psalms27:14 Wait for the LORD

Psalms37:2 Like grass they wither quickly; like green plants they wilt away.
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good that you may dwell in the land and live secure.
Psalms37:4 Find your delight in the LORD who will give you your heart's desire.
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust that God will act
Psalms37:6 And make your integrity shine like the dawn
Psalms37:7 Be still before the LORD; wait for God. Do not be provoked by the prosperous
Psalms37:8 Give up your anger
Psalms37:9 Those who do evil will be cut off
Psalms37:10 Wait a little
Psalms37:11 But the poor will possess the land
Psalms37:12 The wicked plot against the just and grind their teeth at them;
Psalms37:13 But the LORD laughs at them
Psalms37:14 The wicked draw their swords; they string their bows To fell the poor and oppressed
Psalms37:15 Their swords will pierce their own hearts; their bows will be broken.
Psalms37:16 Better the poverty of the just than the great wealth of the wicked.
Psalms37:17 The arms of the wicked will be broken; the LORD will sustain the just.
Psalms37:18 The LORD watches over the days of the blameless; their heritage lasts forever.
Psalms37:19 They will not be disgraced when times are hard; in days of famine they will have plenty.
Psalms37:20 The wicked perish
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrow but do not repay; the just are generous in giving.
Psalms37:22 For those blessed by the Lord will possess the land
Psalms37:23 Those whose steps are guided by the LORD; whose way God approves
Psalms37:24 May stumble
Psalms37:25 Neither in my youth
Psalms37:26 The just always lend generously
Psalms37:27 Turn from evil and do good
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves justice and does not abandon the faithful. When the unjust are destroyed
Psalms37:29 The just will possess the land and live in it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouths of the just utter wisdom; their tongues speak what is right.
Psalms37:31 God's teaching is in their hearts; their steps do not falter.
Psalms37:32 The wicked spy on the just and seek to kill them.
Psalms37:33 But the LORD does not leave the just in their power
Psalms37:34 Wait eagerly for the LORD

Psalms37:36 When I passed by again
Psalms37:37 Observe the honest
Psalms37:38 But all sinners will be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the just is from the LORD

Psalms50:2 From Zion God shines forth. perfect in beauty.
Psalms50:3 Our God comes and will not be silent! Devouring fire precedes
Psalms50:4 God summons the heavens above and the earth to the judgment of his people:
Psalms50:5 Gather my faithful ones before me
Psalms50:6 The heavens proclaim divine justice
Psalms50:7 Listen
Psalms50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you
Psalms50:9 I need no bullock from your house
Psalms50:10 For every animal of the forest is mine
Psalms50:11 I know every bird of the heavens; the creatures of the field belong to me.
Psalms50:12 Were I hungry
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of goats?
Psalms50:14 Offer praise as your sacrifice to God; fulfill your vows to the Most High.
Psalms50:15 Then call on me in time of distress; I will rescue you
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked God says: Why do you recite my commandments and profess my covenant with your lips?
Psalms50:17 You hate discipline; you cast my words behind you!
Psalms50:18 When you see thieves
Psalms50:19 You give your mouth free rein for evil; you harness your tongue to deceit.
Psalms50:20 You sit maligning your own kin
Psalms50:21 When you do these things should I be silent? Or do you think that I am like you? I accuse you
Psalms50:22 Understand this
Psalms50:23 Those who offer praise as a sacrifice honor me; to the obedient I will show the salvation of God.

Psalms69:1 For the leader; according to Lilies. Of David.
Psalms69:2 Save me
Psalms69:3 I have sunk into the mire of the deep
Psalms69:4 I am weary with crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes have failed
Psalms69:5 More numerous than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause. Too many for my strength are my treacherous enemies. Must I now restore what I did not steal?
Psalms69:6 God
Psalms69:7 Let those who wait for you
Psalms69:8 For your sake I bear insult
Psalms69:9 I have become an outcast to my kin
Psalms69:10 Because zeal for your house consumes me
Psalms69:11 I have wept and fasted
Psalms69:12 I clothed myself in sackcloth; I became a byword for them.
Psalms69:13 They who sit at the gate gossip about me; drunkards make me the butt of their songs.
Psalms69:14 But I pray to you
Psalms69:15 Rescue me from the mire; do not let me sink. Rescue me from my enemies and from the watery depths.
Psalms69:16 Do not let the floodwaters overwhelm me
Psalms69:17 Answer me
Psalms69:18 Do not hide your face from your servant; in my distress hasten to answer me.
Psalms69:19 Come and ransom my life; because of my enemies redeem me.
Psalms69:20 You know my reproach
Psalms69:21 Insult has broken my heart
Psalms69:22 Instead they put gall in my food; for my thirst they gave me vinegar.
Psalms69:23 Make their own table a snare for them
Psalms69:24 Make their eyes so dim they cannot see; keep their backs ever feeble.
Psalms69:25 Pour out your wrath upon them; let the fury of your anger overtake them.
Psalms69:26 Make their camp desolate
Psalms69:27 For they pursued the one you struck
Psalms69:28 Add that to their crimes; let them not attain to your reward.
Psalms69:29 Strike them from the book of the living; do not count them among the just!
Psalms69:30 But I am afflicted and in pain; let your saving help protect me
Psalms69:31 That I may praise God's name in song and glorify it with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:32 My song will please the LORD more than oxen
Psalms69:33 See

Psalms69:36 God will rescue Zion
Psalms69:37 it shall be the heritage of their descendants; those who love God's name shall dwell there.

Psalms73:1 A psalm of Asaph. How good God is to the upright
Psalms73:2 But
Psalms73:3 Because I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 For they suffer no pain; their bodies are healthy and sleek.
Psalms73:5 They are free of the burdens of life; they are not afflicted like others.
Psalms73:6 Thus pride adorns them as a necklace; violence clothes them as a robe.
Psalms73:7 Out of their stupidity comes sin; evil thoughts flood their hearts.
Psalms73:8 They scoff and spout their malice; from on high they utter threats.
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against the heavens
Psalms73:10 So my people turn to them and drink deeply of their words.
Psalms73:11 They say
Psalms73:12 Such
Psalms73:13 Is it in vain that I have kept my heart clean
Psalms73:14 For I am afflicted day after day
Psalms73:15 Had I thought
Psalms73:16 Though I tried to understand all this
Psalms73:17 Till I entered the sanctuary of God and came to understand their end.
Psalms73:18 You set them
Psalms73:19 How suddenly they are devastated; undone by disasters forever!
Psalms73:20 They are like a dream after waking
Psalms73:21 Since my heart was embittered and my soul deeply wounded
Psalms73:22 I was stupid and could not understand; I was like a brute beast in your presence.
Psalms73:23 Yet I am always with you; you take hold of my right hand.
Psalms73:24 With your counsel you guide me
Psalms73:25 Whom else have I in the heavens? None beside you delights me on earth.
Psalms73:26 Though my flesh and my heart fail
Psalms73:27 But those who are far from you perish; you destroy those unfaithful to you.
Psalms73:28 As for me

Psalms77:1 For the leader; al Jeduthun. A psalm of Asaph.
Psalms77:2 I cry aloud to God
Psalms77:3 On the day of my distress I seek the Lord; by night my hands are raised unceasingly; I refuse to be consoled.
Psalms77:4 When I think of God
Psalms77:5 My eyes cannot close in sleep; I am troubled and cannot speak.
Psalms77:6 I consider the days of old; the years long past
Psalms77:7 I remember. In the night I meditate in my heart; I ponder and my spirit broods:
Psalms77:8 Will the Lord reject us forever
Psalms77:9 Has God's love ceased forever? Has the promise failed for all ages?
Psalms77:10 Has God forgotten mercy
Psalms77:11 I conclude: My sorrow is this
Psalms77:12 I will remember the deeds of the LORD; yes
Psalms77:13 I will recite all your works; your exploits I will tell.
Psalms77:14 Your way
Psalms77:15 You alone are the God who did wonders; among the peoples you revealed your might.
Psalms77:16 With your arm you redeemed your people
Psalms77:17 The waters saw you
Psalms77:18 The clouds poured down their rains; the thunderheads rumbled; your arrows flashed back and forth.
Psalms77:19 The thunder of your chariot wheels resounded; your lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and quaked.
Psalms77:20 Through the sea was your path; your way
Psalms77:21 You led your people like a flock under the care of Moses and Aaron.

Psalms78:7 that they too might put their trust in God
Psalms78:8 They were not to be like their ancestors
Psalms78:9 Like the ranks of Ephraimite archers
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant; they refused to walk by his law.
Psalms78:11 They forgot his works
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their ancestors God did wonders
Psalms78:13 He split the sea and led them across
Psalms78:14 God led them with a cloud by day
Psalms78:15 He split rock in the desert
Psalms78:16 He made streams flow from crags
Psalms78:17 But they went on sinning against him
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their hearts
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 True
Psalms78:21 The LORD heard and grew angry; fire blazed up against Jacob; anger flared up against Israel.
Psalms78:22 For they did not believe in God
Psalms78:23 So he commanded the skies above; the doors of heaven he opened.
Psalms78:24 God rained manna upon them for food; bread from heaven he gave them.
Psalms78:25 All ate a meal fit for heroes; food he sent in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He stirred up the east wind in the heavens; by his power God brought on the south wind.
Psalms78:27 He rained meat upon them like dust
Psalms78:28 Brought them down in the midst of the camp
Psalms78:29 They ate and were well filled; he gave them what they had craved.
Psalms78:30 But while they still wanted more
Psalms78:31 God's anger attacked them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they went on sinning
Psalms78:33 God ended their days abruptly
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they deceived him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were not constant toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
Psalms78:38 But God is merciful and forgave their sin; he did not utterly destroy them. Time and again he turned back his anger
Psalms78:39 He was mindful that they were flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against God in the desert
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power
Psalms78:43 When he displayed his wonders in Egypt
Psalms78:44 God changed their rivers to blood; their streams they could not drink.
Psalms78:45 He sent insects that devoured them
Psalms78:46 He gave their harvest to the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He exposed their flocks to deadly hail
Psalms78:49 He unleashed against them his fiery breath
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death; he delivered their beasts to the plague.
Psalms78:51 He struck all the firstborn of Egypt
Psalms78:52 God led forth his people like sheep; he guided them through the desert like a flock.
Psalms78:53 He led them on secure and unafraid
Psalms78:54 He brought them to his holy land
Psalms78:55 God drove out the nations before them
Psalms78:56 But they tested
Psalms78:57 They turned back
Psalms78:58 They enraged him with their high places; with their idols they goaded him.
Psalms78:59 God heard and grew angry; he rejected Israel completely.
Psalms78:60 He forsook the shrine at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He gave up his might into captivity
Psalms78:62 God abandoned his people to the sword; he was enraged against his heritage.
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed their young men; their young women heard no wedding songs.
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword; their widows made no lamentation.
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 He put his enemies to flight; everlasting shame he dealt them.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 God chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his shrine like the heavens
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant
Psalms78:71 From tending sheep God brought him
Psalms78:72 He shepherded them with a pure heart; with skilled hands he guided them.

Psalms103:12 As far as the east is from the west
Psalms103:13 As a father has compassion on his children
Psalms103:14 For he knows how we are formed
Psalms103:15 Our days are like the grass; like flowers of the field we blossom.
Psalms103:16 The wind sweeps over us and we are gone; our place knows us no more.
Psalms103:17 But the LORD'S kindness is forever
Psalms103:18 of those who keep his covenant
Psalms103:19 The LORD'S throne is established in heaven; God's royal power rules over all.

Psalms104:1 Bless the LORD
Psalms104:2 robed in light as with a cloak. You spread out the heavens like a tent;
Psalms104:3 you raised your palace upon the waters. You make the clouds your chariot; you travel on the wings of the wind.
Psalms104:4 You make the winds your messengers; flaming fire
Psalms104:5 You fixed the earth on its foundation
Psalms104:6 The ocean covered it like a garment; above the mountains stood the waters.
Psalms104:7 At your roar they took flight; at the sound of your thunder they fled.
Psalms104:8 They rushed up the mountains
Psalms104:9 You set a limit they cannot pass; never again will they cover the earth.
Psalms104:10 You made springs flow into channels that wind among the mountains.
Psalms104:11 They give drink to every beast of the field; here wild asses quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 Beside them the birds of heaven nest; among the branches they sing.
Psalms104:13 You water the mountains from your palace; by your labor the earth abounds.
Psalms104:14 You raise grass for the cattle and plants for our beasts of burden. You bring bread from the earth
Psalms104:15 and wine to gladden our hearts
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD drink their fill
Psalms104:17 There the birds build their nests; junipers are the home of the stork.
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for wild goats; the rocky cliffs
Psalms104:19 You made the moon to mark the seasons
Psalms104:20 You bring darkness and night falls
Psalms104:21 Young lions roar for prey; they seek their food from God.
Psalms104:22 When the sun rises
Psalms104:23 People go forth to their work
Psalms104:24 How varied are your works
Psalms104:25 Look at the sea
Psalms104:26 Here ships ply their course; here Leviathan
Psalms104:27 All of these look to you to give them food in due time.
Psalms104:28 When you give to them
Psalms104:29 When you hide your face
Psalms104:30 When you send forth your breath
Psalms104:31 May the glory of the LORD endure forever; may the LORD be glad in these works!
Psalms104:32 If God glares at the earth
Psalms104:33 I will sing to the LORD all my life; I will sing praise to my God while I live.
Psalms104:34 May my theme be pleasing to God; I will rejoice in the LORD.
Psalms104:35 May sinners vanish from the earth
Psalms105:1 Give thanks to the LORD
Psalms105:2 Sing praise
Psalms105:3 Glory in his holy name; rejoice
Psalms105:4 Rely on the mighty LORD; constantly seek his face.
Psalms105:5 Recall the wondrous deeds he has done
Psalms105:6 You descendants of Abraham his servant
Psalms105:7 The LORD is our God who rules the whole earth.
Psalms105:8 He remembers forever his covenant
Psalms105:9 Which was made with Abraham
Psalms105:10 And ratified as binding for Jacob
Psalms105:11 To you I give the land of Canaan
Psalms105:12 When they were few in number
Psalms105:13 Wandering from nation to nation
Psalms105:14 He let no one oppress them; for their sake he rebuked kings:
Psalms105:15  Do not touch my anointed
Psalms105:16 Then he called down a famine on the land
Psalms105:17 He had sent a man ahead of them
Psalms105:18 They shackled his feet with chains; collared his neck in iron
Psalms105:19 Till his prediction came to pass
Psalms105:20 The king sent and released him; the ruler of peoples set him free.
Psalms105:21 He made him lord over his palace
Psalms105:22 To instruct his princes by his word
Psalms105:23 Then Israel entered Egypt; Jacob lived in the land of Ham.
Psalms105:24 God greatly increased his people
Psalms105:25 He turned their hearts to hate his people
Psalms105:26 He sent his servant Moses
Psalms105:27 They worked his signs in Egypt and wonders in the land of Ham.
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and it grew dark
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood and killed all their fish.
Psalms105:30 Their land swarmed with frogs
Psalms105:31 He spoke and there came swarms of flies
Psalms105:32 For rain he gave them hail
Psalms105:33 He struck down their vines and fig trees
Psalms105:34 He spoke and the locusts came
Psalms105:35 They devoured every plant in the land; they ravaged the crops of their fields.
Psalms105:36 He struck down every firstborn in the land
Psalms105:37 He brought his people out
Psalms105:38 Egypt rejoiced when they left
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud as a cover
Psalms105:40 They asked and he brought them quail; with bread from heaven he filled them.
Psalms105:41 He split the rock and water gushed forth; it flowed through the desert like a river.
Psalms105:42 For he remembered his sacred word to Abraham his servant.
Psalms105:43 He brought his people out with joy
Psalms105:44 He gave them the lands of the nations
Psalms105:45 That they might keep his laws and observe his teachings. Hallelujah!
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Give thanks to the LORD
Psalms106:2 Who can tell the mighty deeds of the LORD
Psalms106:3 Happy those who do what is right
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the prosperity of your chosen
Psalms106:6 We have sinned like our ancestors; we have done wrong and are guilty.
Psalms106:7 Our ancestors in Egypt did not attend to your wonders. They did not remember your great love; they defied the Most High at the Red Sea.
Psalms106:8 Yet he saved them for his name's sake to make his power known.
Psalms106:9 He roared at the Red Sea and it dried up. He led them through the deep as through a desert.
Psalms106:10 He rescued them from hostile hands
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their oppressors; not one of them survived.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed his words and sang songs of praise.
Psalms106:13 But they soon forgot all he had done; they had no patience for his plan.
Psalms106:14 In the desert they gave way to their cravings
Psalms106:15 So he gave them what they asked and sent among them a wasting disease.
Psalms106:16 In the camp they challenged Moses and Aaron
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed Dathan
Psalms106:18 Against that company the fire blazed; flames consumed the wicked.
Psalms106:19 At Horeb they fashioned a calf
Psalms106:20 They exchanged their glorious God for the image of a grass-eating bull.
Psalms106:21 They forgot the God who saved them
Psalms106:22 Amazing deeds in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 He would have decreed their destruction
Psalms106:24 Next they despised the beautiful land; they did not believe the promise.
Psalms106:25 In their tents they complained; they did not obey the LORD.
Psalms106:26 So with raised hand he swore to destroy them in the desert
Psalms106:27 To scatter their descendants among the nations
Psalms106:28 They joined in the rites of Baal of Peor
Psalms106:29 They provoked him by their actions
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas rose to intervene
Psalms106:31 This was counted for him as a righteous deed for all generations to come.
Psalms106:32 At the waters of Meribah they angered God
Psalms106:33 They so embittered his spirit that rash words crossed his lips.
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples as the LORD had commanded them
Psalms106:35 But mingled with the nations and imitated their ways.
Psalms106:36 They worshiped their idols and were ensnared by them.
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed to the gods their own sons and daughters
Psalms106:38 Shedding innocent blood
Psalms106:39 They defiled themselves by their actions
Psalms106:40 So the LORD grew angry with his people
Psalms106:41 He handed them over to the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times did he rescue them
Psalms106:44 Still God had regard for their affliction when he heard their wailing.
Psalms106:45 For their sake he remembered his covenant and relented in his abundant love
Psalms106:46 Winning for them compassion from all who held them captive.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the LORD

Psalms107:2 Let that be the prayer of the LORD'S redeemed
Psalms107:3 Those gathered from foreign lands
Psalms107:4 Some had lost their way in a barren desert; found no path toward a city to live in.
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty; their life was ebbing away.
Psalms107:6 In their distress they cried to the LORD
Psalms107:7 Guided them by a direct path so they reached a city to live in.
Psalms107:8 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness
Psalms107:9 For he satisfied the thirsty
Psalms107:10 Some lived in darkness and gloom
Psalms107:11 Because they rebelled against God's word
Psalms107:12 Who humbled their hearts through hardship; they stumbled with no one to help.
Psalms107:13 In their distress they cried to the LORD
Psalms107:14 Led them forth from darkness and gloom and broke their chains asunder.
Psalms107:15 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness
Psalms107:16 For he broke down the gates of bronze and snapped the bars of iron.
Psalms107:17 Some fell sick from their wicked ways
Psalms107:18 They loathed all manner of food; they were at the gates of death.

Psalms107:20 Sent forth the word to heal them
Psalms107:21 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness
Psalms107:22 Let them offer a sacrifice in thanks
Psalms107:23 Some went off to sea in ships

Psalms107:27 They reeled
Psalms107:28 In their distress they cried to the LORD
Psalms107:29 Hushed the storm to a murmur; the waves of the sea were stilled.
Psalms107:30 They rejoiced that the sea grew calm
Psalms107:31 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness
Psalms107:32 Let them praise him in the assembly of the people
Psalms107:33 God changed rivers into desert
Psalms107:34 Fruitful land into a salty waste
Psalms107:35 He changed the desert into pools of water
Psalms107:36 And settled the hungry there; they built a city to live in.
Psalms107:37 They sowed fields and planted vineyards
Psalms107:38 God blessed them
Psalms107:39 Where they were diminished and brought low through misery and cruel oppression
Psalms107:40 But he poured out contempt on princes
Psalms107:41 While the poor were released from their affliction; their families increased like their flocks.
Psalms107:42 The upright saw this and rejoiced; all wickedness shut its mouth.
Psalms107:43 Whoever is wise will take note of these things

Psalms109:2 O God

Psalms109:5 They repay me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Find a lying witness
Psalms109:7 That he may be judged and found guilty
Psalms109:8 May his days be few; may another take his office.
Psalms109:9 May his children be fatherless
Psalms109:10 May his children be vagrant beggars
Psalms109:11 May the usurer snare all he owns
Psalms109:12 May no one treat him kindly or pity his fatherless children.
Psalms109:13 May his posterity be destroyed
Psalms109:14 May the LORD remember his fathers' guilt; his mother's sin not be canceled.
Psalms109:15 May their guilt be always before the LORD
Psalms109:16 For he did not remember to show kindness
Psalms109:17 He loved cursing; may it come upon him; he hated blessing; may none come to him.
Psalms109:18 May cursing clothe him like a robe; may it enter his belly like water
Psalms109:19 May it be near as the clothes he wears
Psalms109:20 May the LORD bring all this upon my accusers
Psalms109:21 But you
Psalms109:22 For I am sorely in need; my heart is pierced within me.

Psalms109:24 My knees totter from fasting; my flesh has wasted away.
Psalms109:25 I have become a mockery to them; when they see me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 Make them know this is your hand
Psalms109:28 Though they curse
Psalms109:29 Clothe my accusers with disgrace; make them wear shame like a mantle.
Psalms109:30 I will give fervent thanks to the LORD; before all I will praise my God.
Psalms109:31 For God stands at the right hand of the poor to defend them against unjust accusers.

Psalms119:1 Happy those whose way is blameless
Psalms119:2 Happy those who observe God's decrees
Psalms119:3 They do no wrong; they walk in God's ways.
Psalms119:4 You have given them the command to keep your precepts with care.
Psalms119:5 May my ways be firm in the observance of your laws!
Psalms119:6 Then I will not be ashamed to ponder all your commands.
Psalms119:7 I will praise you with sincere heart as I study your just edicts.
Psalms119:8 I will keep your laws; do not leave me all alone.
Psalms119:9 How can the young walk without fault? Only by keeping your words.
Psalms119:10 With all my heart I seek you; do not let me stray from your commands.
Psalms119:11 In my heart I treasure your promise
Psalms119:12 Blessed are you
Psalms119:13 With my lips I recite all the edicts you have spoken.
Psalms119:14 I find joy in the way of your decrees more than in all riches.
Psalms119:15 I will ponder your precepts and consider your paths.
Psalms119:16 In your laws I take delight; I will never forget your word.
Psalms119:17 Be kind to your servant that I may live
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes to see clearly the wonders of your teachings.
Psalms119:19 I am a sojourner in the land; do not hide your commands from me.
Psalms119:20 At all times my soul is stirred with longing for your edicts.
Psalms119:21 With a curse you rebuke the proud who stray from your commands.
Psalms119:22 Free me from disgrace and contempt
Psalms119:23 Though princes meet and talk against me
Psalms119:24 Your decrees are my delight; they are my counselors.
Psalms119:25 I lie prostrate in the dust; give me life in accord with your word.
Psalms119:26 I disclosed my ways and you answered me; teach me your laws.
Psalms119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts; I will ponder your wondrous deeds.
Psalms119:28 I weep in bitter pain; in accord with your word to strengthen me.
Psalms119:29 Lead me from the way of deceit; favor me with your teaching.
Psalms119:30 The way of loyalty I have chosen; I have set your edicts before me.
Psalms119:31 I cling to your decrees
Psalms119:32 I will run the way of your commands
Psalms119:33 LORD
Psalms119:34 Give me insight to observe your teaching
Psalms119:35 Lead me in the path of your commands
Psalms119:36 Direct my heart toward your decrees and away from unjust gain.
Psalms119:37 Avert my eyes from what is worthless; by your way give me life.
Psalms119:38 For your servant fulfill your promise made to those who fear you.
Psalms119:39 Turn away from me the taunts I dread
Psalms119:40 See how I long for your precepts; in your justice give me life.
Psalms119:41 Let your love come to me
Psalms119:42 Let me answer my taunters with a word
Psalms119:43 Do not take the word of truth from my mouth
Psalms119:44 I will keep your teachings always
Psalms119:45 I will walk freely in an open space because I cherish your precepts.
Psalms119:46 I will speak openly of your decrees without fear even before kings.
Psalms119:47 I delight in your commands
Psalms119:48 I lift up my hands to your commands; I study your laws
Psalms119:49 Remember your word to your servant by which you give me hope.
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in affliction
Psalms119:51 Though the arrogant utterly scorn me
Psalms119:52 When I recite your edicts of old I am comforted
Psalms119:53 Rage seizes me because of the wicked; they forsake your teaching.
Psalms119:54 Your laws become my songs wherever I make my home.
Psalms119:55 Even at night I remember your name in observance of your teaching
Psalms119:56 This is my good fortune
Psalms119:57 My portion is the LORD; I promise to keep your words.
Psalms119:58 I entreat you with all my heart: have mercy on me in accord with your promise.
Psalms119:59 I have examined my ways and turned my steps to your decrees.
Psalms119:60 I am prompt
Psalms119:61 Though the snares of the wicked surround me
Psalms119:62 At midnight I rise to praise you because your edicts are just.
Psalms119:63 I am the friend of all who fear you
Psalms119:64 The earth
Psalms119:65 You have treated your servant well
Psalms119:66 Teach me wisdom and knowledge
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray
Psalms119:68 You are good and do what is good; teach me your laws.
Psalms119:69 The arrogant smear me with lies
Psalms119:70 Their hearts are gross and fat; as for me
Psalms119:71 It was good for me to be afflicted
Psalms119:72 Teaching from your lips is more precious to me than heaps of silver and gold.
Psalms119:73 Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me insight to learn your commands.
Psalms119:74 Those who fear you rejoice to see me
Psalms119:75 I know
Psalms119:76 May your love comfort me in accord with your promise to your servant.
Psalms119:77 Show me compassion that I may live
Psalms119:78 Shame the proud for oppressing me unjustly
Psalms119:79 Let those who fear you turn to me
Psalms119:80 May I be wholehearted toward your laws
Psalms119:81 My soul longs for your salvation; I put my hope in your word.
Psalms119:82 My eyes long to see your promise. When will you comfort me?
Psalms119:83 I am like a wineskin shriveled by smoke
Psalms119:84 How long can your servant survive? When will your edict doom my foes?
Psalms119:85 The arrogant have dug pits for me; defying your teaching.
Psalms119:86 All your commands are steadfast. Help me! I am pursued without cause.
Psalms119:87 They have almost ended my life on earth
Psalms119:88 In your kindness give me life
Psalms119:89 Your word
Psalms119:90 Through all generations your truth endures; fixed to stand firm like the earth.
Psalms119:91 By your edicts they stand firm to this day
Psalms119:92 Had your teaching not been my delight
Psalms119:93 I will never forget your precepts; through them you give me life.
Psalms119:94 I am yours; save me
Psalms119:95 The wicked hope to destroy me
Psalms119:96 I have seen the limits of all perfection
Psalms119:97 How I love your teaching
Psalms119:98 Your command makes me wiser than my foes
Psalms119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers
Psalms119:100 I have more insight than my elders
Psalms119:101 I keep my steps from every evil path
Psalms119:102 From your edicts I do not turn
Psalms119:103 How sweet to my tongue is your promise
Psalms119:104 Through your precepts I gain insight; therefore I hate all false ways.
Psalms119:105 Your word is a lamp for my feet
Psalms119:106 I make a solemn vow to keep your just edicts.
Psalms119:107 I am very much afflicted
Psalms119:108 Accept my freely offered praise; LORD
Psalms119:109 My life is always at risk
Psalms119:110 The wicked have set snares for me
Psalms119:111 Your decrees are my heritage forever; they are the joy of my heart.
Psalms119:112 My heart is set on fulfilling your laws; they are my reward forever.
Psalms119:113 I hate every hypocrite; your teaching I love.
Psalms119:114 You are my refuge and shield; in your word I hope.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Sustain me by your promise that I may live; do not disappoint me in my hope.
Psalms119:117 Strengthen me that I may be safe
Psalms119:118 You reject all who stray from your laws
Psalms119:119 Like dross you regard all the wicked on earth; therefore I love your decrees.
Psalms119:120 My flesh shudders with dread of you; I hold your edicts in awe.
Psalms119:121 I have fulfilled your just edict; do not abandon me to my oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Guarantee your servant's welfare; do not let the arrogant oppress me.
Psalms119:123 My eyes long to see your salvation and the justice of your promise.
Psalms119:124 Act with kindness toward your servant; teach me your laws.
Psalms119:125 I am your servant; give me discernment that I may know your decrees.
Psalms119:126 It is time for the LORD to act; they have disobeyed your teaching.
Psalms119:127 Truly I love your commands more than the finest gold.
Psalms119:128 Thus I follow all your precepts; every wrong way I hate.
Psalms119:129 Wonderful are your decrees; therefore I observe them.
Psalms119:130 The revelation of your words sheds light
Psalms119:131 I sigh with open mouth
Psalms119:132 Turn to me and be gracious
Psalms119:133 Steady my feet in accord with your promise; do not let iniquity lead me.
Psalms119:134 Free me from human oppression
Psalms119:135 Let your face shine upon your servant; teach me your laws.
Psalms119:136 My eyes shed streams of tears because your teaching is not followed.
Psalms119:137 You are righteous
Psalms119:138 You have issued your decrees in justice and in surpassing faithfulness.
Psalms119:139 I am consumed with rage
Psalms119:140 Your servant loves your promise; it has been proved by fire.
Psalms119:141 Though belittled and despised
Psalms119:142 Your justice is forever right
Psalms119:143 Though distress and anguish come upon me
Psalms119:144 Your decrees are forever just; give me discernment that I may live.
Psalms119:145 I call with all my heart
Psalms119:146 I call to you to save me that I may keep your decrees.
Psalms119:147 I rise before dawn and cry out; I put my hope in your words.
Psalms119:148 My eyes greet the night watches as I meditate on your promise.
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice in your love
Psalms119:150 Malicious persecutors draw near me; they are far from your teaching.
Psalms119:151 You are near
Psalms119:152 Long have I known from your decrees that you have established them forever.
Psalms119:153 Look at my affliction and rescue me
Psalms119:154 Take up my cause and redeem me; for the sake of your promise give me life.
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from sinners because they do not cherish your laws.
Psalms119:156 Your compassion is great
Psalms119:157 Though my persecutors and foes are many I do not turn from your decrees.
Psalms119:158 I view the faithless with loathing
Psalms119:159 See how I love your precepts
Psalms119:160 Your every word is enduring; all your just edicts are forever.
Psalms119:161 Princes persecute me without reason
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at your promise
Psalms119:163 Falsehood I hate and abhor; your teaching I love.
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day I praise you because your edicts are just.
Psalms119:165 Lovers of your teaching have much peace; for them there is no stumbling block.
Psalms119:166 I look for your salvation
Psalms119:167 I observe your decrees; I love them very much.
Psalms119:168 I observe your precepts and decrees; all my ways are before you.
Psalms119:169 Let my cry come before you
Psalms119:170 Let my prayer come before you; rescue me according to your promise.

Psalms119:172 May my tongue sing of your promise
Psalms119:173 Keep your hand ready to help me
Psalms119:174 I long for your salvation
Psalms119:175 Let me live to praise you; may your edicts give me help.

Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For many days
Proverbs3:3 Let not kindness and fidelity leave you; bind them around your neck;
Proverbs3:4 Then will you win favor and good esteem before God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways be mindful of him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes
Proverbs3:8 This will mean health for your flesh and vigor for your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the LORD with your wealth
Proverbs3:10 Then will your barns be filled with grain
Proverbs3:11 The discipline of the LORD
Proverbs3:12 For whom the LORD loves he reproves
Proverbs3:13 Happy the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 For her profit is better than profit in silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than corals
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who grasp her
Proverbs3:19 The LORD by wisdom founded the earth
Proverbs3:20 By his knowledge the depths break open
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 So will they be life to your soul
Proverbs3:23 Then you may securely go your way; your foot will never stumble;
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Be not afraid of sudden terror
Proverbs3:26 For the LORD will be your confidence
Proverbs3:27 Refuse no one the good on which he has a claim when it is in your power to do it for him.
Proverbs3:28 Say not to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Plot no evil against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Quarrel not with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Envy not the lawless man and choose none of his ways:
Proverbs3:32 To the LORD the perverse man is an abomination
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 When he is dealing with the arrogant
Proverbs3:35 Honor is the possession of wise men
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 Yes
Proverbs4:3 When I was my father's child
Proverbs4:4 He taught me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Forsake her not
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom is: get wisdom; at the cost of all you have
Proverbs4:8 Extol her
Proverbs4:9 She will put on your head a graceful diadem; a glorious crown will she bestow on you.
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 On the way of wisdom I direct you
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Hold fast to instruction
Proverbs4:14 The path of the wicked enter not
Proverbs4:15 Shun it
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot rest unless they have done evil; to have made no one stumble steals away their sleep.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the just is like shining light
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like darkness; they know not on what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not slip out of your sight
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 With closest custody
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you dishonest talk
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look straight ahead and your glance be directly forward.
Proverbs4:26 Survey the path for your feet
Proverbs4:27 Turn neither to right nor to left
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 That discretion may watch over you
Proverbs5:3 The lips of an adulteress drip with honey
Proverbs5:4 But in the end she is as bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death
Proverbs5:6 Lest you see before you the road to life
Proverbs5:7 So now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her
Proverbs5:9 Lest you give your honor to others
Proverbs5:10 Lest strangers have their fill of your wealth
Proverbs5:11 And you groan in the end
Proverbs5:12 And you say
Proverbs5:13 Why did I not listen to the voice of my teachers
Proverbs5:14 I have all but come to utter ruin
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern
Proverbs5:16 How may your water sources be dispersed abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let your fountain be yours alone
Proverbs5:18 And have joy of the wife of your youth
Proverbs5:19 your lovely hind
Proverbs5:20 Why then
Proverbs5:21 For each man's ways are plain to the LORD'S sight; all their paths he surveys;
Proverbs5:22 By his own iniquities the wicked man will be caught
Proverbs5:23 He will die from lack of discipline
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 You have been snared by the utterance of your lips
Proverbs6:3 So do this
Proverbs6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes
Proverbs6:5 Free yourself as a gazelle from the snare
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 For though she has no chief
Proverbs6:8 She procures her food in the summer
Proverbs6:9 How long
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 Then will poverty come upon you like a highway man
Proverbs6:12 A scoundrel
Proverbs6:13 He winks his eyes
Proverbs6:14 He has perversity in his heart
Proverbs6:15 Therefore suddenly ruin comes upon him; in an instant he is crushed beyond cure.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things the LORD hates
Proverbs6:17 Haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 A heart that plots wicked schemes
Proverbs6:19 The false witness who utters lies
Proverbs6:20 Observe
Proverbs6:21 Keep them fastened over your heart always
Proverbs6:22 When you lie down she will watch over you
Proverbs6:23 For the bidding is a lamp
Proverbs6:24 To keep you from your neighbor's wife
Proverbs6:25 Lust not in your heart after her beauty
Proverbs6:26 For the price of a loose woman may be scarcely a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire to his bosom
Proverbs6:28 Or can a man walk on live coals
Proverbs6:29 So with him who goes in to his neighbor's wife-- none who touches her shall go unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 Men despise not the thief if he steals to satisfy his appetite when he is hungry;
Proverbs6:31 Yet if he be caught he must pay back sevenfold; all the wealth of his house he may yield up.
Proverbs6:32 But he who commits adultery is a fool; he who would destroy himself does it.
Proverbs6:33 A degrading beating will he get
Proverbs6:34 For vindictive is the husband's wrath
Proverbs6:35 He will not consider any restitution
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commands and live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers
Proverbs7:4 Say to Wisdom
Proverbs7:5 That they may keep you from another's wife
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house
Proverbs7:7 And I saw among the simple ones
Proverbs7:8 Going along the street near the corner
Proverbs7:9 In the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And lo! the woman comes to meet him
Proverbs7:11 She is fickle and unruly
Proverbs7:12 Now she is in the streets
Proverbs7:13 When she seizes him
Proverbs7:14 I owed peace offerings
Proverbs7:15 So I came out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 With coverlets I have spread my couch
Proverbs7:17 I have sprinkled my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home
Proverbs7:20 A bag of money he took with him
Proverbs7:21 She wins him over by her repeated urging
Proverbs7:22 He follows her stupidly
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow pierces its liver; Like a bird that rushes into a snare
Proverbs7:24 So now
Proverbs7:25 Let not your heart turn to her ways
Proverbs7:26 For many are those she has struck down dead
Proverbs7:27 Her house is made up of ways to the nether world
Proverbs8:1 Does not Wisdom call
Proverbs8:2 On the top of the heights along the road
Proverbs8:3 By the gates at the approaches of the city
Proverbs8:4 To you
Proverbs8:5 You simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Give heed! for noble things I speak; honesty opens my lips.
Proverbs8:7 Yes
Proverbs8:8 Sincere are all the words of my mouth
Proverbs8:9 All of them are plain to the man of intelligence
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction in preference to silver
Proverbs8:11 (For Wisdom is better than corals
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 (The fear of the LORD is to hate evil;) Pride
Proverbs8:14 Mine are counsel and advice; Mine is strength; I am understanding.
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 By me princes govern
Proverbs8:17 Those who love me I also love
Proverbs8:18 With me are riches and honor
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 On the way of duty I walk
Proverbs8:21 Granting wealth to those who love me
Proverbs8:22 The LORD begot me
Proverbs8:23 From of old I was poured forth
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled into place
Proverbs8:26 While as yet the earth and the fields were not made
Proverbs8:27 When he established the heavens I was there
Proverbs8:28 When he made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 When he set for the sea its limit
Proverbs8:30 Then was I beside him as his craftsman
Proverbs8:31 playing on the surface of his earth; and I found delight in the sons of men.
Proverbs8:32 So now
Proverbs8:33 instruction and wisdom do not reject! Happy the man who obeys me
Proverbs8:34 Happy the man watching daily at my gates
Proverbs8:35 For he who finds me finds life
Proverbs8:36 But he who misses me harms himself; all who hate me love death.

Proverbs9:2 She has dressed her meat
Proverbs9:3 She has sent out her maidens; she calls from the heights out over the city:
Proverbs9:4 Let whoever is simple turn in here; to him who lacks understanding
Proverbs9:5 Come
Proverbs9:6 Forsake foolishness that you may live; advance in the way of understanding.

Proverbs9:16 Let whoever is simple turn in here
Proverbs9:17 Stolen water is sweet
