Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of David's son
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What profit has man from all the labor which he toils at under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 One generation passes and another comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun goes down; then it presses on to the place where it rises.
Ecclesiastes1:6 Blowing now toward the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All rivers go to the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All speech is labored; there is nothing man can say. The eye is not satisfied with seeing nor is the ear filled with hearing.
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been
Ecclesiastes1:10 Even the thing of which we say
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of the men of old; nor of those to come will there be any remembrance among those who come after them.
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 and I applied my mind to search and investigate in wisdom all things that are done under the sun. A thankless task God has appointed for men to be busied about.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all things that are done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 Though I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 yet when I applied my mind to know wisdom and knowledge
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom there is much sorrow
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 Of laughter I said: Mad! and of mirth: What good does this do?
Ecclesiastes2:3 I thought of beguiling my senses with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I undertook great works; I built myself houses and planted vineyards;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 And I constructed for myself reservoirs to water a flourishing woodland.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I acquired male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I amassed for myself silver and gold
Ecclesiastes2:9 I became great
Ecclesiastes2:10 Nothing that my eyes desired did I deny them
Ecclesiastes2:11 But when I turned to all the works that my hands had wrought
Ecclesiastes2:12 For what will the man do who is to come after the king? What men have already done! I went on to the consideration of wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that wisdom has the advantage over folly as much as light has the advantage over darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man has eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 So I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 Neither of the wise man nor of the fool will there be an abiding remembrance
Ecclesiastes2:17 Therefore I loathed life
Ecclesiastes2:18 And I detested all the fruits of my labor under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will have control over all the fruits of my wise labor under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So my feelings turned to despair of all the fruits of my labor under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For here is a man who has labored with wisdom and knowledge and skill
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what profit comes to a man from all the toil and anxiety of heart with which he has labored under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 All his days sorrow and grief are his occupation; even at night his mind is not at rest. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for man than to eat and drink and provide himself with good things by his labors. Even this
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat or drink apart from him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to whatever man he sees fit he gives wisdom and knowledge and joy; but to the sinner he gives the task of gathering possessions to be given to whatever man God sees fit. This also is vanity and a chase after wind.
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is an appointed time for everything
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to scatter stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to rend
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What advantage has the worker from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have considered the task which God has appointed for men to be busied about.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything appropriate to its time
Ecclesiastes3:12 I recognized that there is nothing better than to be glad and to do well during life.
Ecclesiastes3:13 For every man
Ecclesiastes3:14 I recognized that whatever God does will endure forever; there is no adding to it
Ecclesiastes3:15 What now is has already been; what is to be
Ecclesiastes3:16 And still under the sun in the judgment place I saw wickedness
Ecclesiastes3:17 And I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said to myself: As for the children of men
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the lot of man and of beast is one lot; the one dies as well as the other. Both have the same life-breath
Ecclesiastes3:20 Both go to the same place; both were made from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows if the life-breath of the children of men goes upward and the life-breath of beasts goes earthward?
Ecclesiastes3:22 And I saw that there is nothing better for a man than to rejoice in his work; for this is his lot. Who will let him see what is to come after him?
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I considered all the oppressions that take place under the sun: the tears of the victims with none to comfort them! From the hand of their oppressors comes violence
Ecclesiastes4:2 And those now dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 And better off than both is the yet unborn
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all toil and skillful work is the rivalry of one man for another. This also is vanity and a chase after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his arms and consumes his own flesh--
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is one handful with tranquility than two with toil and a chase after wind!
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again I found this vanity under the sun:
Ecclesiastes4:8 a solitary man with no companion; with neither son nor brother. Yet there is no end to all his toil
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one: they get a good wage for their labor.
Ecclesiastes4:10 If the one falls
Ecclesiastes4:11 So also
Ecclesiastes4:12 Where a lone man may be overcome
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king who no longer knows caution;
Ecclesiastes4:14 for from a prison house one comes forth to rule
Ecclesiastes4:15 Then I saw all those who are to live and move about under the sun with the heir apparent who will succeed to his place.
Ecclesiastes4:16 There is no end to all these people
Ecclesiastes4:17 Guard your step when you go to the house of God. Let your approach be obedience
Ecclesiastes5:1 Be not hasty in your utterance and let not your heart be quick to make a promise in God's presence. God is in heaven and you are on earth; therefore let your words be few.
Ecclesiastes5:2 For nightmares come with many cares
Ecclesiastes5:3 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:4 You had better not make a vow than make it and not fulfill it.
Ecclesiastes5:5 Let not your utterances make you guilty
Ecclesiastes5:6 Rather
Ecclesiastes5:7 If you see oppression of the poor
Ecclesiastes5:8 Yet an advantage for a country in every respect is a king for the arable land.
Ecclesiastes5:9 The covetous man is never satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes5:10 Where there are great riches
Ecclesiastes5:11 Sleep is sweet to the laboring man
Ecclesiastes5:12 This is a grievous evil which I have seen under the sun: riches kept by their owner to his hurt.
Ecclesiastes5:13 Should the riches be lost through some misfortune
Ecclesiastes5:14 As he came forth from his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:15 This too is a grievous evil
Ecclesiastes5:16 All the days of his life are passed in gloom and sorrow
Ecclesiastes5:17 Here is what I recognize as good: it is well for a man to eat and drink and enjoy all the fruits of his labor under the sun during the limited days of the life which God gives him; for this is his lot.
Ecclesiastes5:18 Any man to whom God gives riches and property
Ecclesiastes5:19 For he will hardly dwell on the shortness of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is another evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 there is the man to whom God gives riches and property and honor
Ecclesiastes6:3 Should a man have a hundred children and live many years
Ecclesiastes6:4 Though it came in vain and goes into darkness and its name is enveloped in darkness;
Ecclesiastes6:5 though it has not seen or known the sun
Ecclesiastes6:6 Should he live twice a thousand years and not enjoy his goods
Ecclesiastes6:7 All man's toil is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool
Ecclesiastes6:9 What the eyes see is better than what the desires wander after. This also is vanity and a chase after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever is
Ecclesiastes6:11 For though there are many sayings that multiply vanity
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for a man in life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than good ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to the house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to hearken to the wise man's rebuke than to hearken to the song of fools;
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 For oppression can make a fool of a wise man
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of speech than its beginning; better is the patient spirit than the lofty spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not in spirit become quickly discontented
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say: How is it that former times were better than these? For it is not in wisdom that you ask about this.
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom and an inheritance are good
Ecclesiastes7:12 For the protection of wisdom is as the protection of money; and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of its owner.
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God. Who can make straight what he has made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 On a good day enjoy good things
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen all manner of things in my vain days: a just man perishing in his justice
Ecclesiastes7:16 Be not just to excess
Ecclesiastes7:17 Be not wicked to excess
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good to hold to this rule
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom is a better defense for the wise man than would be ten princes in the city
Ecclesiastes7:20 yet there is no man on earth so just as to do good and never sin.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not give heed to every word that is spoken lest you hear your servant speaking ill of you
Ecclesiastes7:22 for you know in your heart that you have many times spoken ill of others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All these things I probed in wisdom. I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 What exists is far-reaching; it is deep
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my thoughts toward knowledge; I sought and pursued wisdom and reason
Ecclesiastes7:26 More bitter than death I find the woman who is a hunter's trap
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul still seeks and has not found: One man out of a thousand have I come upon
Ecclesiastes7:29 Behold
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man
Ecclesiastes8:2 Observe the precept of the king
Ecclesiastes8:3 be not hasty to withdraw from the king; do not join in with a base plot
Ecclesiastes8:4 because his word is sovereign
Ecclesiastes8:5 He who keeps the commandment experiences no evil
Ecclesiastes8:6 for there is a time and a judgment for everything.-- Yet it is a great affliction for man
Ecclesiastes8:7 that he is ignorant of what is to come; for who will make known to him how it will be?
Ecclesiastes8:8 There is no man who is master of the breath of life so as to retain it
Ecclesiastes8:9 All these things I considered and I applied my mind to every work that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes8:10 Meanwhile I saw wicked men approach and enter; and as they left the sacred place
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against evildoers is not promptly executed
Ecclesiastes8:12 because the sinner does evil a hundred times and survives. Though indeed I know that it shall be well with those who fear God
Ecclesiastes8:13 and that it shall not be well with the wicked man
Ecclesiastes8:14 This is a vanity which occurs on earth: there are just men treated as though they had done evil and wicked men treated as though they had done justly. This
Ecclesiastes8:15 Therefore I commend mirth
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom and to observe what is done on earth
Ecclesiastes8:17 I recognized that man is unable to find out all God's work that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:1 All this I have kept in mind and recognized: the just
Ecclesiastes9:2 in that there is the same lot for all
Ecclesiastes9:3 Among all the things that happen under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 Indeed
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they are to die
Ecclesiastes9:6 For them
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 At all times let your garments be white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife whom you love
Ecclesiastes9:10 Anything you can turn your hand to
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I saw under the sun that the race is not won by the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 Man no more knows his own time than fish taken in the fatal net
Ecclesiastes9:13 On the other hand I saw this wise deed under the sun

Ecclesiastes9:15 But in the city lived a man who
Ecclesiastes9:16 Though I had said

Ecclesiastes10:1 More weighty than wisdom or wealth is a little folly!
Ecclesiastes10:2 The wise man's understanding turns him to his right; the fool's understanding turns him to his left.
Ecclesiastes10:3 When the fool walks through the street
Ecclesiastes10:4 Should the anger of the ruler burst upon you
Ecclesiastes10:5 I have seen under the sun another evil
Ecclesiastes10:6 a fool put in lofty position while the rich sit in lowly places.
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horseback

Ecclesiastes10:9 He who moves stones may be hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron becomes dull
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bites because it has not been charmed

Ecclesiastes10:14 yet the fool multiplies words. Man knows not what is to come

Ecclesiastes11:2 Make seven or eight portions; you know not what misfortune may come upon the earth.
Ecclesiastes11:3 When the clouds are full

Isaiah1:7 Your country is waste
Isaiah1:8 And daughter Zion is left like a hut in a vineyard

Isaiah1:12 When you come in to visit me
Isaiah1:13 Trample my courts no more! Bring no more worthless offerings; your incense is loathsome to me. New moon and sabbath
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and festivals I detest; they weigh me down
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves clean! Put away your misdeeds from before my eyes; cease doing evil;
Isaiah1:17 learn to do good. Make justice your aim: redress the wronged

Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and resist

Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges as at first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed by judgment
Isaiah1:28 Rebels and sinners alike shall be crushed
Isaiah1:29 You shall be ashamed of the terebinths which you prized
Isaiah1:30 You shall become like a tree with falling leaves
Isaiah1:31 The strong man shall turn to tow

Isaiah7:1 In the days of Ahaz

Isaiah7:4 and say to him: Take care you remain tranquil and do not fear; let not your courage fail before these two stumps of smoldering brands (the blazing anger of Rezin and the Arameans

Isaiah7:19 All of them shall come and settle in the steep ravines and in the rocky clefts
Isaiah7:20 On that day the LORD shall shave with the razor hired from across the River (with the king of Assyria) the head
Isaiah7:21 On that day a man shall keep a heifer or a couple of sheep
Isaiah7:22 and from their abundant yield of milk he shall live on curds; curds and honey shall be the food of all who remain in the land.
Isaiah7:23 On that day every place where there used to be a thousand vines
Isaiah7:24 Men shall go there with bow and arrows; for all the country shall be briers and thorns.
Isaiah7:25 For fear of briers and thorns you shall not go upon any mountainside which used to be hoed with the mattock; they shall be grazing land for cattle and shall be trampled upon by sheep.

Jeremiah1:12 Then the LORD said to me: Well have you seen

Jeremiah1:14 And from the north
Jeremiah1:15 Lo

Jeremiah2:9 Therefore will I yet accuse you

Jeremiah2:13 Two evils have my people done: they have forsaken me

Jeremiah2:29 How dare you still plead with me? You have all rebelled against me
Jeremiah2:30 In vain I struck your children; the correction they did not take. Your sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.

Jeremiah7:1 The following message came to Jeremiah from the LORD:
Jeremiah7:2 Stand at the gate of the house of the LORD
Jeremiah7:3 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah7:4 Put not your trust in the deceitful words: This is the temple of the LORD! The temple of the LORD! The temple of the LORD!
Jeremiah7:5 Only if you thoroughly reform your ways and your deeds; if each of you deals justly with his neighbor;
Jeremiah7:6 if you no longer oppress the resident alien
Jeremiah7:7 will I remain with you in this place
Jeremiah7:8 But here you are
Jeremiah7:9 Are you to steal and murder
Jeremiah7:10 and yet come to stand before me in this house which bears my name
Jeremiah7:11 Has this house which bears my name become in your eyes a den of thieves? I too see what is being done
Jeremiah7:12 You may go to Shiloh
Jeremiah7:13 And now
Jeremiah7:14 I will do to this house named after me
Jeremiah7:15 I will cast you away from me
Jeremiah7:16 You
Jeremiah7:17 Do you not see what they are doing in the cities of Judah
Jeremiah7:18 The children gather wood
Jeremiah7:19 Is it I whom they hurt
Jeremiah7:20 See now
Jeremiah7:21 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah7:22 In speaking to your fathers on the day I brought them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah7:23 This rather is what I commanded them: Listen to my voice; then I will be your God and you shall be my people. Walk in all the ways that I command you
Jeremiah7:24 But they obeyed not
Jeremiah7:25 From the day that your fathers left the land of Egypt even to this day

Lamentations1:1 How lonely she is now
Lamentations1:2 Bitterly she weeps at night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has fled into exile from oppression and cruel slavery; Yet where she lives among the nations she finds no place to rest: All her persecutors come upon her where she is narrowly confined.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn for lack of pilgrims going to her feasts; All her gateways are deserted
Lamentations1:5 Her foes are uppermost
Lamentations1:6 Gone from daughter Zion is all her glory: Her princes
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem is mindful of the days of her wretched homelessness
Lamentations1:8 Through the sin of which she is guilty
Lamentations1:9 Her filth is on her skirt; she gave no thought how she would end. Astounding is her downfall
Lamentations1:10 The foe stretched out his hand to all her treasures; She has seen those nations enter her sanctuary Whom you forbade to come into your assembly.
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan
Lamentations1:12 Come
Lamentations1:13 From on high he sent fire down into my very frame; He spread a net for my feet
Lamentations1:14 He has kept watch over my sins; by his hand they have been plaited: They have settled about my neck
Lamentations1:15 All the mighty ones in my midst the Lord has cast away; He summoned an army against me to crush my young men; The LORD has trodden in the wine press virgin daughter Judah.
Lamentations1:16 At this I weep
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretched out her hands
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is just; I had defied his command. Listen
Lamentations1:19 I cried out to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 Look
Lamentations1:21 Give heed to my groaning; there is no one to console me. All my enemies rejoice at my misfortune: it is you who have wrought it. Bring on the day you have proclaimed
Lamentations1:22 Let all their evil come before you; deal with them As you have dealt with me for all my sins; My groans are many
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord in his wrath has detested daughter Zion! He has cast down from heaven to earth the glory of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has consumed without pity all the dwellings of Jacob; He has torn down in his anger the fortresses of daughter Judah; He has brought to the ground in dishonor her king and her princes.
Lamentations2:3 He broke off
Lamentations2:4 Like an enemy he made taut his bow; with his arrows in his right hand He took his stand as a foe
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become an enemy
Lamentations2:6 He has demolished his shelter like a garden booth
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has disowned his altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD marked for destruction the wall of daughter Zion: He stretched out the measuring line; his hand brought ruin
Lamentations2:9 Sunk into the ground are her gates; he has removed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the pagans; priestly instruction is wanting
Lamentations2:10 On the ground in silence sit the old men of daughter Zion; They strew dust on their heads and gird themselves with sackcloth; The maidens of Jerusalem bow their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 Worn out from weeping are my eyes
Lamentations2:12 They ask their mothers
Lamentations2:13 To what can I liken or compare you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets had for you false and specious visions; They did not lay bare your guilt
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by clap their hands at you; They hiss and wag their heads over daughter Jerusalem: Is this the all-beautiful city
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies open their mouths against you; They hiss and gnash their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done as he decreed: he has fulfilled the threat He set forth from days of old; he has destroyed and had no pity
Lamentations2:18 Cry out to the Lord; moan
Lamentations2:19 Rise up
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 Dead in the dust of the streets lie young and old; My maidens and young men have fallen by the sword; You have slain on the day of your wrath
Lamentations2:22 You summoned as for a feast day terrors against me from all sides; There was not
Lamentations3:1 I am a man who knows affliction from the rod of his anger
Lamentations3:2 One whom he has led and forced to walk in darkness
Lamentations3:3 Against me alone he brings back his hand again and again all the day.
Lamentations3:4 He has worn away my flesh and my skin
Lamentations3:5 He has beset me round about with poverty and weariness;
Lamentations3:6 He has left me to dwell in the dark like those long dead.
Lamentations3:7 He has hemmed me in with no escape and weighed me down with chains;
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out for help
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my ways with fitted stones
Lamentations3:10 A lurking bear he has been to me
Lamentations3:11 He deranged my ways
Lamentations3:12 He bent his bow
Lamentations3:13 He pierces my sides with shafts from his quiver.
Lamentations3:14 I have become a laughingstock for all nations
Lamentations3:15 He has sated me with bitter food
Lamentations3:16 He has broken my teeth with gravel
Lamentations3:17 My soul is deprived of peace
Lamentations3:18 I tell myself my future is lost
Lamentations3:19 The thought of my homeless poverty is wormwood and gall;
Lamentations3:20 Remembering it over and over leaves my soul downcast within me.
Lamentations3:21 But I will call this to mind
Lamentations3:22 The favors of the LORD are not exhausted
Lamentations3:23 They are renewed each morning
Lamentations3:24 My portion is the LORD
Lamentations3:25 Good is the LORD to one who waits for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good to hope in silence for the saving help of the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear the yoke from his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and in silence
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth to the dust; there may yet be hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him offer his cheek to be struck
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord's rejection does not last forever;
Lamentations3:32 Though he punishes
Lamentations3:33 He has no joy in afflicting or grieving the sons of men.
Lamentations3:34 When anyone tramples underfoot all the prisoners in the land
Lamentations3:35 When he distorts men's rights in the very sight of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 When he presses a crooked claim

Lamentations3:47 Terror and the pit have been our lot

Lamentations3:58 You defended me in mortal danger

Lamentations3:61 You hear their insults

Lamentations3:63 Whether they sit or stand
Lamentations3:64 Requite them as they deserve
Lamentations3:65 Give them hardness of heart
Lamentations3:66 Pursue them in wrath and destroy them from under your heavens!

Ezekiel16:5 No one looked on you with pity or compassion to do any of these things for you. Rather

Ezekiel16:10 I clothed you with an embroidered gown

Ezekiel16:12 a ring in your nose

Ezekiel16:14 You were renowned among the nations for your beauty

Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your gowns and made for yourself gaudy high places
Ezekiel16:17 You took the splendid gold and silver ornaments that I had given you and made for yourself male images
Ezekiel16:18 You took your embroidered gowns to cover them; my oil and my incense you set before them;
Ezekiel16:19 the food that I had given you
Ezekiel16:20 The sons and daughters you had borne me you took and offered as sacrifices to be devoured by them! Was it not enough that you had become a harlot?
Ezekiel16:21 You slaughtered and immolated my children to them
Ezekiel16:22 And through all your abominations and harlotries you remembered nothing of when you were a girl
Ezekiel16:23 Then after all your evildoing-- woe
Ezekiel16:24 you raised for yourself a platform and a dais in every public place.

Ezekiel16:27 Therefore I stretched out my hand against you
Ezekiel16:28 You also played the harlot with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 Again and again you played the harlot
Ezekiel16:30 How wild your lust! says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel16:31 building your platform at every street corner and erecting your dais in every public place! Yet you were unlike a prostitute
Ezekiel16:32 The adulterous wife receives
Ezekiel16:33 All harlots receive gifts. But you rather bestowed your gifts on all your lovers
Ezekiel16:34 Thus in your harlotry you were different from all other women. No one sought you out for prostitution. Since you gave payment instead of receiving it
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 Thus says the Lord GOD: Because you poured out your lust and revealed your nakedness in your harlotry with your lovers and abominable idols
Ezekiel16:37 I will now gather together all your lovers whom you tried to please
Ezekiel16:38 I will inflict on you the sentence of adulteresses and murderesses; I will wreak fury and jealousy upon you.
Ezekiel16:39 I will hand you over to them to tear down your platform and demolish your dais; they shall strip you of your garments and take away your splendid ornaments
Ezekiel16:40 They shall lead an assembly against you to stone you and hack you with their swords.
Ezekiel16:41 They shall burn your apartments with fire and inflict punishments on you while many women look on. Thus I will put an end to your harlotry
Ezekiel16:42 When I have wreaked my fury upon you I will cease to be jealous of you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you did not remember what happened when you were a girl
Ezekiel16:44 See
Ezekiel16:45 Yes
Ezekiel16:46 Your elder sister was Samaria with her daughters
Ezekiel16:47 Yet not only in their ways did you walk
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 And look at the guilt of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters were proud
Ezekiel16:50 Rather
Ezekiel16:51 Samaria did not commit half your sins! You have done more abominable things than they
Ezekiel16:52 You
Ezekiel16:53 I will restore their fortunes
Ezekiel16:54 that you may bear your shame and be disgraced for all the comfort you brought them.
Ezekiel16:55 Yes
Ezekiel16:56 Was not your sister Sodom kept in bad repute by you while you felt proud of yourself
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness became evident? Now you are like her
Ezekiel16:58 The penalty of your lewdness and your abominations-- you must bear it all
Ezekiel16:59 For thus speaks the Lord GOD: I will deal with you according to what you have done
Ezekiel16:60 Yet I will remember the covenant I made with you when you were a girl
Ezekiel16:61 Then you shall remember your conduct and be ashamed when I take your sisters
Ezekiel16:62 For I will re-establish my covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 that you may remember and be covered with confusion
Ezekiel17:1 Thus the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel17:2 Son of man
Ezekiel17:3 Thus speaks the Lord GOD: The great eagle
Ezekiel17:4 tearing off its topmost branch
Ezekiel17:5 Then he took some seed of the land
Ezekiel17:6 To sprout and grow up a vine

Ezekiel17:8 In a fertile field by plentiful waters it was planted
Ezekiel17:9 Say: Thus says the Lord GOD: Can it prosper? Will he not rather tear it out by the roots and strip off its fruit
Ezekiel17:10 True

Ezekiel17:14 so that the kingdom would remain a modest one

Ezekiel17:17 When ramps are cast up and siege towers are built for the destruction of many lives

Ezekiel18:1 Thus the word of the LORD came to me: Son of man
Ezekiel18:2 what is the meaning of this proverb that you recite in the land of Israel: Fathers have eaten green grapes
Ezekiel18:3 As I live
Ezekiel18:4 For all lives are mine; the life of the father is like the life of the son
Ezekiel18:5 If a man is virtuous-- if he does what is right and just
Ezekiel18:6 if he does not eat on the mountains
Ezekiel18:7 if he oppresses no one
Ezekiel18:8 if he does not lend at interest nor exact usury; if he holds off from evildoing
Ezekiel18:9 if he lives by my statutes and is careful to observe my ordinances
Ezekiel18:10 But if he begets a son who is a thief
Ezekiel18:11 (though the father does none of them)
Ezekiel18:12 oppresses the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 lends at interest and exacts usury-- this son certainly shall not live. Because he practiced all these abominations
Ezekiel18:14 On the other hand
Ezekiel18:15 a son who does not eat on the mountains
Ezekiel18:16 who does not oppress anyone
Ezekiel18:17 who holds off from evildoing
Ezekiel18:18 Only the father
Ezekiel18:19 You ask: Why is not the son charged with the guilt of his father? Because the son has done what is right and just
Ezekiel18:20 Only the one who sins shall die. The son shall not be charged with the guilt of his father
Ezekiel18:21 But if the wicked man turns away from all the sins he committed
Ezekiel18:22 None of the crimes he committed shall be remembered against him; he shall live because of the virtue he has practiced.
Ezekiel18:23 Do I indeed derive any pleasure from the death of the wicked? says the Lord GOD. Do I not rather rejoice when he turns from his evil way that he may live?
Ezekiel18:24 And if the virtuous man turns from the path of virtue to do evil
Ezekiel18:25 You say
Ezekiel18:26 When a virtuous man turns away from virtue to commit iniquity
Ezekiel18:27 But if a wicked man
Ezekiel18:28 since he has turned away from all the sins which he committed
Ezekiel18:29 And yet the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all the crimes you have committed
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone who dies
Ezekiel19:1 As for you
Ezekiel19:2 What a lioness was your mother
Ezekiel19:3 One whelp she raised up
Ezekiel19:4 Then nations raised cries against him
Ezekiel19:5 Then she saw that in vain she had waited
Ezekiel19:6 He prowled among the lions
Ezekiel19:7 He ravaged their strongholds
Ezekiel19:8 Nations laid out against him snares all about him; They spread their net to take him
Ezekiel19:9 They put him in a cage and took him away to the king of Babylon
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine planted by the water; Fruitful and branchy was she because of the abundant water.
Ezekiel19:11 One strong branch she put out as a royal scepter. Stately was her height amid the dense foliage; Notably tall was she with her many clusters.
Ezekiel19:12 But she was torn up in fury and flung to the ground; The east wind withered her up
Ezekiel19:13 So now she is planted in the desert
Ezekiel19:14 For fire came out of the branch and devoured her shoots; She is now without a strong branch
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel20:3 Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Will you judge them? Will you judge
Ezekiel20:5 in these words: Thus speaks the Lord GOD: The day I chose Israel
Ezekiel20:6 That day I swore to bring them out of the land of Egypt to the land I had scouted for them
Ezekiel20:7 Then I said to them: Throw away
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against me and refused to listen to me; none of them threw away the detestable things that had held their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 but I acted for my name's sake
Ezekiel20:10 Therefore I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the desert.
Ezekiel20:11 Then I gave them my statutes and made known to them my ordinances
Ezekiel20:12 I also gave them my sabbaths to be a sign between me and them
Ezekiel20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the desert. They did not observe my statutes
Ezekiel20:14 but I acted for my name's sake
Ezekiel20:15 Nevertheless I swore to them in the desert not to bring them to the land I had given them
Ezekiel20:16 So much were their hearts devoted to their idols
Ezekiel20:17 But I looked on them with pity
Ezekiel20:18 Then I said to their children in the desert: Do not observe the statutes of your parents or keep their ordinances; do not defile yourselves with their idols.
Ezekiel20:19 I am the LORD
Ezekiel20:20 keep holy my sabbaths
Ezekiel20:21 But their children rebelled against me: they did not observe my statutes or keep my ordinances that bring life to those who observe them
Ezekiel20:22 but I stayed my hand
Ezekiel20:23 Nevertheless I swore to them in the desert that I would disperse them among the nations and scatter them over foreign lands;
Ezekiel20:24 for they did not keep my ordinances
Ezekiel20:25 Therefore I gave them statutes that were not good
Ezekiel20:26 I let them become defiled by their gifts
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore speak to the house of Israel
Ezekiel20:28 when I had brought them to the land I had sworn to give them
Ezekiel20:29 I asked them: To what sort of high place do you betake yourselves?-- and so they call it a high place even to the present day.
Ezekiel20:30 Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord GOD: Will you defile yourselves like your fathers? Will you lust after their detestable idols?
Ezekiel20:31 By offering your gifts
Ezekiel20:32 What you are thinking of shall never happen: We shall be like the nations
Ezekiel20:33 As I live
Ezekiel20:34 With a mighty hand and outstretched arm
Ezekiel20:35 then I will lead you to the desert of the peoples
Ezekiel20:36 Just as I entered into judgment with your fathers in the desert of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel20:37 I will count you with the staff and bring back but a small number.
Ezekiel20:38 I will separate from you those who have rebelled and transgressed against me; from the land where they sojourned as aliens I will bring them out
Ezekiel20:39 As for you
Ezekiel20:40 For on my holy mountain
Ezekiel20:41 As a pleasing odor I will accept you
Ezekiel20:42 Thus you shall know that I am the LORD
Ezekiel20:43 There you shall recall your conduct and all the deeds by which you defiled yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves because of all the evil things you did.
Ezekiel20:44 And you shall know that I am the LORD when I deal with you thus

Ezekiel26:6 And her daughters on the mainland shall be slaughtered by the sword; thus they shall know that I am the LORD.

Ezekiel27:1 Thus the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel27:2 As for you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre that is situated at the approaches of the sea
Ezekiel27:4 In the midst of the sea your builders placed you
Ezekiel27:5 With cypress from Senir they built for you all of your decks; Cedar from Lebanon they took to make you a mast;
Ezekiel27:6 From the highest oaks of Bashan they made your oars; Your bridge they made of cypress wood from the coasts of Kittim.
Ezekiel27:7 Fine embroidered linen from Egypt became your sail (to serve you as a banner). Purple and scarlet from the coasts of Elishah covered your cabin.
Ezekiel27:8 Citizens of Sidon and Arvad served as your oarsmen; Skilled men of Zemer were in you to be your mariners;
Ezekiel27:9 The elders and experts of Gebal were in you to caulk your seams. Every ship and sailor on the sea came to you to carry trade.
Ezekiel27:10 Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as warriors; shield and helmet they hung upon you
Ezekiel27:11 The men of Arvad were all about your walls
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish traded with you
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 From Beth-togarmah horses
Ezekiel27:15 The Rhodanites trafficked with you; many coastlands traded with you; ivory tusks and ebony wood they gave you for payment.
Ezekiel27:16 Edom traded with you
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel trafficked with you
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus traded with you
Ezekiel27:19 Javan exchanged wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you for riding gear.
Ezekiel27:21 The trade of Arabia and of all the sheikhs of Kedar belonged to you; they dealt in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah also traded with you
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 traded with you
Ezekiel27:25 Ships of Tarshish journeyed for you in your merchandising. You were full and heavily laden in the heart of the sea.
Ezekiel27:26 Through the deep waters your oarsmen brought you home
Ezekiel27:27 Your wealth
Ezekiel27:28 Hearing the shouts of your mariners
Ezekiel27:29 Down from their ships come all who ply the oar; The sailors
Ezekiel27:30 Making their voice heard on your behalf
Ezekiel27:31 For you they shave their heads and put on sackcloth
Ezekiel27:32 In their mourning they utter a lament over you; thus they wail over you: Who was ever destroyed like Tyre in the midst of the sea?
Ezekiel27:33 With your goods which you drew from the seas you filled many peoples; With your great wealth and merchandise you enriched the kings of the earth.
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are wrecked in the sea
Ezekiel27:35 All who dwell on the coastlands are aghast over you
Ezekiel27:36 The traders among the peoples now hiss at you; You have become a horror

Ezekiel28:2 Son of man
Ezekiel28:3 Oh yes
Ezekiel28:4 By your wisdom and your intelligence you have made riches for yourself; You have put gold and silver into your treasuries.

Ezekiel28:6 therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have thought yourself to have the mind of a god
Ezekiel28:7 Therefore I will bring against you foreigners

Ezekiel28:12 Son of man

Ezekiel28:19 Among the peoples

Ezekiel44:1 Then he brought me back to the outer gate of the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:2 He said to me: This gate is to remain closed; it is not to be opened for anyone to enter by it; since the LORD
Ezekiel44:3 Only the prince may sit down in it to eat his meal in the presence of the LORD. He must enter by way of the vestibule of the gate
Ezekiel44:4 Then he brought me by way of the north gate to the facade of the temple
Ezekiel44:5 Then he said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel44:6 Say to that rebellious house
Ezekiel44:7 You have admitted foreigners
Ezekiel44:8 Instead of caring for the service of my temple
Ezekiel44:9 Thus says the Lord GOD: No foreigners
Ezekiel44:10 But as for the Levites who departed from me when Israel strayed from me to pursue their idols
Ezekiel44:11 They shall serve in my sanctuary as gatekeepers and temple servants; they shall slaughter the holocausts and the sacrifices for the people
Ezekiel44:12 Because they used to minister for them before their idols
Ezekiel44:13 They shall no longer draw near me to serve as my priests
Ezekiel44:14 But I will set them to the service of the temple
Ezekiel44:15 As for the levitical priests
Ezekiel44:16 It is they who shall enter my sanctuary
Ezekiel44:17 Whenever they enter the gates of the inner court
Ezekiel44:18 They shall have linen turbans on their heads and linen drawers on their loins; they shall not gird themselves with anything that causes sweat.
Ezekiel44:19 When they are to go out to the people in the outer court
Ezekiel44:20 They shall not shave their heads nor let their hair hang loose
Ezekiel44:21 No priest shall drink wine when he is to enter the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 They shall not take for their wives either widows or divorced women
Ezekiel44:23 They shall teach my people to distinguish between the sacred and the profane
Ezekiel44:24 In capital cases they shall stand as judges
Ezekiel44:25 They shall not make themselves unclean by coming near any dead person
Ezekiel44:26 After a priest has been cleansed
Ezekiel44:27 and on the day he enters the inner court to minister in the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:28 They shall have no inheritance
Ezekiel44:29 They shall eat the cereal offering
Ezekiel44:30 All the choicest first fruits of every kind
Ezekiel44:31 The priests shall not eat anything

Daniel1:4 young men without any defect
Daniel1:5 after three years' training they were to enter the king's service. The king allotted them a daily portion of food and wine from the royal table.
Daniel1:6 Among these were men of Judah: Daniel
Daniel1:7 The chief chamberlain changed their names: Daniel to Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel was resolved not to defile himself with the king's food or wine; so he begged the chief chamberlain to spare him this defilement.
Daniel1:9 Though God had given Daniel the favor and sympathy of the chief chamberlain
Daniel1:10 he nevertheless said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel said to the steward whom the chief chamberlain had put in charge of Daniel
Daniel1:12 Please test your servants for ten days. Give us vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 Then see how we look in comparison with the other young men who eat from the royal table
Daniel1:14 He acceded to this request
Daniel1:15 after ten days they looked healthier and better fed than any of the young men who ate from the royal table.
Daniel1:16 So the steward continued to take away the food and wine they were to receive

Daniel1:19 When the king had spoken with all of them
Daniel1:20 In any question of wisdom or prudence which the king put to them
Daniel1:21 Daniel remained there until the first year of King Cyrus.

Daniel6:1 And Darius the Mede succeeded to the kingdom at the age of sixty-two.
Daniel6:2 Darius decided to appoint over his entire kingdom one hundred and twenty satraps
Daniel6:3 these were accountable to three supervisors
Daniel6:4 Daniel outshone all the supervisors and satraps because an extraordinary spirit was in him
Daniel6:5 Therefore the supervisors and satraps tried to find grounds for accusation against Daniel as regards the administration. But they could accuse him of no wrongdoing; because he was trustworthy
Daniel6:6 Then these men said to themselves
Daniel6:7 So these supervisors and satraps went thronging to the king and said to him
Daniel6:8 All the supervisors of the kingdom

Daniel6:13 Then they went to remind the king about the prohibition: Did you not decree
Daniel6:14 To this they replied
Daniel6:15 The king was deeply grieved at this news and he made up his mind to save Daniel; he worked till sunset to rescue him.
Daniel6:16 But these men insisted. Keep in mind
Daniel6:17 So the king ordered Daniel to be brought and cast into the lions' den. To Daniel he said
Daniel6:18 To forestall any tampering
Daniel6:19 Then the king returned to his palace for the night; he refused to eat and he dismissed the entertainers. Since sleep was impossible for him
Daniel6:20 the king rose very early the next morning and hastened to the lions' den.
Daniel6:21 As he drew near
Daniel6:22 Daniel answered the king: O king
Daniel6:23 My God has sent his angel and closed the lions' mouths so that they have not hurt me. For I have been found innocent before him; neither to you have I done any harm
Daniel6:24 This gave the king great joy. At his order Daniel was removed from the den
Daniel6:25 The king then ordered the men who had accused Daniel
Daniel6:26 Then King Darius wrote to the nations and peoples of every language
Daniel6:27 I decree that throughout my royal domain the God of Daniel is to be reverenced and feared: For he is the living God
Daniel6:28 He is a deliverer and savior
Daniel6:29 So Daniel fared well during the reign of Darius and the reign of Cyrus the Persian.
Daniel7:1 In the first year of King Belshazzar of Babylon
Daniel7:2 In the vision I saw during the night
Daniel7:3 from which emerged four immense beasts
Daniel7:4 The first was like a lion
Daniel7:5 The second was like a bear; it was raised up on one side
Daniel7:6 After this I looked and saw another beast
Daniel7:7 After this
Daniel7:8 I was considering the ten horns it had
Daniel7:9 As I watched
Daniel7:10 A surging stream of fire flowed out from where he sat; Thousands upon thousands were ministering to him

Daniel7:12 The other beasts
Daniel7:13 As the visions during the night continued
Daniel7:14 He received dominion
Daniel7:15 I

Daniel7:19 But I wished to make certain about the fourth beast
Daniel7:20 about the ten horns on its head
Daniel7:21 For
Daniel7:22 until the Ancient One arrived; judgment was pronounced in favor of the holy ones of the Most High

Daniel7:24 The ten horns shall be ten kings rising out of that kingdom; another shall rise up after them
Daniel7:25 He shall speak against the Most High and oppress the holy ones of the Most High

Daniel8:1 After this first vision
Daniel8:2 In my vision I saw myself in the fortress of Susa in the province of Elam; I was beside the river Ulai.

Daniel8:4 I saw the ram butting toward the west
Daniel8:5 As I was reflecting
Daniel8:6 It approached the two-horned ram I had seen standing by the river
Daniel8:7 I saw it attack the ram with furious blows when they met
Daniel8:8 The he-goat became very powerful
Daniel8:9 Out of one of them came a little horn which kept growing toward the south
Daniel8:10 Its power extended to the host of heaven
Daniel8:11 It boasted even against the prince of the host
Daniel8:12 as well as the host
Daniel8:13 I heard a holy one speaking
Daniel8:14 He answered him

Daniel8:17 When he came near where I was standing

Daniel8:20 The two-horned ram you saw represents the kings of the Medes and Persians.

Daniel8:23 After their reign
Daniel8:24 He shall be strong and powerful
Daniel8:25 his cunning shall be against the holy ones

Daniel11:1 standing as a reinforcement and a bulwark for me.
Daniel11:2 Now I shall tell you the truth. Three kings of Persia are yet to come; and a fourth shall acquire the greatest riches of all. Strengthened by his riches
Daniel11:3 But a powerful king shall appear and rule with great might
Daniel11:4 No sooner shall he appear than his kingdom shall be broken and divided in four directions under heaven; but not among his descendants or in keeping with his mighty rule
Daniel11:5 The king of the south shall grow strong
Daniel11:6 After some years they shall become allies: the daughter of the king of the south shall come to the king of the north in the interest of peace. But her bid for power shall fail: and her line shall not be recognized
Daniel11:7 a descendant of her line shall succeed to his rank
Daniel11:8 Even their gods
Daniel11:9 Then the latter shall invade the land of the king of the south
Daniel11:10 But his sons shall prepare and assemble a great armed host
Daniel11:11 the king of the south
Daniel11:12 and be carried off. In the pride of his heart
Daniel11:13 For the king of the north shall raise another army
Daniel11:14 In those times many shall resist the king of the south
Daniel11:15 When the king of the north comes
Daniel11:16 He shall attack him and do as he pleases
Daniel11:17 He shall set himself to penetrate the entire strength of his kingdom. He shall conclude an agreement with him and give him a daughter in marriage in order to destroy the kingdom
Daniel11:18 He shall turn to the coastland and take many
Daniel11:19 He shall turn to the strongholds of his own land
Daniel11:20 In his stead one shall arise who will send a tax collector through the glorious kingdom
Daniel11:21 There shall rise in his place a despicable person
Daniel11:22 Armed might shall be completely overwhelmed by him and crushed
Daniel11:23 After allying with him
Daniel11:24 By stealth he shall enter prosperous provinces and do that which his fathers or grandfathers never did; he shall distribute spoil
Daniel11:25 He shall call on his strength and cleverness to meet the king of the south with a great army; the king of the south shall prepare for battle with a very large and strong army
Daniel11:26 Even his table companions shall seek to destroy him
Daniel11:27 The two kings
Daniel11:28 He shall turn back toward his land with great riches
Daniel11:29 At the time appointed he shall come again to the south
Daniel11:30 When ships of the Kittim confront him
Daniel11:31 Armed forces shall move at his command and defile the sanctuary stronghold
Daniel11:32 By his deceit he shall make some who were disloyal to the covenant apostatize; but those who remain loyal to their God shall take strong action.
Daniel11:33 The nation's wise men shall instruct the many; though for a time they will become victims of the sword
Daniel11:34 When they fall
Daniel11:35 Of the wise men
Daniel11:36 The king shall do as he pleases
Daniel11:37 He shall have no regard for the gods of his ancestors or for the one in whom women delight; for no god shall he have regard
Daniel11:38 Instead
Daniel11:39 To defend the strongholds he shall station a people of a foreign god. Whoever acknowledges him he shall provide with abundant honor; he shall make them rule over the many and distribute the land as a reward.
Daniel11:40 At the appointed time the king of the south shall come to grips with him
Daniel11:41 He shall enter the glorious land and many shall fall
Daniel11:42 He shall extend his power over the countries
Daniel11:43 He shall control the riches of gold and silver and all the treasures of Egypt; Libya and Ethiopia shall be in his train.
Daniel11:44 When news from the east and the north terrifies him
Daniel11:45 He shall pitch the tents of his royal pavilion between the sea and the glorious holy mountain

Daniel13:1 In Babylon there lived a man named Joakim
Daniel13:2 who married a very beautiful and God-fearing woman
Daniel13:3 her pious parents had trained their daughter according to the law of Moses.
Daniel13:4 Joakim was very rich; he had a garden near his house
Daniel13:5 That year
Daniel13:6 These men
Daniel13:7 When the people left at noon
Daniel13:8 When the old men saw her enter every day for her walk
Daniel13:9 They suppressed their consciences; they would not allow their eyes to look to heaven
Daniel13:10 Though both were enamored of her
Daniel13:11 for they were ashamed to reveal their lustful desire to have her.
Daniel13:12 Day by day they watched eagerly for her.
Daniel13:13 One day they said to each other
Daniel13:14 but both turned back
Daniel13:15 One day
Daniel13:16 Nobody else was there except the two elders
Daniel13:17 Bring me oil and soap
Daniel13:18 They did as she said; they shut the garden doors and left by the side gate to fetch what she had ordered
Daniel13:19 As soon as the maids had left
Daniel13:20 Look
Daniel13:21 If you refuse
Daniel13:22 I am completely trapped
Daniel13:23 Yet it is better for me to fall into your power without guilt than to sin before the Lord.
Daniel13:24 Then Susanna shrieked
Daniel13:25 as one of them ran to open the garden doors.
Daniel13:26 When the people in the house heard the cries from the garden
Daniel13:27 At the accusations by the old men
Daniel13:28 When the people came to her husband Joakim the next day
Daniel13:29 Send for Susanna
Daniel13:30 she came with her parents
Daniel13:31 Susanna
Daniel13:32 was veiled; but those wicked men ordered her to uncover her face so as to sate themselves with her beauty.
Daniel13:33 All her relatives and the onlookers were weeping.
Daniel13:34 In the midst of the people the two elders rose up and laid their hands on her head.
Daniel13:35 Through her tears she looked up to heaven
Daniel13:36 The elders made this accusation: As we were walking in the garden alone
Daniel13:37 A young man
Daniel13:38 When we
Daniel13:39 We saw them lying together
Daniel13:40 Then we seized this one and asked who the young man was
Daniel13:41 but she refused to tell us. We testify to this. The assembly believed them
Daniel13:42 But Susanna cried aloud: O eternal God
Daniel13:43 you know that they have testified falsely against me. Here I am about to die
Daniel13:44 The Lord heard her prayer.
Daniel13:45 As she was being led to execution
Daniel13:46 and he cried aloud: I will have no part in the death of this woman.
Daniel13:47 All the people turned and asked him
Daniel13:48 He stood in their midst and continued
Daniel13:49 Return to court
Daniel13:50 Then all the people returned in haste. To Daniel the elders said
Daniel13:51 But he replied
Daniel13:52 After they were separated one from the other
Daniel13:53 passing unjust sentences
Daniel13:54 Now
Daniel13:55 Under a mastic tree
Daniel13:56 Putting him to one side
Daniel13:57 This is how you acted with the daughters of Israel
Daniel13:58 Now

Daniel13:60 The whole assembly cried aloud
Daniel13:61 They rose up against the two elders
Daniel13:62 they put them to death. Thus was innocent blood spared that day.
Daniel13:63 Hilkiah and his wife praised God for their daughter Susanna
Daniel13:64 And from that day onward Daniel was greatly esteemed by the people.
Daniel14:1 After King Astyages was laid with his fathers
Daniel14:2 Daniel was the king's favorite and was held in higher esteem than any of the friends of the king.
Daniel14:3 The Babylonians had an idol called Bel
Daniel14:4 The king worshiped it and went every day to adore it; but Daniel adored only his God.
Daniel14:5 When the king asked him
Daniel14:6 Then the king continued
Daniel14:7 Daniel began to laugh. Do not be deceived
Daniel14:8 Enraged
Daniel14:9 But if you can show that Bel consumes them

Daniel14:11 the priests of Bel said
Daniel14:12 If you do not find that Bel has eaten it all when you return in the morning
Daniel14:13 They were not perturbed
Daniel14:14 After they departed the king set the food before Bel
Daniel14:15 The priests entered that night as usual
Daniel14:16 Early the next morning
Daniel14:17 Are the seals unbroken

Joel2:7 Like warriors they run
Joel2:8 No one crowds another
Joel2:9 They assault the city

Amos1:1 The words of Amos
Amos1:2 The LORD will roar from Zion
Amos1:3 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Damascus
Amos1:4 I will send fire upon the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 I will break the bar of Damascus; I will root out those who live in the Valley of Aven
Amos1:6 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Gaza
Amos1:7 I will send fire upon the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 I will root out those who live in Ashdod
Amos1:9 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Tyre
Amos1:10 I will send fire upon the wall of Tyre
Amos1:11 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Edom
Amos1:12 I will send fire upon Teman
Amos1:13 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of the Ammonites
Amos1:14 I will kindle a fire upon the wall of Rabbah
Amos1:15 Their king shall go into captivity
Amos2:1 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Moab
Amos2:2 I will send fire upon Moab
Amos2:3 I will root out the judge from her midst
Amos2:4 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Judah
Amos2:5 I will send fire upon Judah
Amos2:6 Thus says the LORD: For three crimes of Israel
Amos2:7 They trample the heads of the weak into the dust of the earth
Amos2:8 Upon garments taken in pledge they recline beside any altar; And the wine of those who have been fined they drink in the house of their god.
Amos2:9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorites before them
Amos2:10 It was I who brought you up from the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 I who raised up prophets among your sons
Amos2:12 But you gave the nazirites wine to drink
Amos2:13 Beware
Amos2:14 Flight shall perish from the swift
Amos2:15 nor the bowman stand his ground; The swift of foot shall not escape
Amos2:16 And the most stouthearted of warriors shall flee naked on that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word
Amos3:2 You alone have I favored
Amos3:3 Do two walk together unless they have agreed?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when it has no prey? Does a young lion cry out from its den unless it has seized something?
Amos3:5 Is a bird brought to earth by a snare when there is no lure for it? Does a snare spring up from the ground without catching anything?
Amos3:6 If the trumpet sounds in a city
Amos3:7 Indeed
Amos3:8 The lion roars-- who will not be afraid! The Lord GOD speaks-- who will not prophesy!

Amos3:10 For they know not how to do what is right
Amos3:11 Therefore

Amos3:14 On the day when I punish Israel for his crimes
Amos3:15 Then will I strike the winter house and the summer house; The ivory apartments shall be ruined
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 The Lord GOD has sworn by his holiness: Truly the days are coming upon you When they shall drag you away with hooks
Amos4:3 You shall go out through the breached walls each by the most direct way
Amos4:4 Come to Bethel and sin
Amos4:5 Burn leavened food as a thanksgiving sacrifice
Amos4:6 Though I have made your teeth clean of food in all your cities
Amos4:7 Though I also withheld the rain from you when the harvest was still three months away; I sent rain upon one city but not upon another; One field was watered by rain
Amos4:8 Though two or three cities staggered to one city for water that did not quench their thirst; Yet you returned not to me
Amos4:9 I struck you with blight and searing wind; your many gardens and vineyards
Amos4:10 I sent upon you a pestilence like that of Egypt
Amos4:11 I brought upon you such upheaval as when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah: you were like a brand plucked from the fire; Yet you returned not to me
Amos4:12 So now I will deal with you in my own way
Amos4:13 Him who formed the mountains

Amos5:20 Will not the day of the LORD be darkness and not light
Amos5:21 I hate

Nahum1:1 Oracle about Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.

Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and leaves it dry
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him
Nahum1:6 Before his wrath
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 when the flood rages; He makes an end of his opponents

Nahum1:10 As when a tangle of thornbushes is set aflame
Nahum1:11 From you he came who devised evil against the LORD
Nahum1:12 For

Nahum1:14 The LORD has commanded regarding you: no descendant shall come to bear your name; From your temple I will abolish the carved and the molten image; I will make your grave a mockery.

Habakkuk1:1 The oracle which Habakkuk the prophet received in vision.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you let me see ruin; why must I look at misery? Destruction and violence are before me; there is strife
Habakkuk1:4 This is why the law is benumbed
Habakkuk1:5 Look over the nations and see
Habakkuk1:6 For see
Habakkuk1:7 Terrible and dreadful is he
Habakkuk1:8 Swifter than leopards are his horses
Habakkuk1:9 each comes for the rapine
Habakkuk1:10 He scoffs at kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then he veers like the wind and is gone-- this culprit who makes his own strength his god!
Habakkuk1:12 Are you not from eternity
Habakkuk1:13 Too pure are your eyes to look upon evil
Habakkuk1:14 You have made man like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 He brings them all up with his hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net
Habakkuk1:17 Shall he
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my guard post
Habakkuk2:2 Then the LORD answered me and said: Write down the vision Clearly upon the tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision still has its time
Habakkuk2:4 The rash man has no integrity; but the just man
Habakkuk2:5 He who opens wide his throat like the nether world
Habakkuk2:6 Shall not all these take up a taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Shall not your creditors rise suddenly? Shall not they who make you tremble awake? You shall become their spoil!
Habakkuk2:8 Because you despoiled many peoples all the rest of the nations shall despoil you; Because of men's blood shed
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who pursues evil gain for his household
Habakkuk2:10 You have devised shame for your household
Habakkuk2:11 For the stone in the wall shall cry out
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a city by bloodshed
Habakkuk2:13 Is not this from the LORD of hosts: peoples toil for the flames

Matthew1:1 The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham became the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah
Matthew1:4 Ram the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon
Matthew1:5 Salmon the father of Boaz
Matthew1:6 Jesse the father of David the king. David became the father of Solomon
Matthew1:7 Solomon became the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asaph became the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah became the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers at the time of the Babylonian exile.
Matthew1:12 After the Babylonian exile
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim
Matthew1:14 Azor the father of Zadok. Zadok became the father of Achim
Matthew1:15 Eliud the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan
Matthew1:16 Jacob the father of Joseph
Matthew1:17 Thus the total number of generations from Abraham to David is fourteen generations; from David to the Babylonian exile
Matthew1:18 Now this is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about. When his mother Mary was betrothed to Joseph
Matthew1:19 Joseph her husband
Matthew1:20 Such was his intention when
Matthew1:21 She will bear a son and you are to name him Jesus
Matthew1:22 All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 Behold
Matthew1:24 When Joseph awoke
Matthew1:25 He had no relations with her until she bore a son

Matthew6:34 Do not worry about tomorrow; tomorrow will take care of itself. Sufficient for a day is its own evil.

Matthew10:30 Even all the hairs of your head are counted.
Matthew10:31 So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

Matthew13:1 On that day
Matthew13:2 Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down
Matthew13:3 And he spoke to them at length in parables
Matthew13:4 And as he sowed
Matthew13:5 Some fell on rocky ground
Matthew13:6 and when the sun rose it was scorched
Matthew13:7 Some seed fell among thorns
Matthew13:8 But some seed fell on rich soil
Matthew13:9 Whoever has ears ought to hear.
Matthew13:10 The disciples approached him and said
Matthew13:11 He said to them in reply
Matthew13:12 To anyone who has
Matthew13:13 This is why I speak to them in parables
Matthew13:14 Isaiah's prophecy is fulfilled in them
Matthew13:15 Gross is the heart of this people
Matthew13:16 But blessed are your eyes
Matthew13:17 Amen
Matthew13:18 Hear then the parable of the sower.
Matthew13:19 The seed sown on the path is the one who hears the word of the kingdom without understanding it
Matthew13:20 The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and receives it at once with joy.
Matthew13:21 But he has no root and lasts only for a time. When some tribulation or persecution comes because of the word
Matthew13:22 The seed sown among thorns is the one who hears the word
Matthew13:23 But the seed sown on rich soil is the one who hears the word and understands it
Matthew13:24 He proposed another parable to them. The kingdom of heaven may be likened to a man who sowed good seed in his field.
Matthew13:25 While everyone was asleep his enemy came and sowed weeds all through the wheat
Matthew13:26 When the crop grew and bore fruit
Matthew13:27 The slaves of the householder came to him and said
Matthew13:28 He answered
Matthew13:29 He replied
Matthew13:30 Let them grow together until harvest; then at harvest time I will say to the harvesters
Matthew13:31 He proposed another parable to them. The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a person took and sowed in a field.
Matthew13:32 It is the smallest of all the seeds
Matthew13:33 He spoke to them another parable. The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed with three measures of wheat flour until the whole batch was leavened.
Matthew13:34 All these things Jesus spoke to the crowds in parables. He spoke to them only in parables
Matthew13:35 to fulfill what had been said through the prophet: I will open my mouth in parables
Matthew13:36 Then
Matthew13:37 He said in reply
Matthew13:38 the field is the world
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age
Matthew13:40 Just as weeds are collected and burned (up) with fire
Matthew13:41 The Son of Man will send his angels
Matthew13:42 They will throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:43 Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Whoever has ears ought to hear.
Matthew13:44 The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure buried in a field
Matthew13:45 Again
Matthew13:46 When he finds a pearl of great price
Matthew13:47 Again
Matthew13:48 When it is full they haul it ashore and sit down to put what is good into buckets. What is bad they throw away.
Matthew13:49 Thus it will be at the end of the age. The angels will go out and separate the wicked from the righteous
Matthew13:50 and throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:51 Do you understand all these things? They answered
Matthew13:52 And he replied
Matthew13:53 When Jesus finished these parables
Matthew13:54 He came to his native place and taught the people in their synagogue. They were astonished and said
Matthew13:55 Is he not the carpenter's son? Is not his mother named Mary and his brothers James
Matthew13:56 Are not his sisters all with us? Where did this man get all this?
Matthew13:57 And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them
Matthew13:58 And he did not work many mighty deeds there because of their lack of faith.

Matthew15:8 'This people honors me with their lips
Matthew15:9 in vain do they worship me

Matthew15:14 Let them alone; they are blind guides (of the blind). If a blind person leads a blind person

Matthew15:16 He said to them

Matthew15:18 But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart
Matthew15:19 For from the heart come evil thoughts
Matthew15:20 These are what defile a person

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ (the Son of God).
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: Behold
Mark1:3 A voice of one crying out in the desert: 'Prepare the way of the Lord
Mark1:4 John (the) Baptist appeared in the desert proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

Acts20:27 for I did not shrink from proclaiming to you the entire plan of God.

Acts20:30 And from your own group
Acts20:31 So be vigilant and remember that for three years
Acts20:32 And now I commend you to God and to that gracious word of his that can build you up and give you the inheritance among all who are consecrated.
Acts20:33 I have never wanted anyone's silver or gold or clothing.
Acts20:34 You know well that these very hands have served my needs and my companions.
Acts20:35 In every way I have shown you that by hard work of that sort we must help the weak

Acts20:37 They were all weeping loudly as they threw their arms around Paul and kissed him
Acts20:38 for they were deeply distressed that he had said that they would never see his face again. Then they escorted him to the ship.

Acts27:1 When it was decided that we should sail to Italy

Acts27:12 Since the harbor was unfavorably situated for spending the winter

Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which he promised previously through his prophets in the holy scriptures
Romans1:3 the gospel about his Son
Romans1:4 but established as Son of God in power according to the spirit of holiness through resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 Through him we have received the grace of apostleship

Romans1:7 to all the beloved of God in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 God is my witness
Romans1:10 always asking in my prayers that somehow by God's will I may at last find my way clear to come to you.
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I do not want you to be unaware
Romans1:14 To Greeks and non-Greeks alike
Romans1:15 that is why I am eager to preach the gospel also to you in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel. It is the power of God for the salvation of everyone who believes: for Jew first
Romans1:17 For in it is revealed the righteousness of God from faith to faith; as it is written
Romans1:18 The wrath of God is indeed being revealed from heaven against every impiety and wickedness of those who suppress the truth by their wickedness.
Romans1:19 For what can be known about God is evident to them
Romans1:20 Ever since the creation of the world
Romans1:21 for although they knew God they did not accord him glory as God or give him thanks. Instead
Romans1:22 While claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for the likeness of an image of mortal man or of birds or of four-legged animals or of snakes.
Romans1:24 Therefore
Romans1:25 They exchanged the truth of God for a lie and revered and worshiped the creature rather than the creator
Romans1:26 Therefore
Romans1:27 and the males likewise gave up natural relations with females and burned with lust for one another. Males did shameful things with males and thus received in their own persons the due penalty for their perversity.
Romans1:28 And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They are filled with every form of wickedness
Romans1:30 and scandalmongers and they hate God. They are insolent
Romans1:31 They are senseless
Romans1:32 Although they know the just decree of God that all who practice such things deserve death
Romans2:1 Therefore
Romans2:2 We know that the judgment of God on those who do such things is true.
Romans2:3 Do you suppose
Romans2:4 Or do you hold his priceless kindness
Romans2:5 By your stubbornness and impenitent heart
Romans2:6 who will repay everyone according to his works:
Romans2:7 eternal life to those who seek glory
Romans2:8 but wrath and fury to those who selfishly disobey the truth and obey wickedness.
Romans2:9 Yes
Romans2:10 But there will be glory
Romans2:11 There is no partiality with God.

Romans2:13 For it is not those who hear the law who are just in the sight of God; rather

Romans2:22 You who forbid adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast of the law

Romans2:26 Again

Romans3:1 What advantage is there then in being a Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much
Romans3:3 What if some were unfaithful? Will their infidelity nullify the fidelity of God?
Romans3:4 Of course not! God must be true
Romans3:5 But if our wickedness provides proof of God's righteousness
Romans3:6 Of course not! For how else is God to judge the world?
Romans3:7 But if God's truth redounds to his glory through my falsehood
Romans3:8 And why not say-- as we are accused and as some claim we say-- that we should do evil that good may come of it? Their penalty is what they deserve.
Romans3:9 Well
Romans3:10 as it is written: There is no one just
Romans3:11 there is no one who understands
Romans3:12 All have gone astray; all alike are worthless; there is not one who does good
Romans3:13 Their throats are open graves; they deceive with their tongues; the venom of asps is on their lips;
Romans3:14 their mouths are full of bitter cursing.
Romans3:15 Their feet are quick to shed blood;
Romans3:16 ruin and misery are in their ways
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they know not.
Romans3:18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Romans3:19 Now we know that what the law says is addressed to those under the law
Romans3:20 since no human being will be justified in his sight by observing the law; for through the law comes consciousness of sin.
Romans3:21 But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law
Romans3:22 the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction;
Romans3:23 all have sinned and are deprived of the glory of God.
Romans3:24 They are justified freely by his grace through the redemption in Christ Jesus
Romans3:25 whom God set forth as an expiation
Romans3:26 through the forbearance of God-- to prove his righteousness in the present time
Romans3:27 What occasion is there then for boasting? It is ruled out. On what principle
Romans3:28 For we consider that a person is justified by faith apart from works of the law.
Romans3:29 Does God belong to Jews alone? Does he not belong to Gentiles
Romans3:30 for God is one and will justify the circumcised on the basis of faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
Romans3:31 Are we then annulling the law by this faith? Of course not! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then can we say that Abraham found
Romans4:2 Indeed
Romans4:3 For what does the scripture say? Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 A worker's wage is credited not as a gift
Romans4:5 But when one does not work
Romans4:6 So also David declares the blessedness of the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven and whose sins are covered.
Romans4:8 Blessed is the man whose sin the Lord does not record.
Romans4:9 Does this blessedness apply only to the circumcised
Romans4:10 Under what circumstances was it credited? Was he circumcised or not? He was not circumcised
Romans4:11 And he received the sign of circumcision as a seal on the righteousness received through faith while he was uncircumcised. Thus he was to be the father of all the uncircumcised who believe
Romans4:12 as well as the father of the circumcised who not only are circumcised
Romans4:13 It was not through the law that the promise was made to Abraham and his descendants that he would inherit the world
Romans4:14 For if those who adhere to the law are the heirs
Romans4:15 For the law produces wrath; but where there is no law
Romans4:16 For this reason
Romans4:17 as it is written
Romans4:18 He believed
Romans4:19 He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body as (already) dead (for he was almost a hundred years old) and the dead womb of Sarah.
Romans4:20 He did not doubt God's promise in unbelief; rather
Romans4:21 and was fully convinced that what he had promised he was also able to do.
Romans4:22 That is why it was credited to him as righteousness.

1Corinthians7:7 Indeed

1Corinthians7:10 To the married

1Corinthians7:19 Circumcision means nothing
1Corinthians7:20 Everyone should remain in the state in which he was called.

1Corinthians7:23 You have been purchased at a price. Do not become slaves to human beings.
1Corinthians7:24 Brothers
1Corinthians7:25 Now in regard to virgins
1Corinthians7:26 So this is what I think best because of the present distress: that it is a good thing for a person to remain as he is.
1Corinthians7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek a separation. Are you free of a wife? Then do not look for a wife.
1Corinthians7:28 If you marry
1Corinthians7:29 I tell you
1Corinthians7:30 those weeping as not weeping
1Corinthians7:31 those using the world as not using it fully. For the world in its present form is passing away.

1Corinthians7:33 But a married man is anxious about the things of the world
1Corinthians7:34 and he is divided. An unmarried woman or a virgin is anxious about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I am telling you this for your own benefit

1Corinthians7:37 The one who stands firm in his resolve

1Corinthians7:40 She is more blessed

1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only myself and Barnabas who do not have the right not to work?

1Corinthians9:8 Am I saying this on human authority

1Corinthians9:19 Although I am free in regard to all

1Corinthians9:21 To those outside the law I became like one outside the law-- though I am not outside God's law but within the law of Christ-- to win over those outside the law.
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 All this I do for the sake of the gospel

1Corinthians9:25 Every athlete exercises discipline in every way. They do it to win a perishable crown

1Corinthians12:7 To each individual the manifestation of the Spirit is given for some benefit.
1Corinthians12:8 To one is given through the Spirit the expression of wisdom; to another the expression of knowledge according to the same Spirit;
1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit; to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit;
1Corinthians12:10 to another mighty deeds; to another prophecy; to another discernment of spirits; to another varieties of tongues; to another interpretation of tongues.
1Corinthians12:11 But one and the same Spirit produces all of these
1Corinthians12:12 As a body is one though it has many parts
1Corinthians12:13 For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body
1Corinthians12:14 Now the body is not a single part

1Corinthians12:16 Or if an ear should say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But as it is
1Corinthians12:19 If they were all one part
1Corinthians12:20 But as it is
1Corinthians12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 Indeed
1Corinthians12:23 and those parts of the body that we consider less honorable we surround with greater honor
1Corinthians12:24 whereas our more presentable parts do not need this. But God has so constructed the body as to give greater honor to a part that is without it
1Corinthians12:25 so that there may be no division in the body
1Corinthians12:26 If (one) part suffers

1Corinthians15:20 But now Christ has been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since death came through a human being
1Corinthians15:22 For just as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 but each one in proper order: Christ the firstfruits; then
1Corinthians15:24 then comes the end
