1Corinthians15:29 Otherwise
1Corinthians15:30 Moreover
1Corinthians15:31 Every day I face death; I swear it by the pride in you (brothers) that I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.
1Corinthians15:32 If at Ephesus I fought with beasts
1Corinthians15:33 Do not be led astray: Bad company corrupts good morals.
1Corinthians15:34 Become sober as you ought and stop sinning. For some have no knowledge of God; I say this to your shame.
1Corinthians15:35 But someone may say
1Corinthians15:36 You fool! What you sow is not brought to life unless it dies.
1Corinthians15:37 And what you sow is not the body that is to be but a bare kernel of wheat
1Corinthians15:38 but God gives it a body as he chooses
1Corinthians15:39 Not all flesh is the same
1Corinthians15:40 There are both heavenly bodies and earthly bodies
1Corinthians15:41 The brightness of the sun is one kind
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown corruptible; it is raised incorruptible.
1Corinthians15:43 It is sown dishonorable; it is raised glorious. It is sown weak; it is raised powerful.
1Corinthians15:44 It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So
1Corinthians15:46 But the spiritual was not first; rather the natural and then the spiritual.
1Corinthians15:47 The first man was from the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As was the earthly one
1Corinthians15:49 Just as we have borne the image of the earthly one
1Corinthians15:50 This I declare
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 in an instant
1Corinthians15:53 For that which is corruptible must clothe itself with incorruptibility
1Corinthians15:54 And when this which is corruptible clothes itself with incorruptibility and this which is mortal clothes itself with immortality
1Corinthians15:55 Where
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin
1Corinthians15:57 But thanks be to God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore

Ephesians5:2 and live in love

Ephesians5:14 for everything that becomes visible is light. Therefore

Ephesians5:18 And do not get drunk on wine

Hebrews11:2 Because of it the ancients were well attested.

Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance; he went out
Hebrews11:9 By faith he sojourned in the promised land as in a foreign country
Hebrews11:10 for he was looking forward to the city with foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith he received power to generate
Hebrews11:12 So it was that there came forth from one man
Hebrews11:13 All these died in faith. They did not receive what had been promised but saw it and greeted it from afar and acknowledged themselves to be strangers and aliens on earth
Hebrews11:14 for those who speak thus show that they are seeking a homeland.
Hebrews11:15 If they had been thinking of the land from which they had come
Hebrews11:16 But now they desire a better homeland
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 He reasoned that God was able to raise even from the dead
Hebrews11:20 By faith regarding things still to come Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months after his birth
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 he chose to be ill-treated along with the people of God rather than enjoy the fleeting pleasure of sin.
Hebrews11:26 He considered the reproach of the Anointed greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if it were dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell after being encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish with the disobedient
Hebrews11:32 What more shall I say? I have not time to tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 who by faith conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 put out raging fires
Hebrews11:35 Women received back their dead through resurrection. Some were tortured and would not accept deliverance
Hebrews11:36 Others endured mockery
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 The world was not worthy of them. They wandered about in deserts and on mountains
Hebrews11:39 Yet all these
Hebrews11:40 God had foreseen something better for us

Hebrews13:2 Do not neglect hospitality
Hebrews13:3 Be mindful of prisoners as if sharing their imprisonment
Hebrews13:4 Let marriage be honored among all and the marriage bed be kept undefiled
Hebrews13:5 Let your life be free from love of money but be content with what you have
Hebrews13:6 Thus we may say with confidence: The Lord is my helper

Hebrews13:9 Do not be carried away by all kinds of strange teaching. It is good to have our hearts strengthened by grace and not by foods

Hebrews13:14 For here we have no lasting city

James3:1 Not many of you should become teachers
James3:2 for we all fall short in many respects. If anyone does not fall short in speech
James3:3 If we put bits into the mouths of horses to make them obey us
James3:4 It is the same with ships: even though they are so large and driven by fierce winds
James3:5 In the same way the tongue is a small member and yet has great pretensions. Consider how small a fire can set a huge forest ablaze.
James3:6 The tongue is also a fire. It exists among our members as a world of malice
James3:7 For every kind of beast and bird
James3:8 but no human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil
James3:9 With it we bless the Lord and Father
James3:10 From the same mouth come blessing and cursing. This need not be so
James3:11 Does a spring gush forth from the same opening both pure and brackish water?
James3:12 Can a fig tree

James3:16 For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is first of all pure

1John5:4 for whoever is begotten by God conquers the world. And the victory that conquers the world is our faith.

1John5:8 the Spirit
1John5:9 If we accept human testimony

1John5:12 Whoever possesses the Son has life; whoever does not possess the Son of God does not have life.

Revelation17:6 I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the holy ones and on the blood of the witnesses to Jesus. When I saw her I was greatly amazed.
Revelation17:7 The angel said to me
Revelation17:8 The beast that you saw existed once but now exists no longer. It will come up from the abyss and is headed for destruction. The inhabitants of the earth whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world shall be amazed when they see the beast

Revelation17:10 five have already fallen
Revelation17:11 The beast that existed once but exists no longer is an eighth king

Revelation17:13 They are of one mind and will give their power and authority to the beast.

Revelation17:17 For God has put it into their minds to carry out his purpose and to make them come to an agreement to give their kingdom to the beast until the words of God are accomplished.
Revelation17:18 The woman whom you saw represents the great city that has sovereignty over the kings of the earth.

Revelation18:2 He cried out in a mighty voice: Fallen

Revelation19:1 After this I heard what sounded like the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven
Revelation19:2 for true and just are his judgments. He has condemned the great harlot who corrupted the earth with her harlotry. He has avenged on her the blood of his servants.
Revelation19:3 They said a second time: Alleluia! Smoke will rise from her forever and ever.

Revelation19:18 to eat the flesh of kings

Revelation20:1 Then I saw an angel come down from heaven
Revelation20:2 He seized the dragon
Revelation20:3 and threw it into the abyss
Revelation20:4 Then I saw thrones; those who sat on them were entrusted with judgment. I also saw the souls of those who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and for the word of God
Revelation20:5 The rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
Revelation20:6 Blessed and holy is the one who shares in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over these; they will be priests of God and of Christ
Revelation20:7 When the thousand years are completed
Revelation20:8 He will go out to deceive the nations at the four corners of the earth
Revelation20:9 They invaded the breadth of the earth and surrounded the camp of the holy ones and the beloved city. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
Revelation20:10 The Devil who had led them astray was thrown into the pool of fire and sulfur
Revelation20:11 Next I saw a large white throne and the one who was sitting on it. The earth and the sky fled from his presence and there was no place for them.
Revelation20:12 I saw the dead
Revelation20:13 The sea gave up its dead; then Death and Hades gave up their dead. All the dead were judged according to their deeds.
Revelation20:14 Then Death and Hades were thrown into the pool of fire. (This pool of fire is the second death.)
Revelation20:15 Anyone whose name was not found written in the book of life was thrown into the pool of fire.
